model_requirements.description: E-Commerce is a rapidly growing digital industry operating online marketplaces, fulfillment networks, and technology platforms that connect millions of buyers and sellers with fast delivery, personalized recommendations, and seamless transactions.

_vibe_session_metadata.summary: Model quality: 90/100 (30 warnings, 0 errors). 148 tables across 14 domains.

model.name: Ecommerce
model.description: E-Commerce is a rapidly growing digital industry operating online marketplaces, fulfillment networks, and technology platforms that connect millions of buyers and sellers with fast delivery, personalized recommendations, and seamless transactions.
model.core_business_processes: Product Catalog Management, Order Processing and Fulfillment, Customer Acquisition and Retention, Inventory Management and Replenishment, Payment Processing and Settlement, Marketplace Seller Onboarding and Management, Search and Recommendation Engine Optimization, Last-Mile Delivery and Logistics, Returns and Refunds Processing, Pricing and Promotions Management
model.data_domains: Customer, Product, Order, Inventory, Payment, Marketplace, Fulfillment, Marketing, Pricing, Analytics, Seller, Content, Logistics, Finance
model.common_business_jargons: SKU (Stock Keeping Unit), AOV (Average Order Value), CLTV (Customer Lifetime Value), GMV (Gross Merchandise Value), CVR (Conversion Rate), CTR (Click-Through Rate), ROAS (Return on Ad Spend), CAC (Customer Acquisition Cost), NPS (Net Promoter Score), CRM (Customer Relationship Management), OMS (Order Management System), WMS (Warehouse Management System), TMS (Transportation Management System), ERP (Enterprise Resource Planning), PDP (Product Detail Page), PLP (Product Listing Page), SEO (Search Engine Optimization), SEM (Search Engine Marketing), API (Application Programming Interface), EDI (Electronic Data Interchange), 3PL (Third-Party Logistics), FBA (Fulfillment By Amazon-style), B2C (Business to Consumer), B2B (Business to Business), C2C (Consumer to Consumer), RMA (Return Merchandise Authorization), SLA (Service Level Agreement), KPI (Key Performance Indicator), UPC (Universal Product Code), EAN (European Article Number), ASIN (Amazon Standard Identification Number), PCI DSS (Payment Card Industry Data Security Standard), GDPR (General Data Protection Regulation), SOX (Sarbanes-Oxley Act), ETL (Extract Transform Load), CDN (Content Delivery Network), A/B Testing, MRR (Monthly Recurring Revenue), ARR (Annual Recurring Revenue), BOPIS (Buy Online Pick Up In Store), DTC (Direct to Consumer), LTV (Lifetime Value), CPC (Cost Per Click), CPM (Cost Per Mille), RPM (Revenue Per Mille), FIFO (First In First Out), MOQ (Minimum Order Quantity), PO (Purchase Order), ASP (Average Selling Price), DSR (Daily Sales Rate), OOS (Out of Stock), BSR (Best Seller Rank), FTL (Full Truck Load), LTL (Less Than Truck Load)
model.operational_systems_of_records: Order Management System (order capture; fulfillment orchestration; order lifecycle tracking), Warehouse Management System (inventory allocation; pick-pack-ship; receiving), Product Information Management System (catalog enrichment; attribute management; digital asset management), Customer Data Platform (unified customer profiles; segmentation; behavioral tracking), Payment Gateway and Processing Platform (transaction authorization; settlement; fraud detection), Enterprise Resource Planning - SAP S/4HANA (finance GL-AP-AR; procurement; supply chain planning), Transportation Management System (carrier selection; route optimization; shipment tracking), Marketing Automation Platform - Salesforce Marketing Cloud (campaign management; email; personalization), Search and Recommendation Engine (product discovery; relevance ranking; personalization algorithms), Seller Management Portal (seller onboarding; performance monitoring; commission management)
model.industry_governing_body: Federal Trade Commission (FTC) - consumer protection and advertising standards, Payment Card Industry Security Standards Council (PCI SSC) - PCI DSS compliance for payment data, International Organization for Standardization (ISO) - ISO 27001 information security management, General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR) - EU data privacy and protection, California Consumer Privacy Act (CCPA) - US state-level consumer data privacy, Consumer Product Safety Commission (CPSC) - product safety standards, World Trade Organization (WTO) - international trade regulations, National Institute of Standards and Technology (NIST) - cybersecurity framework, Electronic Commerce Directive (EU) - e-commerce regulatory framework, SOX (Sarbanes-Oxley Act) - financial reporting and internal controls for public companies

domains.name: customer
domains.description: Single source of truth for customer identity, profiles, accounts, segments, preferences, and behavioral data across B2C, B2B, and C2C customer types. Manages unified customer views, household relationships, loyalty programs, CLTV scoring, and NPS measurement. Owns consent and preference management under GDPR and CCPA. Integrates with Customer Data Platform for unified profile orchestration.
domains.references: Customer Data Platform (CDP), GDPR, CCPA, ISO 27001

products.name: customer_profile
products.description: Unified master record for every customer identity across B2C, B2B, and C2C customer types. Serves as the single source of truth for customer identity data sourced from the Customer Data Platform. Stores full name, email, phone, date of birth, gender, preferred language, preferred currency, account status, customer type (individual/business/marketplace), acquisition channel, acquisition date, first purchase date, last active date, and CDP profile ID. Owns KYC/identity verification records: verification type (email, phone, government ID, business registration), verification status, provider, verification date, expiry date, document type, risk level, and failure reason. This is the anchor entity for the entire customer domain — all other customer products reference back to profile.

attributes.name: customer_profile_id
attributes.description: Unique identifier for the customer profile. Primary key for the profile entity. Serves as the anchor for all customer domain relationships.

attributes.name: price_list_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to pricing.price_list. Business justification: REQUIRED: Negotiated price list per customer profile used in B2B contracts; sales team needs to retrieve the specific price list for each customer during order pricing.

attributes.name: segment_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to customer.segment. Business justification: Profile can be assigned to a primary segment; many profiles per segment, add FK to enable segment membership lookup.

attributes.name: account_closed_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when the customer account was permanently closed or deactivated. Null for active accounts. Used for churn analysis and GDPR right-to-erasure compliance.

attributes.name: account_created_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when the customer profile record was first created in the system. Used for audit trails and account age calculations.

attributes.name: account_status
attributes.description: Current lifecycle status of the customer account. Active accounts can transact; suspended accounts are temporarily restricted; closed accounts are permanently deactivated.
attributes.references: PCI DSS Requirement 8 - User Account Management

attributes.name: account_updated_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when the customer profile record was last modified. Used for change tracking and data freshness monitoring.

attributes.name: acquisition_channel
attributes.description: Marketing channel through which the customer was originally acquired. Used for CAC (Customer Acquisition Cost) analysis and ROAS (Return on Ad Spend) measurement. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: organic_search|paid_search|social_media|email|affiliate|direct|referral|mobile_app|marketplace|offline — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

attributes.name: acquisition_date
attributes.description: Date when the customer first registered or created an account. Used for cohort analysis and CLTV (Customer Lifetime Value) calculation.

attributes.name: cdp_profile_code
attributes.description: External identifier from the Customer Data Platform system. Used for unified profile orchestration and cross-system reconciliation.
attributes.references: Customer Data Platform - Unified Profile Standard

attributes.name: consent_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when the customer provided or last updated their marketing consent preferences. Required for GDPR consent audit trails.

attributes.name: customer_type
attributes.description: Classification of customer type: B2C (Business to Consumer individual shopper), B2B (Business to Business corporate buyer), C2C (Consumer to Consumer marketplace participant), marketplace_seller (third-party seller), or guest (one-time purchaser without account).

attributes.name: data_processing_consent
attributes.description: Indicates whether the customer has consented to data processing for analytics, personalization, and AI/ML model training. Required for GDPR and CCPA compliance.
attributes.references: GDPR Article 6 - Lawful Basis for Processing, CCPA Section 1798.100

attributes.name: date_of_birth
attributes.description: Date of birth of the customer. Used for age verification, compliance with age-restricted product regulations, and lifecycle marketing segmentation.
attributes.references: GDPR Article 4 - Personal Data Definition, COPPA Compliance

attributes.name: email
attributes.description: Primary email address for customer communication, order confirmations, marketing campaigns, and account recovery. Serves as the primary digital contact channel.
attributes.references: GDPR Article 4 - Personal Data Definition, CAN-SPAM Act

attributes.name: email_verified_flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether the customers email address has been verified through a confirmation link or OTP (One-Time Password). True if verified, False if pending verification.

attributes.name: first_name
attributes.description: Given name of the customer. Used for personalized communication and segmentation.

attributes.name: first_purchase_date
attributes.description: Date of the customers first completed order. Used to measure conversion from registration to first purchase and calculate time-to-first-purchase metrics.

attributes.name: full_name
attributes.description: Complete legal name of the customer as provided during registration or KYC verification. Used for identity verification and personalization.

attributes.name: gender
attributes.description: Self-identified gender of the customer. Used for personalization and demographic segmentation. Optional field respecting customer privacy preferences.
attributes.references: GDPR Article 9 - Special Categories of Personal Data

attributes.name: kyc_document_type
attributes.description: Type of identity document submitted for KYC verification. Used for compliance reporting and document expiry tracking. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: passport|drivers_license|national_id|business_license|tax_id|bank_statement|utility_bill — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

attributes.name: kyc_expiry_date
attributes.description: Date when the KYC verification expires and re-verification is required. Typically set based on regulatory requirements or risk assessment policies.
attributes.references: Financial Action Task Force (FATF) - Ongoing Due Diligence

attributes.name: kyc_failure_reason
attributes.description: Reason for KYC verification failure if status is failed. Examples: document expired, document unreadable, name mismatch, address mismatch, watchlist match. Used for customer support and fraud investigation.

attributes.name: kyc_risk_level
attributes.description: Risk assessment level assigned to the customer based on KYC verification results, transaction patterns, and fraud signals. Used for enhanced due diligence and transaction monitoring.
attributes.references: Financial Action Task Force (FATF) - Risk-Based Approach

attributes.name: kyc_verification_date
attributes.description: Date when the KYC identity verification was successfully completed. Used for compliance audit trails and re-verification scheduling.
attributes.references: Financial Action Task Force (FATF) - KYC Guidelines

attributes.name: kyc_verification_provider
attributes.description: Name of the third-party identity verification service provider used for KYC checks (e.g., Jumio, Onfido, Trulioo). Used for audit trails and provider performance analysis.

attributes.name: kyc_verification_status
attributes.description: Status of the KYC identity verification process. Required for high-value transactions, B2B accounts, and marketplace sellers to comply with anti-fraud and regulatory requirements.
attributes.references: Financial Action Task Force (FATF) - KYC Guidelines

attributes.name: kyc_verification_type
attributes.description: Type of identity verification method used for KYC compliance. Government ID includes passport, drivers license, national ID; business registration for B2B customers.

attributes.name: last_active_date
attributes.description: Date of the customers most recent interaction with any platform touchpoint (login, browse, cart activity, customer service contact). Used for engagement scoring and dormancy detection.

attributes.name: last_name
attributes.description: Family name or surname of the customer. Used for identity verification and customer matching.

attributes.name: last_purchase_date
attributes.description: Date of the customers most recent completed order. Used for recency scoring in RFM (Recency, Frequency, Monetary) analysis and churn prediction.

attributes.name: loyalty_points_balance
attributes.description: Current balance of loyalty reward points available for redemption. Points are earned through purchases, referrals, reviews, and promotional activities.

attributes.name: loyalty_tier
attributes.description: Current tier level in the customer loyalty program. Determines benefits such as free shipping, exclusive discounts, early access to sales, and priority customer service.

attributes.name: marketing_opt_in_email
attributes.description: Indicates whether the customer has consented to receive marketing emails. True if opted in, False if opted out. Required for GDPR and CAN-SPAM compliance.
attributes.references: GDPR Article 6 - Lawful Basis for Processing, CAN-SPAM Act

attributes.name: marketing_opt_in_push
attributes.description: Indicates whether the customer has consented to receive push notifications through the mobile app. True if opted in, False if opted out.

attributes.name: marketing_opt_in_sms
attributes.description: Indicates whether the customer has consented to receive marketing SMS messages. True if opted in, False if opted out. Required for TCPA compliance.

attributes.name: nps_score
attributes.description: Most recent NPS score provided by the customer on a scale of 0-10. Scores 9-10 are promoters, 7-8 are passives, 0-6 are detractors. Used for customer satisfaction measurement and churn prediction.

attributes.name: nps_survey_date
attributes.description: Date when the most recent NPS survey response was collected. Used for tracking sentiment trends over time.

attributes.name: phone
attributes.description: Primary contact phone number for customer service, delivery coordination, and two-factor authentication. Includes country code and area code.
attributes.references: GDPR Article 4 - Personal Data Definition, TCPA Compliance

attributes.name: phone_verified_flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether the customers phone number has been verified through SMS OTP or voice call verification. True if verified, False if pending verification.

attributes.name: preferred_currency
attributes.description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code representing the customers preferred currency for pricing display and transactions.

attributes.name: preferred_language
attributes.description: ISO 639-2 three-letter language code representing the customers preferred language for communication, user interface, and content delivery.

attributes.name: third_party_sharing_consent
attributes.description: Indicates whether the customer has consented to sharing their data with third-party partners for marketing or analytics purposes. Required for CCPA compliance.

products.name: account
products.description: Customer account record representing the commercial relationship between a customer and the Ecommerce platform. Tracks account type (B2C personal, B2B corporate, C2C marketplace), account tier, account status (active, suspended, closed, pending), account open date, account close date, credit limit, default payment method reference, default shipping address reference, loyalty program enrollment status, marketplace seller flag, B2B company name, tax ID, VAT number, and account manager assignment. One profile may have one or more accounts (e.g., personal + business). Owns the immutable account status change audit trail: each transition records previous status, new status, change reason code, change initiated by (customer, agent, automated rule, fraud system), change timestamp, effective date, reversal flag, and associated case reference. Provides full account lifecycle audit for compliance, dispute resolution, and customer service operations.

attributes.name: account_id
attributes.description: Unique identifier for the customer account. Primary key for the account entity.

attributes.name: account_customer_address_id
attributes.description: Reference to the customer's preferred shipping address for this account. Pre-populated at checkout for faster order placement.

attributes.name: account_default_billing_address_customer_address_id
attributes.description: Reference to the customer's preferred billing address for invoices and payment processing. May differ from shipping address for B2B accounts.

attributes.name: agent_id
attributes.description: Reference to the dedicated account manager or customer success representative assigned to this account. Typically assigned to Enterprise B2B accounts and VIP customers.

attributes.name: customer_profile_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to customer.customer_profile. Business justification: Account belongs to a single Customer Profile; adding account.customer_profile_id creates the necessary parent‑child relationship and eliminates the orphaned Account side.

attributes.name: default_payment_method_id
attributes.description: Reference to the customer's preferred payment method for this account. Used for one-click checkout and subscription billing.

attributes.name: default_wallet_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to payment.wallet. Business justification: Checkout wallet selection: e-commerce accounts need a designated default digital wallet for one-click checkout and balance-based payments. This FK supports wallet balance display at checkout, auto-deb

attributes.name: price_list_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to pricing.price_list. Business justification: REQUIRED: Account‑level price list assignment for corporate customers; finance reports aggregate spend by account using the linked price list.

attributes.name: account_number
attributes.description: Externally-visible unique account number used for customer communication, statements, and support interactions.

attributes.name: account_source
attributes.description: Channel through which the account was originally created. Used for Customer Acquisition Cost (CAC) analysis and channel attribution.

attributes.name: account_status
attributes.description: Current lifecycle status of the account. Active for operational accounts, Suspended for temporarily restricted accounts, Closed for permanently terminated accounts, Pending for accounts awaiting verification or approval.
attributes.references: Customer Data Platform - Account Lifecycle Management

attributes.name: account_tier
attributes.description: Service tier or membership level of the account determining benefits, pricing, and service levels. Standard for basic accounts, Premium for paid memberships, VIP for high-value customers, Enterprise for B2B corporate accounts.

attributes.name: account_type
attributes.description: Classification of the account based on the commercial relationship: B2C (Business to Consumer) for personal shopping accounts, B2B (Business to Business) for corporate procurement accounts, C2C (Consumer to Consumer) for marketplace seller accounts.

attributes.name: b2b_company_name
attributes.description: Legal company name for B2B corporate accounts. Null for B2C personal accounts. Used for invoicing, tax reporting, and corporate account management.

attributes.name: close_date
attributes.description: Date when the account was permanently closed. Null for active and suspended accounts. Used for churn analysis and retention reporting.

attributes.name: cltv_last_calculated_date
attributes.business_glossary_term: Customer Lifetime Value (CLTV) Last Calculated Date
attributes.description: Date when the CLTV score was last recalculated. CLTV is typically refreshed monthly or quarterly based on updated transaction data.
attributes.references: Customer Relationship Management (CRM) - CLTV Refresh Cadence

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when this account record was first created in the system. Used for audit trails and data lineage tracking.

attributes.name: credit_limit
attributes.description: Maximum credit amount extended to the account for B2B corporate accounts or premium consumer accounts. Null for standard pay-per-order accounts. Used for order approval and risk management.
attributes.references: Payment Card Industry Data Security Standard (PCI DSS) - Credit Management

attributes.name: credit_limit_currency
attributes.description: Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the credit limit amount (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).

attributes.name: customer_lifetime_value
attributes.description: Predicted total revenue expected from this account over its lifetime. Calculated by analytics models based on purchase history, frequency, and retention probability. Used for segmentation and marketing investment decisions.
attributes.references: Customer Relationship Management (CRM) - CLTV Modeling Standards

attributes.name: data_processing_consent
attributes.description: Indicates whether the account holder has consented to data processing for personalization, analytics, and AI/ML applications beyond core transaction processing. Required under GDPR.
attributes.references: General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR) Article 6 - Lawful Basis for Processing

attributes.name: data_processing_consent_date
attributes.description: Timestamp when the customer provided data processing consent. Required for GDPR compliance audit trails. Null if data_processing_consent is False.
attributes.references: General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR) Article 7 - Consent Records

attributes.name: last_login_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp of the most recent successful login to the account. Used for engagement analysis, dormancy detection, and security monitoring.

attributes.name: last_modified_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when this account record was last updated. Used for change tracking, audit trails, and data synchronization.

attributes.name: last_order_date
attributes.description: Date of the most recent order placed by this account. Used for recency-frequency-monetary (RFM) analysis and churn prediction. Null if no orders have been placed.
attributes.references: Order Management System (OMS) - Order History Tracking

attributes.name: loyalty_points_balance
attributes.description: Current balance of loyalty points or rewards currency available for redemption. Zero if not enrolled or no points earned.
attributes.references: Customer Relationship Management (CRM) - Loyalty Points Management

attributes.name: loyalty_program_enrolled
attributes.description: Indicates whether the account is enrolled in the company's loyalty or rewards program. True if enrolled, False otherwise.
attributes.references: Customer Relationship Management (CRM) - Loyalty Program Standards

attributes.name: loyalty_tier
attributes.description: Current tier level within the loyalty program based on spending or engagement. Null if not enrolled in loyalty program.
attributes.references: Customer Relationship Management (CRM) - Loyalty Tier Classification

attributes.name: marketing_opt_in
attributes.description: Indicates whether the account holder has consented to receive marketing communications (emails, SMS, push notifications). Managed under GDPR and CCPA consent requirements.
attributes.references: General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR) Article 7 - Consent Management

attributes.name: marketing_opt_in_date
attributes.description: Timestamp when the customer provided marketing consent. Required for GDPR and CCPA compliance audit trails. Null if marketing_opt_in is False.
attributes.references: General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR) Article 7 - Consent Records

attributes.name: marketplace_seller_flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether this account is also registered as a marketplace seller (C2C model). True if the account holder sells products on the platform, False for buyer-only accounts.

attributes.name: nps_score
attributes.description: Most recent Net Promoter Score (NPS) rating provided by the account holder, ranging from 0 (detractor) to 10 (promoter). Used for customer satisfaction measurement and churn prediction.
attributes.references: Net Promoter System - NPS Methodology Standards

attributes.name: nps_survey_date
attributes.description: Date when the most recent NPS survey response was collected. Null if the customer has never completed an NPS survey.
attributes.references: Net Promoter System - Survey Administration Standards

attributes.name: open_date
attributes.description: Date when the account was first opened and activated. Used for calculating account tenure and Customer Lifetime Value (CLTV).

attributes.name: outstanding_balance
attributes.description: Current outstanding balance owed by the account holder for credit-based accounts. Represents unpaid invoices and charges. Zero for prepaid accounts.
attributes.references: Generally Accepted Accounting Principles (GAAP) - Accounts Receivable

attributes.name: preferred_currency
attributes.description: Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the customer's preferred pricing and billing currency (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Used for multi-currency pricing display.

attributes.name: preferred_language
attributes.description: Two-letter ISO 639-1 language code representing the customer's preferred language for communication, emails, and user interface (e.g., en, es, fr, de).

attributes.name: referral_code
attributes.description: Referral or promotional code used during account registration. Used for tracking referral program effectiveness and attributing customer acquisition.

attributes.name: risk_score
attributes.description: Fraud risk score assigned to the account by fraud detection systems, typically ranging from 0 (low risk) to 100 (high risk). Used for transaction approval, account monitoring, and fraud prevention.
attributes.references: Payment Card Industry Data Security Standard (PCI DSS) - Fraud Detection

attributes.name: risk_score_last_updated
attributes.description: Timestamp when the fraud risk score was last recalculated. Risk scores are updated in real-time or near-real-time based on transaction patterns and fraud signals.
attributes.references: Payment Card Industry Data Security Standard (PCI DSS) - Risk Assessment

attributes.name: tax_identifier
attributes.description: Government-issued tax identification number for B2B accounts. Format varies by jurisdiction (e.g., EIN in USA, CRN in UK). Used for tax reporting and compliance.

attributes.name: total_order_count
attributes.description: Lifetime count of orders placed by this account. Used for customer segmentation and loyalty tier calculation.
attributes.references: Order Management System (OMS) - Order History Aggregation

attributes.name: vat_number
attributes.description: VAT registration number for B2B accounts in VAT-applicable jurisdictions (primarily EU). Used for VAT exemption and cross-border invoicing.
attributes.references: European Union VAT Directive - VAT Registration Standards

products.name: customer_address
products.description: Physical and virtual address records associated with a customer profile. Stores address type (shipping, billing, registered, pickup), address line 1 and 2, city, state/province, postal code, country ISO code, address validation status, geocoded latitude/longitude, is_default_shipping flag, is_default_billing flag, residential vs commercial classification, BOPIS-eligible flag, and address verification source. Supports multi-address customers and international address formats per ISO 3166.

attributes.name: customer_address_id
attributes.description: Unique identifier for the address record. Primary key.

attributes.name: customer_profile_id
attributes.description: Reference to the customer profile that owns this address. Links address to unified customer view in Customer Data Platform (CDP).
attributes.references: Customer Data Platform - Unified Customer Profile

attributes.name: delivery_zone_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to logistics.delivery_zone. Business justification: Address serviceability check at checkout: the system must determine which delivery zone a customer address falls into to display accurate SLA, apply residential/remote surcharges, and validate carrier

attributes.name: price_zone_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to pricing.price_zone. Business justification: Customer addresses determine applicable price zones for geographic pricing, regional tax rates, and delivery surcharges. E-commerce pricing engines resolve the correct price list by mapping a customer

attributes.name: address_status
attributes.description: Current lifecycle status of the address record. Active = currently usable, inactive = temporarily disabled, archived = historical record, flagged = requires review due to delivery failures or fraud indicators.
attributes.references: Customer Data Platform - Address Lifecycle Management

attributes.name: address_type
attributes.description: Classification of address purpose: shipping (delivery destination), billing (invoice address), registered (legal/account address), pickup (BOPIS location), return (RMA destination).

attributes.name: city
attributes.description: City or municipality name for the address. Used for delivery routing and tax jurisdiction determination.
attributes.references: Universal Postal Union (UPU) S42 Standard, GDPR Article 4

attributes.name: consent_marketing_flag
attributes.description: Boolean flag indicating whether the customer has consented to receive marketing communications at this address. Required for GDPR and CCPA compliance. Used by Marketing Automation Platform for campaign targeting.
attributes.references: GDPR Article 6 - Lawful Basis for Processing, CCPA Section 1798.120

attributes.name: consent_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when marketing consent was granted or last updated for this address. Required for GDPR and CCPA audit trail. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.
attributes.references: GDPR Article 7 - Conditions for Consent, CCPA

attributes.name: country_code
attributes.description: Three-letter ISO country code identifying the country of the address. Used for international shipping, customs, tax jurisdiction, and regulatory compliance (e.g., GDPR for EU countries).

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when this address record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail, data lineage, and GDPR compliance (right to access). Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.
attributes.references: ISO 8601 - Date and Time Format, GDPR Article 15

attributes.name: delivery_instructions
attributes.description: Special delivery instructions provided by the customer for this address (e.g., gate code, leave at door, ring bell, call upon arrival). Used by last-mile delivery carriers and 3PL (Third-Party Logistics) providers.

attributes.name: failed_delivery_count
attributes.description: Number of failed delivery attempts to this address. Used for address quality scoring and to flag problematic addresses. High count triggers address verification workflow.

attributes.name: first_used_date
attributes.description: Date when this address was first used for an order or transaction. Used for address age analysis and Customer Lifetime Value (CLTV) modeling.

attributes.name: geocoding_accuracy
attributes.description: Precision level of the geocoded coordinates. Rooftop = exact building location, range_interpolated = estimated within address range, geometric_center = center of postal code area, approximate = city/region level. Impacts delivery time estimation accuracy.
attributes.references: Google Geocoding API Standards, OpenStreetMap Geocoding Guidelines

attributes.name: is_bopis_eligible
attributes.business_glossary_term: Buy Online Pick Up In Store (BOPIS) Eligible Flag
attributes.description: Boolean flag indicating whether this address is eligible for BOPIS (Buy Online Pick Up In Store) fulfillment. True if address is within serviceable radius of a store or pickup location with inventory availability.
attributes.references: BOPIS Fulfillment Standards, Omnichannel Retail Guidelines

attributes.name: is_default_billing
attributes.description: Boolean flag indicating whether this is the customers preferred default billing address. Used for payment processing, invoice generation, and tax calculation. Only one address per customer should have this flag set to true.
attributes.references: PCI DSS - Payment Card Industry Data Security Standard

attributes.name: is_default_shipping
attributes.description: Boolean flag indicating whether this is the customers preferred default shipping address. Used for checkout pre-population and Order Management System (OMS) fulfillment routing. Only one address per customer should have this flag set to true.

attributes.name: is_military_address
attributes.description: Boolean flag indicating whether this is a military address (APO, FPO, DPO). Military addresses have special handling requirements, customs exemptions, and carrier restrictions. Used for fulfillment routing and compliance.

attributes.name: is_po_box
attributes.description: Boolean flag indicating whether this is a PO Box address. Some carriers and products cannot deliver to PO Boxes. Used for fulfillment eligibility checks and carrier selection.
attributes.references: USPS PO Box Standards, Carrier Delivery Restrictions

attributes.name: is_verified_by_customer
attributes.description: Boolean flag indicating whether the customer has explicitly confirmed this address is correct. Used to reduce failed deliveries and Return Merchandise Authorization (RMA) costs. Set to true after successful delivery or customer confirmation.

attributes.name: last_used_date
attributes.description: Date when this address was most recently used for an order or transaction. Used for address recency analysis and inactive address cleanup.

attributes.name: latitude
attributes.description: Geocoded latitude coordinate in decimal degrees format. Used for delivery route optimization, last-mile logistics planning, and proximity-based services. Range: -90 to +90.

attributes.name: line_1
attributes.description: Primary street address line including street number, street name, and unit/apartment/suite number. First line of physical or mailing address.
attributes.references: Universal Postal Union (UPU) S42 Standard, GDPR Article 4

attributes.name: line_2
attributes.description: Secondary address line for additional location details such as building name, floor, department, or c/o information. Optional supplementary address information.
attributes.references: Universal Postal Union (UPU) S42 Standard, GDPR Article 4

attributes.name: longitude
attributes.description: Geocoded longitude coordinate in decimal degrees format. Used for delivery route optimization, last-mile logistics planning, and proximity-based services. Range: -180 to +180.

attributes.name: nickname
attributes.description: Customer-defined friendly name for the address (e.g., Home, Office, Moms House, Vacation Home). Used for easy address selection during checkout in Customer Relationship Management (CRM) and e-commerce platforms.

attributes.name: phone_number
attributes.description: Contact phone number associated with this address for delivery coordination and customer communication. Used by carriers for delivery notifications and issue resolution. May differ from customer primary phone.

attributes.name: postal_code
attributes.description: Postal code, ZIP code, or postcode for the address. Used for delivery routing, carrier selection, and service level determination. Format varies by country (e.g., 5-digit ZIP in USA, alphanumeric in Canada/UK).
attributes.references: Universal Postal Union (UPU) S42 Standard, GDPR Article 4

attributes.name: residential_commercial_flag
attributes.description: Classification of address as residential (home delivery) or commercial (business delivery). Impacts carrier selection, delivery time windows, signature requirements, and shipping costs. Commercial addresses may have different Service Level Agreement (SLA) options.
attributes.references: Transportation Management System (TMS) - Carrier Selection Rules

attributes.name: serviceable_by_carrier
attributes.description: Comma-separated list of carrier codes that can service this address (e.g., UPS, FEDEX, USPS, DHL). Determined by Transportation Management System (TMS) based on carrier coverage maps and Service Level Agreement (SLA) capabilities. Used for carrier selection during fulfillment.

attributes.name: state_province
attributes.description: State, province, or region code or name. Used for tax calculation, shipping cost determination, and regulatory compliance (e.g., CCPA for California addresses).

attributes.name: tax_jurisdiction_code
attributes.description: Tax jurisdiction identifier for this address used for sales tax calculation and compliance. Links to tax rate tables in Enterprise Resource Planning (ERP) system. Critical for multi-state/multi-country tax compliance and SOX (Sarbanes-Oxley Act) reporting.
attributes.references: SOX - Sarbanes-Oxley Act, Sales Tax Compliance Standards

attributes.name: updated_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when this address record was last modified. Used for change tracking, audit trail, and data synchronization across Customer Data Platform (CDP) and downstream systems. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.
attributes.references: ISO 8601 - Date and Time Format, GDPR Article 15

attributes.name: usage_count
attributes.description: Total number of times this address has been used across all orders and transactions. Used for address preference analysis and fraud detection (unusually high usage may indicate drop-shipping or fraud).

attributes.name: validation_source
attributes.description: Name of the address verification service or postal authority used to validate the address (e.g., USPS, Google Maps API, Loqate, Melissa Data, Canada Post). Null if unvalidated.

attributes.name: validation_status
attributes.description: Status of address verification against postal authority databases. Validated = confirmed deliverable, unvalidated = not yet checked, failed = undeliverable, corrected = standardized by validation service, partial = some components verified.
attributes.references: USPS Address Validation Standards, Canada Post Addressing Guidelines

products.name: contact
products.description: Individual contact person associated with a customer account, primarily used for B2B accounts with multiple authorized contacts (procurement officers, finance contacts, shipping coordinators). Stores contact first name, last name, job title, department, email address, phone number, contact type (primary, billing, shipping, technical), is_primary flag, communication opt-in status, authorization level, and contact status. Enables multi-contact B2B account management, purchase approval routing, and targeted communication delivery to the correct role within an enterprise account.

attributes.name: contact_id
attributes.description: Unique identifier for the contact person. Primary key.

attributes.name: account_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to customer.account. Business justification: Contact belongs to an account (B2B); many contacts per account, add FK to associate contact with its owning account.

attributes.name: customer_address_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to customer.customer_address. Business justification: In B2B e-commerce, individual contacts (buyers, procurement officers, decision-makers) associated with an account often have their own mailing, shipping, or billing addresses distinct from the account

attributes.name: customer_profile_id
attributes.description: Reference to the parent customer account (typically B2B enterprise account) that this contact is associated with. Enables multi-contact account management.

attributes.name: reports_to_contact_id
attributes.description: Reference to the contact_id of the person this contact reports to within the customer organization. Enables organizational hierarchy mapping and approval escalation workflows.

attributes.name: approval_limit_amount
attributes.description: Maximum monetary amount (in account base currency) that this contact is authorized to approve for purchase orders without escalation. Used in B2B purchase approval workflows.
attributes.references: SOX Section 404 - Internal Controls, ISO/IEC 27001 - Information Security Management

attributes.name: assistant_email
attributes.description: Email address of the contact's executive assistant. Used for meeting scheduling and communication coordination in high-value B2B accounts.
attributes.references: GDPR Article 4 - Personal Data Definition, RFC 5322 - Internet Message Format

attributes.name: assistant_name
attributes.description: Name of the contact's executive assistant or administrative support person. Used for scheduling and communication routing in enterprise B2B accounts.

attributes.name: assistant_phone
attributes.description: Phone number of the contact's executive assistant. Used for urgent communication routing and meeting coordination.
attributes.references: GDPR Article 4 - Personal Data Definition, ITU-T E.164 - International Telephone Numbering

attributes.name: authorization_level
attributes.description: Level of authorization and permissions granted to the contact within the customer account. Determines purchase approval limits, account management capabilities, and data access rights.
attributes.references: NIST SP 800-53 - Security and Privacy Controls, ISO/IEC 27001 - Information Security Management

attributes.name: contact_status
attributes.description: Current lifecycle status of the contact record. Active contacts can receive communications and perform authorized actions; inactive contacts are retained for historical reference but cannot transact.

attributes.name: contact_type
attributes.description: Classification of the contact role within the account structure. Determines routing of specific communication types and approval workflows. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: primary|billing|shipping|technical|procurement|finance|legal — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when the contact record was first created in the system. Used for contact tenure analysis and audit trail.
attributes.references: ISO 8601 - Date and Time Format, SOX Section 404 - Internal Controls

attributes.name: deactivated_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when the contact was deactivated or marked inactive. Null for active contacts. Used for contact lifecycle analysis and retention reporting.

attributes.name: deactivation_reason
attributes.description: Free-text explanation of why the contact was deactivated (e.g., left company, role change, duplicate record, customer request). Used for contact churn analysis and data quality improvement.

attributes.name: department
attributes.description: Department or business unit within the customer organization that the contact belongs to (e.g., Procurement, Finance, Operations, IT). Enables department-level segmentation and targeted communications.

attributes.name: email_address
attributes.description: Primary email address for the contact. Used for order confirmations, purchase approvals, shipping notifications, and targeted B2B communications.
attributes.references: GDPR Article 4 - Personal Data Definition, RFC 5322 - Internet Message Format

attributes.name: email_opt_in
attributes.description: Boolean flag indicating whether the contact has opted in to receive marketing and promotional email communications. Managed under GDPR consent requirements and CAN-SPAM Act compliance.
attributes.references: GDPR Article 6 - Lawfulness of Processing, CAN-SPAM Act 15 USC 7704

attributes.name: email_verified_flag
attributes.description: Boolean flag indicating whether the contact's email address has been verified through a confirmation process. Used for security and communication deliverability assurance.

attributes.name: external_contact_code
attributes.description: Contact identifier from the source system or external CRM platform. Used for cross-system reconciliation and integration mapping.

attributes.name: first_name
attributes.description: First name or given name of the contact person. Used for personalized communication and identification within B2B account structures.

attributes.name: is_primary
attributes.description: Boolean flag indicating whether this contact is the primary point of contact for the customer account. Primary contacts receive all general account communications and have highest authorization level.

attributes.name: job_title
attributes.description: Job title or role of the contact within their organization (e.g., Procurement Manager, Finance Director, Shipping Coordinator). Used for routing communications and approvals to the appropriate role.

attributes.name: last_login_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp of the contact's most recent login to the customer portal or B2B platform. Used for engagement tracking and inactive contact identification.

attributes.name: last_name
attributes.description: Last name or family name of the contact person. Combined with first name for full contact identification.

attributes.name: linkedin_profile_url
attributes.description: URL to the contact's LinkedIn professional profile. Used for B2B relationship management, account intelligence, and professional network mapping.

attributes.name: mobile_number
attributes.description: Mobile phone number for the contact. Used for SMS notifications, two-factor authentication, and mobile-first communication channels.
attributes.references: GDPR Article 4 - Personal Data Definition, ITU-T E.164 - International Telephone Numbering

attributes.name: phone_number
attributes.description: Primary phone number for the contact. Used for order clarifications, delivery coordination, and urgent account communications.
attributes.references: GDPR Article 4 - Personal Data Definition, ITU-T E.164 - International Telephone Numbering

attributes.name: phone_opt_in
attributes.description: Boolean flag indicating whether the contact has opted in to receive phone call communications for marketing purposes. Managed under TCPA compliance and do-not-call registry requirements.
attributes.references: TCPA 47 USC 227, FTC Telemarketing Sales Rule

attributes.name: phone_verified_flag
attributes.description: Boolean flag indicating whether the contact's phone number has been verified through SMS or voice confirmation. Used for two-factor authentication and fraud prevention.

attributes.name: preferred_contact_method
attributes.description: Contact's preferred method of communication for non-urgent account matters. Used to optimize communication delivery and improve contact engagement rates.

attributes.name: preferred_language
attributes.description: Two-letter ISO 639-1 language code representing the contact's preferred language for communications (e.g., EN for English, ES for Spanish, FR for French). Used for localized email templates and customer service routing.

attributes.name: sms_opt_in
attributes.description: Boolean flag indicating whether the contact has opted in to receive SMS text message communications. Managed under TCPA compliance and GDPR consent requirements.
attributes.references: GDPR Article 6 - Lawfulness of Processing, TCPA 47 USC 227

attributes.name: source_system
attributes.description: Name of the operational system that originated this contact record (e.g., Customer Data Platform, Salesforce Marketing Cloud, Seller Management Portal). Used for data lineage tracking and system integration management.
attributes.references: ISO/IEC 11179 - Metadata Registries, DAMA-DMBOK Data Lineage

attributes.name: time_zone
attributes.description: IANA time zone identifier for the contact's location (e.g., America/New_York, Europe/London). Used for scheduling communications and calls during appropriate business hours.
attributes.references: IANA Time Zone Database, ISO 8601 - Date and Time Format

attributes.name: updated_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when the contact record was last modified. Used for data freshness tracking and change audit.
attributes.references: ISO 8601 - Date and Time Format, SOX Section 404 - Internal Controls

products.name: loyalty_program
products.description: Master definition of loyalty programs offered on the Ecommerce platform. Stores program name, program type (points-based, tier-based, cashback, subscription), program status, enrollment eligibility rules, points-to-currency conversion rate, tier thresholds, program start date, program end date, sponsoring brand or seller reference, terms and conditions URL, and applicable customer segments. This is the catalog-level definition of loyalty programs, distinct from individual customer enrollment records.

attributes.name: loyalty_program_id
attributes.description: Unique identifier for the loyalty program. Primary key.

attributes.name: campaign_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to marketing.campaign. Business justification: Loyalty programs are launched and sustained via marketing campaigns. Linking loyalty_program to its associated campaign enables spend attribution, enrollment ROI, and campaign budget allocation report

attributes.name: promotional_campaign_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to pricing.promotional_campaign. Business justification: Loyalty programs are backed by promotional campaigns for launch periods, bonus-point events, and seasonal multipliers. Linking enables finance to track promotional spend attributed to loyalty programs

attributes.name: scoped_category_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to product.category. Business justification: Category-scoped loyalty programs (e.g., double points on Electronics) are a standard e-commerce promotional mechanic. Program managers configure which product category earns bonus points. A domain exp

attributes.name: seller_profile_id
attributes.description: Reference to the marketplace seller entity that sponsors this loyalty program. Applicable for seller-specific programs.

attributes.name: sponsoring_brand_id
attributes.description: Reference to the brand entity that sponsors or owns this loyalty program. Nullable for platform-wide programs.

attributes.name: applicable_customer_segments
attributes.description: Comma-separated list or description of customer segments eligible for this loyalty program (e.g., B2C, B2B, premium customers, geographic regions).

attributes.name: auto_enrollment_flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether customers are automatically enrolled in this loyalty program upon account creation (True) or must opt-in manually (False).
attributes.references: GDPR Article 7 - Conditions for consent, CCPA Section 1798.120

attributes.name: cashback_percentage
attributes.description: Percentage of purchase amount returned as cashback to the customer. Applicable for cashback and hybrid programs.

attributes.name: created_by_user
attributes.description: Username or identifier of the user who created this loyalty program record.

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when this loyalty program record was first created in the system.

attributes.name: currency_code
attributes.description: Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the loyalty program's monetary transactions and conversions.

attributes.name: current_enrollment_count
attributes.description: Current number of customers actively enrolled in this loyalty program.

attributes.name: earn_rate_multiplier
attributes.description: Multiplier applied to base earning rate for this program (e.g., 2.0 means customers earn points at twice the standard rate).

attributes.name: end_date
attributes.description: The date when the loyalty program terminates. Nullable for open-ended programs.

attributes.name: enrollment_eligibility_rules
attributes.description: Business rules defining which customer segments or criteria qualify for enrollment in this loyalty program (e.g., geographic restrictions, customer type requirements, minimum purchase thresholds).

attributes.name: last_modified_by_user
attributes.description: Username or identifier of the user who last modified this loyalty program record.

attributes.name: last_modified_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when this loyalty program record was last updated.

attributes.name: max_enrollment_count
attributes.description: Maximum number of customers allowed to enroll in this loyalty program. Null indicates no enrollment cap.

attributes.name: partner_coalition_flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether this loyalty program is part of a coalition with external partner brands (True) or is a standalone program (False).

attributes.name: points_currency_conversion_rate
attributes.description: The conversion rate from loyalty points to base currency (e.g., 100 points = 1 USD). Applicable for points-based and hybrid programs.

attributes.name: points_expiration_period_days
attributes.description: Number of days after which earned loyalty points expire if not redeemed. Null indicates points do not expire.

attributes.name: privacy_policy_url
attributes.description: URL link to the privacy policy explaining how customer data is collected, used, and protected within the loyalty program.
attributes.references: GDPR Article 13 - Information to be provided, CCPA Section 1798.100

attributes.name: program_code
attributes.description: Unique business identifier code for the loyalty program, used for external references and integrations.

attributes.name: program_description
attributes.description: Detailed description of the loyalty program benefits, features, and value proposition for marketing and customer communication.

attributes.name: program_manager_email
attributes.description: Email address of the program manager for internal contact and escalation purposes.

attributes.name: program_manager_name
attributes.description: Name of the business owner or manager responsible for this loyalty program.

attributes.name: program_name
attributes.description: The marketing name of the loyalty program displayed to customers.

attributes.name: program_status
attributes.description: Current lifecycle status of the loyalty program: active (accepting enrollments and accruals), inactive (not accepting new enrollments), suspended (temporarily paused), draft (under development), or archived (historical record).

attributes.name: program_type
attributes.description: Classification of the loyalty program structure: points-based (earn and redeem points), tier-based (status levels with benefits), cashback (percentage return), subscription (paid membership), hybrid (combination), or coalition (multi-brand partnership).

attributes.name: redemption_minimum_points
attributes.description: Minimum number of points required before a customer can redeem rewards. Applicable for points-based programs.

attributes.name: start_date
attributes.description: The date when the loyalty program becomes active and customers can begin enrolling.

attributes.name: subscription_billing_frequency
attributes.description: Frequency at which subscription fees are charged: monthly, quarterly, annual, or one-time.

attributes.name: subscription_fee_amount
attributes.description: Recurring fee amount charged for subscription-based loyalty programs (e.g., annual membership fee).

attributes.name: terms_conditions_url
attributes.business_glossary_term: Terms and Conditions Uniform Resource Locator (URL)
attributes.description: URL link to the full terms and conditions document governing the loyalty program, required for regulatory compliance and customer transparency.
attributes.references: GDPR Article 13 - Information to be provided, FTC Act Section 5

attributes.name: tier_threshold_bronze
attributes.description: Minimum points or spend required to achieve bronze tier status. Applicable for tier-based programs.

attributes.name: tier_threshold_gold
attributes.description: Minimum points or spend required to achieve gold tier status. Applicable for tier-based programs.

attributes.name: tier_threshold_platinum
attributes.description: Minimum points or spend required to achieve platinum tier status. Applicable for tier-based programs.

attributes.name: tier_threshold_silver
attributes.description: Minimum points or spend required to achieve silver tier status. Applicable for tier-based programs.

products.name: loyalty_enrollment
products.description: Customer enrollment and ongoing loyalty standing for a specific loyalty program. Tracks enrollment date, current tier (Bronze, Silver, Gold, Platinum), tier qualification date, tier expiry date, current points balance, lifetime points earned, lifetime points redeemed, points expiry date, enrollment channel, enrollment status (active, suspended, expired), and next tier threshold gap. Also owns the complete loyalty point transaction history: each transaction records type (earn, redeem, expire, adjust, transfer), points amount, transaction date, triggering order reference, triggering promotion reference, balance before, balance after, expiry date of earned points, transaction status, and reversal reference. Serves as the SSOT for a customer's loyalty standing, points balance, and full transaction audit trail supporting dispute resolution and financial reconciliation of loyalty currency.

attributes.name: loyalty_enrollment_id
attributes.description: Unique identifier for the loyalty program enrollment record. Primary key for this entity.

attributes.name: account_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to customer.account. Business justification: A loyalty enrollment should be tied to the customer's commercial account entity, not just the profile. In B2B and B2C e-commerce, the account is the transactional relationship holder — it carries loya

attributes.name: enrollment_channel_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to marketing.channel. Business justification: Channel performance analysis for loyalty acquisition: which marketing channels (email, push, social, in-app) drive loyalty enrollments is a standard e-commerce KPI. Replaces denormalized string 'enrol

attributes.name: loyalty_program_id
attributes.description: Unique identifier of the loyalty program in which the customer is enrolled. Supports multiple concurrent loyalty programs.

attributes.name: marketplace_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to marketplace.marketplace. Business justification: Loyalty program enrollments are marketplace-scoped in multi-marketplace platforms (e.g., Prime on Amazon.com vs Amazon.co.uk). Benefit application, points earning rules, and settlement reporting requi

attributes.name: primary_loyalty_customer_profile_id
attributes.description: Unique identifier of the customer enrolled in the loyalty program. Links to the customer master record.

attributes.name: promotional_campaign_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to pricing.promotional_campaign. Business justification: Loyalty enrollments are driven by specific promotional campaigns (e.g., double-points enrollment bonuses). Linking enables campaign ROI reporting on enrollment lift and replaces the denormalized enrol

attributes.name: source_campaign_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to marketing.campaign. Business justification: Loyalty acquisition attribution: linking enrollment to the originating campaign enables campaign ROI measurement (cost-per-enrollment, LTV of campaign-acquired loyalty members). E-commerce marketers r

attributes.name: auto_renewal_enabled
attributes.description: Indicates whether the loyalty membership is set to automatically renew at the end of the term. Applicable for paid or subscription-based loyalty programs.

attributes.name: cancellation_date
attributes.description: Date when the loyalty enrollment was cancelled. Null if enrollment has never been cancelled.

attributes.name: cancellation_reason
attributes.description: Reason for cancellation of the loyalty enrollment, if status is cancelled. Examples include customer request, fraud, inactivity, or program termination.

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when this loyalty enrollment record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage.

attributes.name: current_points_balance
attributes.description: Current available points balance that the customer can redeem. Represents the net of all earned, redeemed, expired, and adjusted points. This is the real-time spendable loyalty currency balance.
attributes.references: ISO 4217 - Currency Codes (loyalty points as virtual currency)

attributes.name: current_tier
attributes.description: Current tier or level of the customer within the loyalty program hierarchy. Determines benefits, earning rates, and redemption privileges. Common tiers include Bronze, Silver, Gold, Platinum, Diamond, and VIP.

attributes.name: enrollment_date
attributes.description: Date when the customer enrolled in the loyalty program. Marks the beginning of the customer's loyalty relationship.

attributes.name: enrollment_number
attributes.description: Human-readable unique enrollment number or membership number assigned to the customer for this loyalty program. Used for customer service and member identification.

attributes.name: enrollment_status
attributes.description: Current lifecycle status of the loyalty enrollment. Active indicates the member can earn and redeem points; suspended indicates temporary hold; expired indicates membership lapsed; cancelled indicates voluntary or involuntary termination; pending indicates enrollment awaiting activation.

attributes.name: last_activity_date
attributes.description: Date of the most recent loyalty activity (earn, redeem, or adjustment). Used for inactivity detection and dormancy rules.

attributes.name: last_earn_date
attributes.description: Date when the customer last earned loyalty points. Used for engagement scoring, churn prediction, and reactivation campaigns.

attributes.name: last_redeem_date
attributes.description: Date when the customer last redeemed loyalty points. Used for engagement analysis and redemption behavior modeling.

attributes.name: lifetime_points_earned
attributes.description: Cumulative total of all points earned by the customer since enrollment. Used for tier qualification, lifetime value analysis, and member recognition. This is a monotonically increasing counter.
attributes.references: ISO 4217 - Currency Codes (loyalty points as virtual currency)

attributes.name: lifetime_points_expired
attributes.description: Cumulative total of all points that have expired due to inactivity or program rules since enrollment. Used for breakage analysis and financial reconciliation.
attributes.references: ISO 4217 - Currency Codes (loyalty points as virtual currency)

attributes.name: lifetime_points_redeemed
attributes.description: Cumulative total of all points redeemed by the customer since enrollment. Used for engagement analysis and redemption behavior modeling.
attributes.references: ISO 4217 - Currency Codes (loyalty points as virtual currency)

attributes.name: membership_fee_currency
attributes.description: Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the membership fee. Used for multi-currency loyalty programs.

attributes.name: membership_fee_paid
attributes.description: Total membership fee paid by the customer for enrollment or renewal in the loyalty program. Zero for free programs.

attributes.name: next_tier_gap
attributes.description: Remaining points or spend needed to reach the next tier. Calculated as next_tier_threshold minus current qualifying activity. Used for personalized tier progression campaigns.

attributes.name: next_tier_threshold
attributes.description: Points or spend threshold required to qualify for the next loyalty tier. Used for gamification and tier progression messaging. Null if customer is already at the highest tier.

attributes.name: opt_in_email
attributes.description: Indicates whether the customer has opted in to receive loyalty program communications via email.

attributes.name: opt_in_marketing
attributes.description: Indicates whether the customer has opted in to receive marketing communications related to the loyalty program. Supports GDPR and CCPA consent management.

attributes.name: opt_in_push_notification
attributes.description: Indicates whether the customer has opted in to receive loyalty program push notifications via mobile app.

attributes.name: opt_in_sms
attributes.description: Indicates whether the customer has opted in to receive loyalty program communications via SMS text messages.

attributes.name: points_expiry_date
attributes.description: Date when the next batch of points is scheduled to expire. Used for customer communication and expiry alerts. Null if no points are scheduled to expire or program has no expiration policy.

attributes.name: reactivation_date
attributes.description: Date when a previously suspended or expired enrollment was reactivated. Used for win-back campaign analysis.

attributes.name: referral_code
attributes.description: Referral code used by the customer during enrollment, if applicable. Links to the referring customer for referral bonus attribution.

attributes.name: suspension_date
attributes.description: Date when the loyalty enrollment was suspended. Null if enrollment has never been suspended.

attributes.name: suspension_reason
attributes.description: Reason for suspension of the loyalty enrollment, if status is suspended. Examples include fraud investigation, terms violation, payment failure, or customer request.

attributes.name: tier_expiry_date
attributes.description: Date when the current tier status expires and the customer must re-qualify or be downgraded. Null for lifetime tiers or programs without tier expiration.

attributes.name: tier_qualification_date
attributes.description: Date when the customer qualified for their current loyalty tier. Used to track tier tenure and calculate tier anniversary dates.

attributes.name: tier_qualifying_period_end
attributes.description: End date of the current tier qualification period. After this date, tier status is re-evaluated based on qualifying activity.

attributes.name: tier_qualifying_period_start
attributes.description: Start date of the current tier qualification period. Used to calculate rolling qualification windows and reset tier progress.

attributes.name: tier_qualifying_spend
attributes.description: Total spend amount that counts toward tier qualification in the current qualification period. Used when tier status is based on spend rather than points earned.

attributes.name: updated_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when this loyalty enrollment record was last updated. Used for audit trail and change tracking.

products.name: segment
products.description: Segmentation entity defining named customer segments and managing complete membership lifecycle. Stores segment definition: name, type (behavioral, demographic, RFM-based, CLTV-based, lifecycle stage), rule expression or ML model reference, segment size, creation date, last refresh date, owning team, status, and applicable channels. Owns membership records: each membership links a profile to a segment with temporal window (start/end dates), status (active, expired), assignment method (rule-based, manual, ML-model), confidence score for ML assignments, and segment version at assignment time. Segments are managed by the Customer Data Platform and consumed by Marketing Automation. Supports point-in-time membership queries, A/B testing audience construction, segment overlap analysis, and version tracking.

attributes.name: segment_id
attributes.description: Unique identifier for the customer segment. Primary key.

attributes.name: target_product_category_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to product.product_category. Business justification: Needed for segment‑to‑category targeting: segments are defined by the product categories they are intended to reach in marketing reports.

attributes.name: activated_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when the segment was first activated and made available for campaign targeting and personalization. Null if segment has never been activated.
attributes.references: Customer Data Platform - Segment Lifecycle Management

attributes.name: applicable_channels
attributes.description: Comma-separated list of marketing and engagement channels where this segment is intended to be used (e.g., email, SMS, push notification, display ads, social media, in-app messaging). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: email|sms|push|display|social|in_app|direct_mail|call_center|web_personalization|mobile_app — promote to reference product]
attributes.references: Marketing Automation Platform - Channel Management Standards

attributes.name: assignment_method
attributes.description: Method used to assign customers to this segment: rule_based (criteria-driven), ml_model (machine learning prediction), manual (curated list), hybrid (combination of methods).
attributes.references: Customer Data Platform - Segmentation Methodology Standards

attributes.name: baseline_metric_value
attributes.description: Baseline or benchmark value for the performance_metric_kpi, used to measure segment lift and incremental impact. Established at segment creation or from control group analysis.
attributes.references: Marketing Automation Platform - A/B Testing Framework

attributes.name: business_owner
attributes.description: Name or identifier of the individual business owner accountable for segment strategy, performance, and compliance.
attributes.references: Customer Data Platform - Segment Governance Framework

attributes.name: ccpa_compliant_flag
attributes.business_glossary_term: California Consumer Privacy Act (CCPA) Compliant Flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether this segment definition and processing logic comply with CCPA requirements for consumer rights, opt-out mechanisms, and data sale restrictions (True) or require review (False).

attributes.name: consent_purpose
attributes.description: Specific consent purpose or legal basis for processing customer data in this segment (e.g., marketing communications, personalized recommendations, analytics). Required when consent_required_flag is True.
attributes.references: GDPR Article 6 - Lawfulness of Processing, CCPA Section 1798.100

attributes.name: consent_required_flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether explicit customer consent is required for inclusion in this segment under GDPR, CCPA, or other privacy regulations (True) or if legitimate interest applies (False).
attributes.references: GDPR Article 6 - Lawfulness of Processing, CCPA Section 1798.100

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when the segment definition was first created in the Customer Data Platform. Immutable audit field.

attributes.name: deactivated_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when the segment was deactivated or archived. Null if segment is currently active or has never been deactivated.
attributes.references: Customer Data Platform - Segment Lifecycle Management

attributes.name: exclusion_rules
attributes.description: Logical expression defining criteria for excluding customers from this segment (e.g., opted out of marketing, fraudulent account, employee account, test account).
attributes.references: Customer Data Platform - Segment Exclusion Policy

attributes.name: gdpr_compliant_flag
attributes.business_glossary_term: General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR) Compliant Flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether this segment definition and processing logic comply with GDPR requirements for lawful processing, data minimization, and purpose limitation (True) or require review (False).
attributes.references: GDPR Article 5 - Principles Relating to Processing

attributes.name: last_refresh_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp of the most recent segment membership recalculation and update. Used to track data freshness and SLA compliance.
attributes.references: Customer Data Platform - Segment Refresh Audit Trail

attributes.name: member_count
attributes.description: Current number of active customer profiles assigned to this segment. Updated with each segment refresh.

attributes.name: ml_model_reference
attributes.description: Reference identifier or URI to the ML model used for predictive or propensity-based segment assignment (e.g., churn prediction model, next-best-offer model). Null for rule-based segments.
attributes.references: Customer Data Platform - ML Model Registry

attributes.name: modified_by
attributes.description: User identifier or email of the person who last modified the segment definition. Used for audit trail and change tracking.
attributes.references: Customer Data Platform - Audit Trail Standards

attributes.name: modified_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp of the most recent modification to the segment definition (name, rules, configuration). Updated with each change.

attributes.name: next_refresh_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp of the next scheduled segment membership recalculation. Null for on-demand or real-time segments.
attributes.references: Customer Data Platform - Segment Refresh Scheduling

attributes.name: notes
attributes.description: Free-text field for additional context, business rationale, testing results, or operational notes about the segment. Used for knowledge sharing and documentation.
attributes.references: Customer Data Platform - Segment Documentation Standards

attributes.name: overlap_allowed_flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether customers can be members of this segment and other segments simultaneously (True) or if this segment enforces mutual exclusivity (False).
attributes.references: Customer Data Platform - Segment Overlap Management Policy

attributes.name: owning_team
attributes.description: Business team or department responsible for defining, maintaining, and governing this segment (e.g., Marketing, CRM, Analytics, Retention).
attributes.references: Customer Data Platform - Segment Governance Framework

attributes.name: performance_metric_kpi
attributes.business_glossary_term: Segment Performance Metric Key Performance Indicator (KPI)
attributes.description: Primary KPI used to measure segment performance and business impact (e.g., CVR, ROAS, CLTV, NPS, churn rate, AOV). Used for segment effectiveness tracking and optimization.
attributes.references: Marketing Automation Platform - Campaign Performance Standards

attributes.name: priority
attributes.description: Business priority level for this segment, used for resource allocation, refresh scheduling, and conflict resolution when customers qualify for multiple segments.
attributes.references: Customer Data Platform - Segment Prioritization Policy

attributes.name: refresh_frequency
attributes.description: Cadence at which segment membership is recalculated and updated: real_time (streaming), hourly, daily, weekly, monthly, on_demand (manual trigger).
attributes.references: Customer Data Platform - Segment Refresh Policy

attributes.name: retention_period_days
attributes.description: Number of days that segment membership records are retained for compliance, audit, and historical analysis purposes before archival or deletion.
attributes.references: GDPR Article 5 - Principles Relating to Processing, CCPA Section 1798.105

attributes.name: rule_expression
attributes.description: Logical expression or SQL-like query defining the segment membership criteria for rule-based segments (e.g., AOV > 200 AND purchase_count >= 3 AND last_purchase_days <= 90). Null for ML-model-based segments.
attributes.references: Customer Data Platform - Segment Rule Engine Specification

attributes.name: segment_description
attributes.description: Detailed business description of the segment purpose, target audience characteristics, and intended use cases (e.g., marketing campaigns, personalization, retention programs).
attributes.references: Customer Data Platform - Segment Documentation Standards

attributes.name: segment_name
attributes.description: Human-readable name of the customer segment (e.g., High-Value Shoppers, Cart Abandoners, Loyal Customers).
attributes.references: Customer Data Platform - Segment Naming Standards

attributes.name: segment_status
attributes.description: Current lifecycle status of the segment: active (in use), inactive (paused), draft (under development), archived (historical reference), deprecated (replaced by newer version).
attributes.references: Customer Data Platform - Segment Lifecycle Management

attributes.name: segment_type
attributes.description: Classification of the segment based on the segmentation methodology: behavioral (purchase patterns, engagement), demographic (age, gender, income), RFM (Recency Frequency Monetary), CLTV (Customer Lifetime Value), lifecycle (new, active, at-risk, churned), geographic (location-based), or psychographic (interests, values). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: behavioral|demographic|rfm|cltv|lifecycle|geographic|psychographic — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

attributes.name: tags
attributes.description: Comma-separated list of user-defined tags or labels for segment categorization, search, and filtering (e.g., holiday_campaign, high_value, retention, test_segment).
attributes.references: Customer Data Platform - Segment Metadata Standards

attributes.name: target_size
attributes.description: Intended or target number of members for this segment, used for capacity planning and campaign sizing. Null if no target is defined.
attributes.references: Marketing Automation Platform - Campaign Planning Standards

attributes.name: use_case
