attributes.description: The resulting on-hand inventory quantity after the adjustment is applied. Calculated as quantity_before_adjustment + adjusted_quantity.

attributes.name: quantity_before_adjustment
attributes.description: The on-hand inventory quantity recorded in the system immediately prior to the adjustment. Provides audit trail for variance analysis.

attributes.name: reason_code
attributes.description: Detailed reason code providing granular classification for the adjustment. Used for root cause analysis and loss prevention reporting.
attributes.references: Internal Inventory Control Policy, SOX Section 404 - Internal Controls

attributes.name: reversal_flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether this adjustment is a reversal of a previously posted adjustment. True when correcting an erroneous prior adjustment.
attributes.references: GAAP - Error Corrections, IAS 8 - Accounting Policies

attributes.name: source_system
attributes.description: The originating system or interface that created the adjustment record. Supports data lineage and integration troubleshooting.
attributes.references: ISO/IEC 27001 - Information Security, Data Governance Framework

attributes.name: unit_cost
attributes.description: The per-unit cost of the SKU at the time of adjustment, used to calculate the financial impact. Sourced from the Enterprise Resource Planning (ERP) system's inventory valuation layer.
attributes.references: GAAP - Inventory Valuation, IAS 2 - Cost Formulas

attributes.name: unit_of_measure
attributes.description: The unit in which the adjusted quantity is expressed. Must align with the SKU's base UOM in the Product Information Management (PIM) system. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: each|case|pallet|kg|lb|liter|gallon — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

products.name: transfer_order
products.description: Transactional record managing the inter-warehouse or inter-location movement of inventory. Captures source warehouse, destination warehouse, SKU, lot, transfer quantity, transfer type (replenishment, rebalancing, returns processing), requested date, confirmed ship date, in-transit quantity, and transfer status (created, picked, shipped, received, closed). Supports multi-node inventory rebalancing and fulfillment network optimization.
products.reference: Stock Transfer Order / STO (SAP MM / WMS)

attributes.name: transfer_order_id
attributes.description: Unique identifier for the transfer order. Primary key for the transfer order entity.

attributes.name: carrier_id
attributes.description: Identifier of the transportation carrier handling the transfer shipment. Links to carrier master data for tracking and performance analysis.
attributes.references: ISO 6523 - Structure for Identification of Organizations

attributes.name: carrier_rate_card_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to logistics.carrier_rate_card. Business justification: Freight cost audit and financial reconciliation: transfer orders carry a freight_cost field that must be auditable against the rate card applied. Finance and procurement teams require this link to val

attributes.name: carrier_service_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to logistics.carrier_service. Business justification: Freight cost reconciliation and SLA tracking: inter-warehouse transfer orders use specific carrier service levels (LTL, FTL, express). Existing FK covers carrier but not service tier. Operations teams

attributes.name: cost_center_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Inter‑warehouse transfer expenses are allocated to a cost center for internal charge‑back and profitability reporting.

attributes.name: destination_warehouse_warehouse_node_id
attributes.description: Identifier of the warehouse or fulfillment center to which inventory is being transferred.
attributes.references: ISO 6523 - Structure for Identification of Organizations

attributes.name: general_ledger_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.general_ledger. Business justification: Inter-warehouse transfer orders incur freight costs and inventory revaluation postings to specific GL accounts. Finance teams track transfer costs by GL account for cost allocation reporting. No exist

attributes.name: primary_transfer_warehouse_node_id
attributes.description: Identifier of the warehouse or fulfillment center from which inventory is being transferred.
attributes.references: ISO 6523 - Structure for Identification of Organizations

attributes.name: rma_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to service.rma. Business justification: RMAs frequently trigger transfer orders to move returned goods from receiving warehouses to returns processing or refurbishment centers. transfer_order has reason_code (plain) but no FK to rma. Revers

attributes.name: seller_profile_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to seller.seller_profile. Business justification: Needed for Transfer Order Ownership Report, attributing inter‑warehouse transfers to the responsible seller.

attributes.name: sku_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to product.sku. Business justification: Transfer orders move inventory of a particular SKU between warehouses; FK enables validation of SKU attributes and regulatory flags.

attributes.name: source_warehouse_location_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to inventory.warehouse_location. Business justification: A transfer order moves inventory FROM a specific source warehouse location (bin/shelf). The existing source_location_code (STRING) on transfer_order is a denormalized reference to warehouse_location.l

attributes.name: approved_by_user_code
attributes.description: Identifier of the user who approved the transfer order. Nullable until approval is granted. Used for authorization audit trail.

attributes.name: approved_date
attributes.description: Date when the transfer order was approved by inventory management or warehouse operations. Nullable for orders not yet approved.

attributes.name: cancellation_reason
attributes.description: Explanation for why the transfer order was cancelled. Nullable for non-cancelled orders. Used for process improvement analysis.

attributes.name: cancelled_by_user_code
attributes.description: Identifier of the user who cancelled the transfer order. Nullable for non-cancelled orders. Used for exception tracking and audit.

attributes.name: closed_timestamp
attributes.description: Date and time when the transfer order was closed after successful receipt and reconciliation. Nullable until order is closed. Used for cycle time analysis.

attributes.name: created_by_user_code
attributes.description: Identifier of the user or system that created the transfer order. Used for audit trail and accountability.

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: Date and time when the transfer order record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and order age analysis.

attributes.name: currency_code
attributes.description: Three-letter ISO currency code for freight cost. Enables multi-currency transfer cost tracking.

attributes.name: destination_location_code
attributes.description: Specific bin, aisle, or zone location within the destination warehouse where inventory will be stored. Used for putaway planning.

attributes.name: expected_delivery_date
attributes.description: Estimated date when the transfer shipment will arrive at the destination warehouse. Used for receiving planning and inventory availability forecasting.

attributes.name: freight_cost
attributes.description: Transportation cost for the transfer shipment in the company's base currency. Used for transfer cost allocation and network optimization analysis.

attributes.name: in_transit_quantity
attributes.description: Quantity currently in transit between warehouses. Used for real-time inventory visibility and available-to-promise calculations.

attributes.name: is_cross_dock
attributes.description: Flag indicating whether this transfer bypasses storage and goes directly to outbound shipping. Used for fast-moving inventory optimization.
attributes.references: APICS Supply Chain Operations Reference (SCOR) Model

attributes.name: is_hazmat
attributes.description: Flag indicating whether the transferred inventory contains hazardous materials requiring special handling and compliance documentation.
attributes.references: US DOT Hazardous Materials Regulations (49 CFR)

attributes.name: lot_number
attributes.description: Lot or batch identifier for inventory tracking. Used for FIFO/LIFO inventory management and traceability.

attributes.name: modified_timestamp
attributes.description: Date and time when the transfer order record was last modified. Used for change tracking and data quality monitoring.

attributes.name: notes
attributes.description: Free-text notes or special instructions for the transfer order. Captures handling requirements, quality concerns, or operational exceptions.

attributes.name: picked_date
attributes.description: Date when inventory was picked from source warehouse location. Nullable until picking is completed.

attributes.name: priority_level
attributes.description: Priority classification for processing the transfer order. Determines sequencing in warehouse pick queues and carrier selection.

attributes.name: reason_code
attributes.description: Detailed reason code for the transfer. Provides granular classification for transfer analytics and root cause analysis. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: stock_out|demand_spike|seasonal_prep|excess_inventory|product_recall|damage|expiry|network_optimization — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
attributes.references: APICS Supply Chain Operations Reference (SCOR) Model

attributes.name: received_date
attributes.description: Date when inventory was received and checked in at the destination warehouse. Nullable until receipt is completed.

attributes.name: received_quantity
attributes.description: Actual quantity received at destination warehouse. May differ from transfer quantity due to damage, loss, or discrepancies.

attributes.name: requested_date
attributes.description: Date by which the transfer is requested to be completed. Used for replenishment planning and fulfillment network optimization.

attributes.name: shipment_method
attributes.description: Transportation mode used for the transfer. Determines transit time and cost for network optimization.

attributes.name: shipped_date
attributes.description: Date when the transfer shipment departed the source warehouse. Used for in-transit inventory tracking and SLA monitoring.

attributes.name: temperature_controlled
attributes.description: Flag indicating whether the transfer requires temperature-controlled transportation. Used for cold chain compliance and carrier selection.

attributes.name: tracking_number
attributes.description: Carrier-provided tracking number for the transfer shipment. Used for real-time shipment visibility and exception management.

attributes.name: transfer_order_number
attributes.description: Externally-visible business identifier for the transfer order. Human-readable reference number used in warehouse operations and tracking systems.

attributes.name: transfer_quantity
attributes.description: Quantity of units being transferred from source to destination warehouse. Measured in the SKU's base unit of measure.

attributes.name: transfer_status
attributes.description: Current lifecycle status of the transfer order. Tracks the order through warehouse operations from creation to completion. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: created|approved|picked|packed|shipped|in_transit|received|closed|cancelled — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
attributes.references: APICS Supply Chain Operations Reference (SCOR) Model

attributes.name: transfer_type
attributes.description: Classification of the transfer order purpose. Indicates the business reason for the inter-warehouse movement.
attributes.references: APICS Supply Chain Operations Reference (SCOR) Model

attributes.name: unit_of_measure
attributes.description: Unit of measure for the transfer quantity. Defines how the transferred inventory is counted and tracked.

attributes.name: variance_quantity
attributes.description: Difference between transfer quantity and received quantity. Positive indicates overage, negative indicates shortage. Used for inventory reconciliation.

products.name: safety_stock_rule
products.description: Master record defining safety stock parameters for a SKU at a warehouse node, calculated to buffer against demand variability and supply uncertainty. Captures safety stock quantity, safety stock days of cover, calculation method (statistical, fixed, seasonal), demand variability coefficient, supplier lead time variability, service level target percentage, and last recalculation date. Supports dynamic safety stock optimization.
products.reference: Safety Stock Policy / Buffer Stock Parameters

attributes.name: safety_stock_rule_id
attributes.description: Unique identifier for the safety stock rule record. Primary key.

attributes.name: campaign_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to marketing.campaign. Business justification: Promotional inventory planning: e-commerce operations teams create or override safety stock rules ahead of specific campaigns (e.g., Prime Day, Black Friday). Linking safety_stock_rule to campaign ena

attributes.name: cost_center_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Safety‑stock calculations allocate holding cost to a cost center, needed for cost‑of‑goods‑sold and inventory valuation reports.

attributes.name: seller_profile_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to seller.seller_profile. Business justification: Seller scorecards track inventory_health_score driven by safety stock compliance. Marketplace operations set seller-specific safety stock rules based on seller tier, SLA commitments, and demand patter

attributes.name: sku_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to product.sku. Business justification: Safety stock rules are defined per SKU for replenishment planning and reorder-point reporting. A domain expert expects a direct sku_id FK on safety_stock_rule to drive SKU-level replenishment decision

attributes.name: sla_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to fulfillment.fulfillment_sla. Business justification: In e-commerce replenishment planning, safety stock targets are calibrated per SLA tier — same-day SLA demands higher safety stock than standard. Planners explicitly align safety_stock_rule parameters

attributes.name: stock_position_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to inventory.stock_position. Business justification: A safety stock rule defines replenishment parameters for a specific SKU at a specific warehouse node — precisely the entity modeled by stock_position. Linking safety_stock_rule to stock_position estab

attributes.name: supplier_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to procurement.supplier. Business justification: Safety stock rules are supplier-specific: lead time variability and reliability differ per supplier. supplier_reliability_score is a denormalized metric from procurement.supplier. Linking enables auto

attributes.name: warehouse_node_id
attributes.description: Identifier of the warehouse or distribution center node where this safety stock rule applies.

attributes.name: abc_classification
attributes.description: Inventory classification based on value and volume: A (high value, tight control), B (moderate), C (low value, loose control).

attributes.name: average_daily_demand
attributes.description: The mean daily sales rate or demand for this SKU at this warehouse node, used as baseline for safety stock calculation.

attributes.name: average_lead_time_days
attributes.description: The mean supplier or replenishment lead time in days for this SKU at this location.

attributes.name: calculation_frequency
attributes.description: How often the safety stock quantity is automatically recalculated to reflect changing demand patterns and supply conditions.

attributes.name: calculation_method
attributes.description: The methodology used to calculate safety stock quantity: statistical (based on demand variability), fixed (constant buffer), seasonal (adjusted for seasonality), dynamic (real-time optimization), min-max (reorder point logic), or service level (target fill rate).

attributes.name: created_by_user_code
attributes.description: Identifier of the user or system process that created this safety stock rule record.

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: The date and time when this safety stock rule record was first created in the system.

attributes.name: demand_forecast_horizon_days
attributes.description: The number of days into the future for which demand forecasts are considered in safety stock calculation.

attributes.name: demand_variability_coefficient
attributes.description: Statistical measure of demand volatility (standard deviation or coefficient of variation) used in safety stock calculation to account for demand uncertainty.

attributes.name: effective_end_date
attributes.description: The date on which this safety stock rule expires or is superseded by a new rule. Null indicates an open-ended rule.

attributes.name: effective_start_date
attributes.description: The date from which this safety stock rule becomes active and is applied in inventory calculations.

attributes.name: holding_cost_per_unit_per_day
attributes.business_glossary_term: Holding Cost Per Unit Per Day
attributes.description: The daily cost of carrying one unit of inventory, including warehousing, insurance, obsolescence, and capital cost.

attributes.name: is_promotional_product
attributes.description: Boolean flag indicating whether this SKU is frequently subject to promotional campaigns that create demand spikes.

attributes.name: is_seasonal_product
attributes.description: Boolean flag indicating whether this SKU exhibits seasonal demand patterns requiring adjusted safety stock strategies.

attributes.name: last_calculation_date
attributes.description: The most recent date on which the safety stock quantity was recalculated based on updated demand and lead time data.

attributes.name: last_modified_by_user_code
attributes.description: Identifier of the user or system process that most recently updated this safety stock rule record.

attributes.name: last_modified_timestamp
attributes.description: The date and time when this safety stock rule record was most recently updated.

attributes.name: lead_time_variability_days
attributes.description: The standard deviation or variance in supplier lead time measured in days, capturing supply uncertainty for safety stock calculation.

attributes.name: maximum_stock_level
attributes.description: The upper inventory threshold for this SKU at this location, used in min-max inventory control systems.

attributes.name: minimum_order_quantity
attributes.description: The minimum quantity that must be ordered from the supplier, which may influence safety stock levels and reorder point calculations.

attributes.name: next_review_date
attributes.description: The scheduled date for the next review and potential recalculation of this safety stock rule.

attributes.name: notes
attributes.description: Additional free-text comments or annotations about this safety stock rule for operational reference and knowledge transfer.

attributes.name: override_reason
attributes.description: Free-text explanation when safety stock levels are manually overridden from system-calculated values, documenting business justification.

attributes.name: priority_classification
attributes.description: Business priority level assigned to this SKU for safety stock management, influencing service level targets and buffer sizing.

attributes.name: reorder_point
attributes.description: The inventory level at which a replenishment order should be triggered, calculated as lead time demand plus safety stock.

attributes.name: review_period_days
attributes.description: The frequency in days at which inventory levels are reviewed and replenishment decisions are made for this SKU.

attributes.name: rule_name
attributes.description: Human-readable name or label for this safety stock rule configuration.

attributes.name: rule_status
attributes.description: Current lifecycle status of the safety stock rule indicating whether it is actively applied in inventory planning.

attributes.name: safety_stock_days_of_cover
attributes.description: The number of days of average demand that the safety stock quantity represents, providing a time-based buffer metric.

attributes.name: safety_stock_quantity
attributes.description: The calculated safety stock quantity in units that should be maintained as buffer inventory to protect against stockouts.
attributes.references: ISO 22400 - Key Performance Indicators for Manufacturing

attributes.name: seasonality_factor
attributes.description: Multiplier applied to safety stock during seasonal demand periods to adjust buffer levels for predictable demand fluctuations.

attributes.name: service_level_target_percentage
attributes.description: The target in-stock probability or fill rate percentage (e.g., 95.00, 99.00) that this safety stock rule is designed to achieve.

attributes.name: stockout_cost_per_unit
attributes.description: The estimated business cost incurred per unit when a stockout occurs, including lost sales, expedited shipping, and customer dissatisfaction.

attributes.name: unit_of_measure
attributes.description: The unit in which safety stock quantity is expressed (each, case, pallet, weight, volume). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: each|case|pallet|kg|lb|liter|gallon — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

attributes.name: z_score
attributes.description: The standard normal distribution z-score corresponding to the service level target, used in statistical safety stock formulas.

products.name: warehouse_node
products.description: Master record for a physical or virtual warehouse node in the fulfillment network, including distribution centers, fulfillment centers, dark stores, 3PL facilities, and virtual drop-ship nodes. Captures node code, node type, address, geographic coordinates, capacity (sqft and pallet positions), operating hours, supported fulfillment types (FBA-style, BOPIS, DTC), and active status. SSOT for warehouse network topology.

attributes.name: warehouse_node_id
attributes.description: Unique surrogate identifier for a warehouse node record in the fulfillment network. Primary key for the warehouse_node master data product.

attributes.name: carrier_contract_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to logistics.carrier_contract. Business justification: Carrier contract management: e-commerce fulfillment centers operate under specific carrier contracts governing outbound rates and SLAs. Network operations teams must know which carrier contract applie

attributes.name: cost_center_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Each warehouse node is a cost center for operational expense tracking (labor, utilities, depreciation). Finance reports warehouse P&L by cost center. gl_cost_center_code is a denormalized reference; p

attributes.name: legal_entity_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.legal_entity. Business justification: Warehouses are owned or operated by specific legal entities — critical for multi-entity e-commerce operators, 3PL arrangements, and cross-border fulfillment. Required for tax jurisdiction assignment,

attributes.name: address_line1
attributes.description: Primary street address line of the warehouse node facility. Used for carrier pickup scheduling, regulatory filings, and geographic routing in the TMS.
attributes.references: GDPR Article 4; CCPA; Transportation Management System (TMS) - Facility Address

attributes.name: address_line2
attributes.description: Secondary address line for the warehouse node (suite, dock number, building identifier). Supplements address_line1 for precise carrier and logistics routing.
attributes.references: GDPR Article 4; Transportation Management System (TMS) - Facility Address

attributes.name: capacity_sqft
attributes.description: Total usable floor area of the warehouse node in square feet. Principal capacity measurement used for space utilization reporting, lease management, and network capacity planning.
attributes.references: Warehouse Management System (WMS) - Facility Capacity; ISO 9001 - Facility Management

attributes.name: carrier_codes
attributes.description: Comma-separated list of carrier codes (e.g., 'UPS,FEDEX,USPS,DHL') representing the shipping carriers that service this warehouse node. Used by the TMS for carrier selection and rate shopping during order fulfillment.
attributes.references: Transportation Management System (TMS) - Carrier Assignment; SLA Management

attributes.name: city
attributes.description: City in which the warehouse node is physically located. Used for geographic clustering, last-mile delivery zone assignment, and regulatory jurisdiction determination.

attributes.name: commissioned_date
attributes.description: Date on which the warehouse node was officially commissioned and became operational in the fulfillment network. Used for asset lifecycle tracking, depreciation calculations, and network history reporting.
attributes.references: ERP SAP S/4HANA - Fixed Asset Management; Warehouse Management System (WMS) - Facility Lifecycle

attributes.name: country_code
attributes.description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 three-letter country code for the country in which the warehouse node is located. Drives cross-border trade compliance, customs documentation, and WTO regulatory requirements.
attributes.references: ISO 3166-1 Alpha-3; WTO International Trade Regulations; EU Electronic Commerce Directive

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when the warehouse node master record was first created in the system. Used for data lineage, audit trail, and record lifecycle management per ISO 27001 information security standards.
attributes.references: ISO 27001 - Information Security Management; GDPR Article 5 - Data Integrity

attributes.name: decommissioned_date
attributes.description: Date on which the warehouse node was decommissioned and removed from the active fulfillment network. Null for active nodes. Used for network topology history and asset lifecycle reporting.
attributes.references: ERP SAP S/4HANA - Fixed Asset Management; Warehouse Management System (WMS) - Facility Lifecycle

attributes.name: dock_doors_inbound
attributes.description: Number of inbound receiving dock doors at the warehouse node. Determines inbound throughput capacity for purchase order receiving, supplier deliveries, and FTL/LTL carrier appointments.
attributes.references: Warehouse Management System (WMS) - Dock Management; Transportation Management System (TMS)

attributes.name: dock_doors_outbound
attributes.description: Number of outbound shipping dock doors at the warehouse node. Determines outbound throughput capacity for order fulfillment, carrier pickups, and cross-dock operations.
attributes.references: Warehouse Management System (WMS) - Dock Management; Transportation Management System (TMS)

attributes.name: fulfillment_model
attributes.description: Primary fulfillment model supported by this node. Determines order routing eligibility and SLA commitments. FBA-style nodes handle seller-stocked inventory; BOPIS nodes support Buy Online Pick Up In Store; DTC nodes serve direct-to-consumer shipments. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: fba_style|bopis|dtc|b2b|omnichannel|cross_dock — promote to reference product]
attributes.references: Order Management System (OMS) - Fulfillment Routing Rules; WMS Facility Configuration

attributes.name: gln
attributes.description: GS1 Global Location Number (GLN) — a 13-digit internationally standardized identifier for this warehouse node. Used in EDI transactions, supply chain partner communications, and global trade compliance.
attributes.references: GS1 GLN Standard; WTO International Trade Regulations; EDI Standards

attributes.name: is_24hr_operation
attributes.description: Indicates whether the warehouse node operates on a 24-hour, continuous basis. When true, overrides operating_hours_start and operating_hours_end for SLA and routing calculations.
attributes.references: Warehouse Management System (WMS) - Operating Hours Configuration

attributes.name: is_3pl
attributes.description: Indicates whether this warehouse node is operated by a third-party logistics (3PL) provider rather than owned/operated by the business. Drives cost allocation, SLA contract management, and operational reporting.
attributes.references: 3PL Industry Standards; Enterprise Resource Planning (ERP) SAP S/4HANA - Procurement

attributes.name: is_cold_storage
attributes.description: Indicates whether the warehouse node has temperature-controlled cold storage capability. Used to route perishable or temperature-sensitive SKUs to eligible nodes during inventory allocation.
attributes.references: Warehouse Management System (WMS) - Temperature Zone Management; ISO 22000 - Food Safety

attributes.name: is_hazmat_certified
attributes.description: Indicates whether the warehouse node holds the required certifications to store and handle hazardous materials (hazmat). Drives SKU-level inventory routing and CPSC compliance checks.
attributes.references: Consumer Product Safety Commission (CPSC) - Hazmat Storage Standards; NIST Cybersecurity Framework

attributes.name: is_returns_enabled
attributes.description: Indicates whether this warehouse node is authorized to receive and process customer returns and RMA (Return Merchandise Authorization) shipments. Controls return routing logic in the OMS.
attributes.references: Order Management System (OMS) - Returns Routing; Warehouse Management System (WMS) - RMA Processing

attributes.name: is_virtual
attributes.description: Indicates whether this is a virtual warehouse node (e.g., a drop-ship node or virtual inventory pool) with no physical facility. Virtual nodes participate in inventory allocation and order routing but have no physical location attributes.
attributes.references: Warehouse Management System (WMS) - Virtual Node Configuration; OMS - Drop-Ship Routing

attributes.name: latitude
attributes.description: Geographic latitude coordinate of the warehouse node in decimal degrees (WGS 84). Used by the TMS for route optimization, delivery radius calculations, and geospatial analytics.
attributes.references: ISO 6709 - Geographic Coordinates; Transportation Management System (TMS) - Route Optimization

attributes.name: lease_expiry_date
attributes.description: Date on which the facility lease for this warehouse node expires. Used for network planning, lease renewal management, and long-term capacity forecasting. Applicable to leased facilities only.
attributes.references: ERP SAP S/4HANA - Fixed Asset and Lease Management; SOX - Financial Reporting

attributes.name: longitude
attributes.description: Geographic longitude coordinate of the warehouse node in decimal degrees (WGS 84). Used by the TMS for route optimization, delivery radius calculations, and geospatial analytics.
attributes.references: ISO 6709 - Geographic Coordinates; Transportation Management System (TMS) - Route Optimization

attributes.name: max_weight_capacity_kg
attributes.business_glossary_term: Warehouse Node Maximum Weight Capacity Kilograms
attributes.description: Maximum total weight capacity of the warehouse node in kilograms. Used for structural load compliance, inventory planning, and safety management per facility engineering specifications.
attributes.references: Warehouse Management System (WMS) - Capacity Planning; ISO 45001 - Occupational Health and Safety

attributes.name: network_tier
attributes.description: Hierarchical tier classification of the warehouse node within the fulfillment network topology. Hub nodes serve as primary distribution points; spoke nodes serve last-mile delivery zones. Drives inventory positioning and replenishment flow.
attributes.references: Transportation Management System (TMS) - Network Topology; WMS - Replenishment Planning

attributes.name: node_code
attributes.description: Externally-known, human-readable alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the warehouse node across all operational systems (WMS, TMS, OMS). Used as the business key in EDI, carrier integrations, and fulfillment routing logic.
attributes.references: Warehouse Management System (WMS) - Facility Master; GS1 GLN Standard

attributes.name: node_name
attributes.description: Full descriptive name of the warehouse node as used in operational communications, reporting dashboards, and carrier documentation (e.g., 'Dallas Fulfillment Center - North').

attributes.name: node_status
attributes.description: Current operational lifecycle status of the warehouse node. Controls whether the node is eligible to receive inventory allocations, fulfill orders, or participate in replenishment planning.
attributes.references: Warehouse Management System (WMS) - Facility Lifecycle Management

attributes.name: node_type
attributes.description: Classification of the warehouse node by its operational role in the fulfillment network. Drives routing logic, SLA assignment, and capacity planning. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: fulfillment_center|distribution_center|dark_store|3pl_facility|drop_ship_node|returns_center — promote to reference product]
attributes.references: Warehouse Management System (WMS) - Facility Type Classification; 3PL Industry Standards

attributes.name: operating_days
attributes.description: Comma-separated list of operating days of the week for the warehouse node (e.g., 'MON,TUE,WED,THU,FRI'). Used for SLA cutoff scheduling, carrier appointment booking, and fulfillment routing logic.

attributes.name: operating_hours_end
attributes.description: Standard daily operating end time for the warehouse node in HH:MM (24-hour) format. Used to determine carrier pickup cutoff windows, SLA calculation, and order routing eligibility.
attributes.references: Warehouse Management System (WMS) - Operating Hours; SLA Management

attributes.name: operating_hours_start
attributes.description: Standard daily operating start time for the warehouse node in HH:MM (24-hour) format. Used to determine carrier pickup cutoff windows, SLA calculation, and order routing eligibility.
attributes.references: Warehouse Management System (WMS) - Operating Hours; SLA Management

attributes.name: pallet_positions
attributes.description: Total number of standard pallet positions available in the warehouse node. Used for inventory capacity planning, MOQ compliance, and replenishment scheduling in the WMS.
attributes.references: Warehouse Management System (WMS) - Capacity Planning; GS1 Pallet Standards

attributes.name: postal_code
attributes.description: Postal or ZIP code of the warehouse node facility. Used for carrier zone mapping, last-mile delivery cost calculation, and geographic analytics.
attributes.references: GDPR Article 4; Universal Postal Union (UPU) Standards

attributes.name: region_code
attributes.description: Internal geographic region code grouping warehouse nodes for network planning, carrier contract management, and regional fulfillment analytics (e.g., 'US-WEST', 'EU-CENTRAL').
attributes.references: Transportation Management System (TMS) - Network Region Configuration; ERP SAP S/4HANA - Organizational Structure

attributes.name: state_province
attributes.description: State or province code where the warehouse node is located. Used for tax nexus determination, labor law compliance, and regional fulfillment routing. Follows ISO 3166-2 subdivision codes.
attributes.references: ISO 3166-2; SOX - Tax Nexus Reporting; CCPA

attributes.name: temperature_max_c
attributes.description: Maximum maintained temperature in degrees Celsius for the warehouse node's temperature-controlled storage areas. Applicable only when is_cold_storage is true. Used for SKU eligibility routing and compliance audits.
attributes.references: Warehouse Management System (WMS) - Temperature Zone Configuration; ISO 22000

attributes.name: temperature_min_c
attributes.description: Minimum maintained temperature in degrees Celsius for the warehouse node's temperature-controlled storage areas. Applicable only when is_cold_storage is true. Used for SKU eligibility routing and compliance audits.
attributes.references: Warehouse Management System (WMS) - Temperature Zone Configuration; ISO 22000

attributes.name: timezone
attributes.description: IANA timezone identifier for the warehouse node location (e.g., 'America/Chicago'). Used to correctly interpret operating hours, SLA cutoff times, and carrier pickup windows across distributed fulfillment networks.
attributes.references: IANA Time Zone Database; WMS - Operating Hours Configuration

attributes.name: tms_location_code
attributes.description: Location code assigned by the Transportation Management System (TMS) for this warehouse node. Used for carrier pickup scheduling, route optimization, and shipment origin identification.

attributes.name: updated_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp of the most recent update to the warehouse node master record. Used for change detection, data synchronization with WMS/TMS, and audit trail compliance.
attributes.references: ISO 27001 - Information Security Management; GDPR Article 5 - Data Integrity

attributes.name: wms_facility_reference
attributes.description: Native facility identifier assigned by the Warehouse Management System (WMS) for this warehouse node. Used for system integration, EDI message routing, and reconciliation between the WMS and the lakehouse SSOT.
attributes.references: Warehouse Management System (WMS) - Facility Master; EDI Standards

domains.name: payment
domains.description: Authoritative source for all monetary transactions on the platform. Owns payment gateway authorization, settlement, refund processing, chargebacks, fraud detection, PCI DSS-compliant card data tokenization, multi-currency transaction records, payment method management, and payment reconciliation. Tracks transaction success rates and processing fees.
domains.references: Payment Gateway and Processing Platform, PCI DSS, ISO 20022, SOX

products.name: payment_transaction
products.description: Core authoritative record of every monetary transaction processed on the platform, including the immutable event log of all state transitions (authorized, captured, settled, voided, failed) and gateway webhook callbacks. Captures payment amount, currency, exchange rate applied, processing status, gateway response codes, authorization codes, timestamps, retry history, and event-driven lifecycle audit trail. Each transaction references a payment method, may have one or more authorizations, and flows through to settlement. Supports PCI DSS audit requirements and PSD2 Strong Customer Authentication (SCA) compliance evidence. SSOT for all payment transaction records, their lifecycle events, and state transition history.

attributes.name: payment_transaction_id
attributes.description: Unique identifier for the payment transaction.

attributes.name: account_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to customer.account. Business justification: Required for account‑level financial statements and reconciliation of each payment transaction to the customer's account.

attributes.name: accounts_receivable_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.accounts_receivable. Business justification: Cash application process matches payment transactions to AR records to clear open receivables. Finance AR aging accuracy, cash application reporting, and DSO calculations require linking each payment

attributes.name: campaign_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to marketing.campaign. Business justification: Required for Marketing ROI report linking each transaction to the driving campaign for revenue attribution.

attributes.name: catalog_item_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to product.catalog_item. Business justification: Tax and regulatory reporting requires mapping each transaction to the catalog item to apply correct jurisdiction‑specific tax codes.

attributes.name: channel_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to marketing.channel. Business justification: Channel-level revenue attribution and ROAS reporting: e-commerce teams report payment revenue by marketing channel (email, paid search, social). payment_transaction already has campaign_id but not cha

attributes.name: cost_center_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Required for allocating processing fees to the appropriate cost center in monthly expense reporting.

attributes.name: gateway_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to payment.gateway. Business justification: Every payment transaction is processed through a specific payment gateway. payment_transaction currently captures gateway_response_code and gateway_response_message as string fields but has no FK to t

attributes.name: general_ledger_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.general_ledger. Business justification: Supports direct posting of each payment transaction to a GL account for real‑time financial reconciliation.

attributes.name: header_id
attributes.description: Identifier of the order associated with this payment.

attributes.name: marketplace_promotion_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to marketplace.marketplace_promotion. Business justification: Payment transactions are directly discounted by marketplace promotions at checkout. Finance teams need this link for promotion ROI analysis, promotional discount liability accounting, and reconciling

attributes.name: merchant_account_id
attributes.description: Identifier of the merchant receiving the payment.

attributes.name: profit_center_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Enables attribution of net revenue from each transaction to the correct profit center for performance dashboards.

attributes.name: reconciliation_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to payment.reconciliation. Business justification: Transactions are part of a reconciliation run; linking via reconciliation_id enables auditability and variance analysis.

attributes.name: seller_profile_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to seller.seller_profile. Business justification: REQUIRED: Seller‑level revenue and commission reporting needs each transaction linked to the seller who fulfilled the order.

attributes.name: settlement_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to payment.settlement. Business justification: Each payment transaction is settled in a settlement batch; adding settlement_id links transaction to its settlement for traceability and reporting.

attributes.name: token_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to payment.token. Business justification: Payment transactions processed with tokenized card data reference a specific PCI DSS token in the token registry. payment_transaction currently has card_last4, card_brand, card_expiry_month, card_expi

attributes.name: tokenized_payment_method_id
attributes.description: PCI‑DSS compliant token representing the stored payment method.

attributes.name: wallet_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to payment.wallet. Business justification: Payment transactions can be funded from a customer digital wallet. payment_transaction currently has no FK to the wallet table, despite wallet being a first-class payment instrument in the domain. Add

attributes.name: amount_fee
attributes.description: Processing fee charged by the payment gateway.

attributes.name: amount_gross
attributes.description: Total amount authorized before fees or taxes.

attributes.name: amount_net
attributes.description: Final amount settled to the merchant after fees and taxes.

attributes.name: amount_tax
attributes.description: Tax component applied to the transaction.

attributes.name: authorization_code
attributes.description: Code returned by the issuing bank authorizing the transaction.

attributes.name: card_brand
attributes.description: Brand of the payment card when card‑based method is used.

attributes.name: card_expiry_month
attributes.description: Expiration month of the payment card.

attributes.name: card_expiry_year
attributes.description: Expiration year of the payment card.

attributes.name: card_last4
attributes.description: Last four digits of the card number for display and reconciliation.

attributes.name: chargeback_flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether the transaction resulted in a chargeback.

attributes.name: compliance_flags
attributes.description: Comma‑separated list of compliance regimes applicable to the transaction.

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when the transaction record was first created in the system.

attributes.name: currency_code
attributes.description: Three‑letter ISO 4217 currency code of the transaction.

attributes.name: device_fingerprint
attributes.description: Identifier of the device used to capture the payment.

attributes.name: exchange_rate
attributes.description: Rate used to convert from original currency to settlement currency, if applicable.

attributes.name: fraud_score
attributes.description: Numeric risk score assigned by fraud detection engine (0‑100).

attributes.name: gateway_response_code
attributes.description: Standardized response code from the payment gateway.

attributes.name: gateway_response_message
attributes.description: Human‑readable message accompanying the gateway response code.

attributes.name: identifier
attributes.description: External identifier assigned by the payment gateway for this transaction.

attributes.name: ip_address
attributes.description: IP address from which the transaction was initiated.

attributes.name: is_recurring
attributes.description: True if the transaction is part of a recurring payment schedule.

attributes.name: merchant_category_code
attributes.description: Four‑digit code classifying the merchant's industry.

attributes.name: payment_channel
attributes.description: Digital or physical channel through which the payment was captured.

attributes.name: payment_method
attributes.description: Instrument used to fund the transaction.

attributes.name: payment_transaction_description
attributes.description: Free‑form description or memo for the transaction.

attributes.name: payment_transaction_status
attributes.description: Current lifecycle state of the transaction.

attributes.name: recurring_cycle
attributes.description: Frequency of the recurring payment.

attributes.name: recurring_end_date
attributes.description: Date when the recurring payment schedule terminates.

attributes.name: refund_amount
attributes.description: Amount refunded to the customer, if any.

attributes.name: refund_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when the refund was processed.

attributes.name: risk_assessment
attributes.description: Overall risk classification derived from fraud score and other signals.

attributes.name: settlement_date
attributes.description: Date on which funds were settled to the merchant account.

attributes.name: settlement_status
attributes.description: Current status of the settlement process.

attributes.name: transaction_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when the transaction event occurred.

attributes.name: updated_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp of the most recent update to the transaction record.

products.name: method
products.description: Master record of all payment instruments registered by customers on the platform, including credit/debit cards (PCI DSS-tokenized), digital wallets (Apple Pay, Google Pay, PayPal), bank accounts (ACH/SEPA), buy-now-pay-later instruments, gift cards, and marketplace credits. Stores tokenized card data reference, billing address, expiry, card network (Visa/MC/Amex/Discover), issuing bank, method lifecycle status (active/expired/suspended/deleted), customer preference ranking, and SCA enrollment status. SSOT for payment instrument definitions referenced by transactions, authorizations, and tokens.

attributes.name: method_id
attributes.description: Primary key for method

attributes.name: billing_customer_address_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to customer.customer_address. Business justification: Address verification and 3NF normalization: payment method billing address is denormalized across five columns. Linking to customer_address enables AVS (Address Verification Service) checks, address c

attributes.name: customer_profile_id
attributes.description: Identifier of the customer who owns this payment method.

attributes.name: card_network
attributes.description: Card scheme or network for card‑based methods.

attributes.name: cardholder_email
attributes.description: Email address associated with the cardholder.

attributes.name: cardholder_name
attributes.description: Legal name of the cardholder as it appears on the card.

attributes.name: cardholder_phone
attributes.description: Phone number associated with the cardholder.

attributes.name: compliance_check_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp of the most recent compliance verification.

attributes.name: compliance_status
attributes.description: Current compliance posture of the payment method with regulatory standards.

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when the payment method record was first created.

attributes.name: currency_code
attributes.description: Three‑letter ISO currency code for the payment method.

attributes.name: daily_limit_amount
attributes.description: Maximum cumulative amount allowed per day.

attributes.name: expiry_month
attributes.description: Expiration month of the card (1‑12).

attributes.name: expiry_year
attributes.description: Expiration year of the card (four‑digit).

attributes.name: fraud_score
attributes.description: Numeric risk score (0‑100) indicating fraud likelihood.

attributes.name: is_default
attributes.description: Indicates whether this method is the customer's default payment instrument.

attributes.name: is_international_allowed
attributes.description: Indicates if the payment method supports cross‑border transactions.

attributes.name: is_one_time_allowed
attributes.description: Indicates if the payment method can be used for one‑off transactions.

attributes.name: is_recurring_allowed
attributes.description: Indicates if the payment method can be used for recurring billing.

attributes.name: issuing_bank
attributes.description: Name of the financial institution that issued the card or account.

attributes.name: last_four
attributes.description: Last four digits of the card number (masked).

attributes.name: last_used_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when the payment method was last used in a transaction.

attributes.name: method_description
attributes.description: Optional free‑text description of the payment method.

attributes.name: method_status
attributes.description: Current lifecycle status of the payment method.

attributes.name: method_type
attributes.description: Category of the payment instrument.

attributes.name: monthly_limit_amount
attributes.description: Maximum cumulative amount allowed per calendar month.

attributes.name: payment_method_name
attributes.description: User‑defined nickname for the payment method.

attributes.name: preference_rank
attributes.description: User‑defined ranking indicating preferred payment method order.

attributes.name: risk_level
attributes.description: Overall risk classification for the payment method.

attributes.name: sca_enrolled
attributes.description: Indicates whether the payment method is enrolled for SCA (e.g., 3‑DS).

attributes.name: token_reference
attributes.description: PCI‑DSS token that represents the underlying card or bank account.

attributes.name: tokenization_provider
attributes.description: Vendor or service that performed tokenization of the payment instrument.

attributes.name: transaction_limit_amount
attributes.description: Maximum amount allowed per individual transaction.

attributes.name: updated_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp of the most recent update to the payment method record.

attributes.name: verification_status
attributes.description: Result of the verification process for the payment method.

products.name: authorization
products.description: Captures the real-time authorization request and response lifecycle for each payment attempt against the issuing bank via the payment gateway. Records authorization code, AVS/CVV result, issuer response, decline reason, authorization amount, hold expiry, 3DS/SCA authentication result and challenge status, and gateway latency. An authorization may be voided, partially captured, retried, or expire without settlement — distinct from the final captured transaction. Supports PSD2 Strong Customer Authentication compliance tracking.

attributes.name: authorization_id
attributes.description: System-generated unique identifier for the payment authorization record.

attributes.name: customer_profile_id
attributes.description: Identifier of the customer who initiated the payment.

attributes.name: gateway_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to payment.gateway. Business justification: Every authorization request is routed through a specific payment gateway. authorization currently has no FK to the gateway master table, despite capturing gateway_response_code and gateway_response_me

attributes.name: marketplace_transaction_id

products.name: settlement
products.description: Records settlement batches and individual settlement entries submitted to acquiring banks and payment networks for fund transfer, including all associated processing fees at transaction level. Settlement attributes: settlement date, amount, currency, acquiring bank, merchant account, status (pending/settled/failed). Fee attributes: fee type (interchange, assessment, gateway fee, cross-border fee, currency conversion fee), fee amount, fee rate, fee currency. SSOT for settlement records and their associated processing fees. Supports daily reconciliation, financial close, cost-of-payment analysis, and fee reconciliation against gateway invoices.

Records refund amount, refund reason code, originating transaction reference, refund method (original payment method or store credit), processing status, and gateway refund confirmation. Supports RMA-driven refund workflows and customer service-initiated refunds.

products.name: fraud_case
products.description: Operational record of confirmed or suspected fraudulent payment activity requiring investigation and action, plus the authoritative registry of fraud detection rules and velocity checks that drive automated prevention. Case attributes: fraud type (CNP, account takeover, friendly fraud, synthetic identity), detection source, fraud amount, case status, investigator assignment, resolution outcome, recovery amount. Rule attributes: rule logic (e.g., max transaction amount per hour, BIN country mismatch, device fingerprint anomaly), rule priority, action (block/review/allow), activation status, performance thresholds. SSOT for both fraud investigation cases and the fraud prevention ruleset. Supports real-time fraud blocking, post-transaction investigation, rule tuning, and regulatory SAR (Suspicious Activity Report) filing.

attributes.name: updated_timestamp

Tracks merchant account number, acquiring bank, payment network agreements, processing currency, settlement currency, merchant category code (MCC), account status, and associated processing fees. Supports multi-merchant-account routing for marketplace and DTC channels.

products.name: reconciliation
products.description: Tracks the daily and periodic reconciliation process between internal transaction records and external bank/gateway statements, at both run-level summary and individual line-item detail. Run attributes: reconciliation period, matched/unmatched counts, variance totals, status (open/reconciled/exception). Line-item attributes: internal transaction reference, external statement reference, match status, variance amount, exception reason code. SSOT for all reconciliation runs and their constituent line items. Supports financial close, SOX-compliant payment reconciliation, and drill-down investigation of discrepancies.

attributes.name: reconciliation_id
attributes.description: System-generated unique identifier for each reconciliation run.

attributes.name: finance_bank_account_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.finance_bank_account. Business justification: Payment reconciliation matches gateway settlement records against bank account statements. The bank account is the primary reference for reconciliation; treasury and finance teams need this link to ru

attributes.name: general_ledger_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.general_ledger. Business justification: Payment reconciliation matches internal transaction records against GL account balances to identify variances. Finance close process and external audit require reconciliation records to reference the

attributes.name: legal_entity_id
attributes.description: Identifier of the legal entity (e.g., corporate subsidiary) for which the reconciliation is performed.

attributes.name: merchant_account_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to payment.merchant_account. Business justification: Reconciliation runs are performed for specific merchant accounts, comparing internal transaction records against external bank/gateway statements. reconciliation currently has no FK to merchant_accoun

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when the reconciliation record was first created in the system.

attributes.name: currency_code
attributes.description: Three-letter ISO currency code for the monetary values.

attributes.name: exception_reason_code

attributes.name: period_end_date

attributes.name: reconciliation_number
attributes.description: Business-visible identifier for the reconciliation run, often used in reporting and audit trails.

attributes.name: reconciliation_status
attributes.description: Current lifecycle status of the reconciliation run.

attributes.name: run_timestamp
attributes.description: Exact time when the reconciliation process was executed.

attributes.name: run_type

attributes.name: total_internal_amount

attributes.name: total_line_count
attributes.description: Total number of line items processed in the reconciliation run.

attributes.name: updated_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp of the most recent update to the reconciliation record.

attributes.name: variance_amount
attributes.description: Difference between total internal and external amounts (internal - external).

products.name: wallet
products.description: Master record of customer digital wallet accounts on the platform with complete transactional ledger of all balance movements. Wallet attributes: balance, currency, type (store credit, loyalty points, prepaid balance), funding sources, status. Transaction attributes: movement type (top-up, refund credit, promotional credit, loyalty redemption, purchase payment, withdrawal, expiry), amount, balance before/after, reference order or promotion, timestamp. SSOT for wallet accounts and their full transaction history. Provides complete audit trail for wallet balance integrity. Distinct from third-party digital wallets (Apple Pay, PayPal) which are captured as payment_method records.

attributes.name: customer_profile_id

products.name: token
products.description: PCI DSS-compliant tokenization registry mapping sensitive payment card data (PAN) to platform-generated tokens used in transaction processing. Stores token value, token type (network token vs. platform token), associated payment method reference, tokenization provider (Visa Token Service, Mastercard MDES, internal vault), token status, and expiry. Ensures raw card data never persists in application systems.

domains.name: seller
domains.description: SSOT for marketplace seller and third-party merchant entities. Owns seller onboarding, KYC/KYB verification, seller profiles, performance scorecards (BSR, DSR), commission structures, GMV attribution, seller tier management, seller agreements, seller ratings, and policy compliance monitoring via the Seller Management Portal.

products.name: seller_profile
products.description: Master record for every third-party merchant and marketplace seller entity on the platform. Stores legal business name, trading name, registration number, business type (individual/SMB/enterprise), incorporation country, tax identification numbers (VAT/EIN/GST), primary and secondary contact details (owner name, email, phone, role, notification preferences), primary category, current seller tier reference, onboarding status, account status (active/suspended/terminated), platform join date, and preferred communication language. This is the SSOT for seller identity and contact information — all other seller domain entities reference this record. Sourced from the Seller Management Portal.

attributes.name: seller_profile_id

attributes.name: cost_center_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Required for internal cost allocation reports that map each seller's expenses to a finance cost center for profitability analysis.

attributes.name: legal_entity_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.legal_entity. Business justification: Maps sellers to the legal entity under which they operate, needed for tax filings, regulatory reporting, and jurisdictional compliance.

attributes.name: merchant_account_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to payment.merchant_account. Business justification: REQUIRED: Mapping sellers to their merchant accounts enables accurate payout processing, settlement reconciliation, and regulatory reporting.

attributes.name: profit_center_id

attributes.name: seller_tier_id

products.name: verification
products.description: Tracks Know Your Customer / Know Your Business (KYC/KYB) identity verification workflows and serves as the authoritative document repository for all seller-submitted compliance materials. Captures verification type (identity/business/bank/tax), verification provider, submission date, workflow status (pending/in_review/approved/rejected/expired), rejection reason codes, risk classification, and compliance officer notes. Stores associated documents: document type (passport/business_license/utility_bill/tax_certificate/insurance_certificate/bank_statement/product_certification), document name, file reference (S3/blob URI), upload date, expiry date, issuing authority, document verification status (unverified/verified/expired/rejected), and whether document is seller-submitted or platform-issued. Each seller may have multiple verification records with multiple associated documents across lifecycle events. Supports regulatory obligations under FTC, GDPR, AML/CFT, CPSC, and EU DAC7 frameworks. Single point of response for regulatory document requests and KYC audit inquiries.

Tracks application submission date, onboarding stage (applied/document_review/kyc_check/category_approval/agreement_signed/live), assigned reviewer, category requested, business model (B2C/B2B/C2C), fulfillment model (FBA-style/self-fulfilled/3PL), estimated GMV, rejection reasons, escalation flags, and go-live date. Sourced from the Seller Management Portal onboarding module.

products.name: commission

Supports GMV-based tiered commission models and category-specific rate overrides. Referenced by payment settlement and GMV attribution processes.

products.name: scorecard

Used for automated tier promotion/demotion triggers, suspension threshold monitoring, and incentive program eligibility. Sourced from Seller Management Portal performance monitoring module.

products.name: gmv_settlement
products.description: Transactional record attributing Gross Merchandise Value (GMV) to individual sellers for each settlement period. Captures gross sales amount, returns and refunds deducted, net GMV, platform commission charged, promotional subsidy applied, tax collected on behalf of seller, currency, exchange rate, attribution period start/end dates, and GMV source channel (web/mobile/API). Supports financial reporting, seller tier evaluation, and marketplace revenue recognition. Links to order and payment domains for reconciliation.

products.description: Records policy breaches, enforcement actions, and account suspension/reinstatement events for sellers who violate marketplace policies, regulatory requirements, or platform terms of service. Captures violation type (counterfeit/prohibited_item/pricing_manipulation/review_fraud/IP_infringement/data_misuse), detection method (automated_rule/manual_review/buyer_report/regulatory_notice), severity level (warning/minor/major/critical), detection date, evidence references, assigned investigator, investigation status (open/under_review/upheld/dismissed/appealed), enforcement action taken (warning_issued/listing_removed/account_suspended/account_terminated/probation_applied), suspension effective date, expected reinstatement date, appeal eligibility and deadline, appeal outcome, and reinstatement conditions. Provides complete enforcement lifecycle from detection through resolution including suspension/reinstatement audit trail. Supports FTC, CPSC, and platform trust & safety compliance.
