attributes.description: Reference to the carrier entity associated with this contract.

attributes.name: cost_center_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Carrier contracts are budgeted to specific cost centers; this FK supports cost‑center level budgeting and variance tracking.

attributes.name: general_ledger_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.general_ledger. Business justification: Contract expenses must be posted to a GL account; linking contracts to general_ledger enables accurate financial statements.

attributes.name: legal_entity_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.legal_entity. Business justification: Carrier contracts are executed by specific legal entities — critical for intercompany logistics arrangements, regulatory compliance (CTPAT, IATA, WTO), and multi-entity e-commerce operations. Legal en

attributes.name: seller_profile_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to seller.seller_profile. Business justification: Contracts are negotiated per seller; the 'Carrier Contract Management' process requires linking each contract to the seller it serves.

attributes.name: amendment_date

attributes.description: Indicates whether the contract applies to domestic or international shipments.

attributes.description: ISO 4217 currency code for monetary amounts in the contract.

attributes.name: volume_unit

domains.name: pricing
domains.description: SSOT for all pricing rules, promotional mechanics, and discount structures. Owns base price lists, dynamic pricing algorithms, markdown schedules, coupon and voucher management, promotional campaign pricing, competitive price intelligence, and A/B testing for pricing experiments. Tracks ASP, price elasticity, and GMV optimization.
domains.references: Enterprise Resource Planning - SAP S/4HANA, FTC Act

products.name: price_list
products.description: Master record defining a named price list (e.g., MSRP, wholesale, member, regional) with effective date ranges, currency, applicable customer segments, and channel scope. Serves as the authoritative base price catalog from which all pricing rules derive. Owned by the Pricing domain as the SSOT for base price structures.

attributes.name: price_list_id
attributes.description: System-generated unique identifier for the price list record.

attributes.name: legal_entity_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.legal_entity. Business justification: Price lists with currency_code and region attributes must be scoped to a legal entity for intercompany transfer pricing, tax compliance, and financial consolidation in multi-entity e-commerce. Domain

attributes.name: marketplace_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to marketplace.marketplace. Business justification: Price lists are frequently scoped to a specific marketplace (e.g., a price list valid only on a given marketplace). Marketplace-scoped price list management, activation, and reporting require this dir

attributes.name: segment_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to customer.segment. Business justification: REQUIRED: Segment‑based pricing; marketing analytics join price list to customer segment to evaluate segment‑specific discount performance.

attributes.name: base_price
attributes.description: Core price value before any adjustments, expressed in the list currency.

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when the price list record was first created in the system.

attributes.name: currency_code
attributes.description: Three‑letter ISO 4217 currency code in which the prices are expressed.

attributes.description: True if additional discounts may be stacked on top of this price list.

attributes.description: Date on which the price list expires; null for open‑ended lists.

attributes.name: effective_start_date
attributes.description: Date on which the price list becomes active for transactions.

attributes.name: is_default
attributes.description: True if this list is the default for its scope when no other list matches.

attributes.description: Ceiling price for the list; used to enforce pricing caps.

attributes.name: max_quantity
attributes.description: Maximum quantity for which the price is valid; null for no upper bound.

attributes.name: min_price
attributes.description: Floor price for the list; used to enforce pricing floors.

attributes.description: Minimum quantity required for the price to apply.

attributes.name: notes
attributes.description: Additional free‑form remarks or operational comments.

attributes.name: price_calculation_method
attributes.description: Method used to derive the prices in this list.

attributes.name: price_elasticity
attributes.description: Historical elasticity metric used for dynamic pricing decisions.

attributes.name: price_list_code
attributes.description: Unique business code used to reference the price list in external systems and contracts.

attributes.description: Free‑form description providing context, purpose, and any special rules for the price list.

attributes.description: Human‑readable name of the price list (e.g., "US Retail MSRP").

attributes.name: price_list_status

attributes.description: Number of decimal places used for price values in this list.

attributes.name: price_rounding_rule
attributes.description: Rounding behavior applied to calculated prices.

attributes.name: price_source
attributes.description: Origin of the price data (e.g., manually entered, system‑generated, partner feed).

attributes.name: price_tier
attributes.description: Logical tier or band within the price list (e.g., "Gold", "Silver").

attributes.name: price_type
attributes.description: Category of the price list indicating its purpose (e.g., base list, discount list, promotional list).

attributes.name: promotional_flag
attributes.description: True if the price list is part of a promotional campaign.

attributes.name: region
attributes.description: Geographic region to which the price list applies.

attributes.name: tax_included_flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether taxes are already included in the listed prices.

attributes.name: updated_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp of the most recent update to the price list record.

attributes.name: version
attributes.description: Incremental version number for change tracking.

products.name: price_list_item
products.description: Line-level entry within a price list associating a specific SKU or product variant to its base unit price, minimum order quantity (MOQ), unit of measure, and pricing structure type (flat, tiered, volume-based, bundle). For tiered/volume pricing, captures quantity thresholds and per-tier unit prices. For bundle pricing, captures constituent SKUs and bundle discount. Serves as the authoritative base price per SKU per price list supporting all pricing structure variants.
products.reference: SKU Price Entry / Base Price Line

attributes.name: price_list_item_id
attributes.description: Unique surrogate key for each price list item record.

attributes.name: bundle_id

attributes.name: cost_center_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Price list items are attributed to cost centers for P&L reporting and budget variance analysis. In e-commerce, each pricing tier must be owned by a business unit (cost center) to track margin impact.

attributes.name: price_list_id
attributes.description: Identifier of the parent price list to which this item belongs.

attributes.name: primary_price_product_variant_sku_id
attributes.description: Internal identifier for the specific product variant when SKU alone is insufficient.

attributes.name: seller_profile_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to seller.seller_profile. Business justification: REQUIRED: Enables seller‑specific SKU price overrides; pricing engine looks up seller_profile_id to apply custom base prices per seller.

attributes.name: supplier_price_list_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to procurement.supplier_price_list. Business justification: Sell-side price list items are derived from supplier cost prices. Linking price_list_item to supplier_price_list enables real-time margin calculation, cost-plus pricing enforcement, and margin reporti

attributes.description: Foreign key linking to inventory.warehouse_node. Business justification: Location-based price list items require warehouse node to apply inventory‑aware pricing per fulfillment center.

products.name: dynamic_price_rule
products.description: Defines algorithmic or rules-based dynamic pricing logic applied to SKUs, categories, or seller listings across platform-owned and marketplace channels. Includes trigger conditions (demand signals, inventory levels, competitor price thresholds, MAP violations, seller compliance triggers), adjustment type (percentage, fixed, floor/ceiling), rule scope (platform-wide, seller-specific, category-level, brand-level), priority rank, activation schedule, and enforcement actions. For marketplace-scoped rules: MAP enforcement thresholds, maximum price caps, commission-adjusted floor prices, seller-specific pricing agreements, and brand protection policies. Powers real-time price optimization, surge/markdown automation, MAP enforcement, marketplace pricing integrity, and seller pricing governance.
products.reference: Dynamic Pricing Rule / Algorithmic Pricing Policy

Serves as the SSOT for all discount code lifecycle management across all code types.

attributes.description: True if the coupon may be redeemed only once across all customers.

attributes.name: is_stackable
attributes.description: Indicates whether the coupon can be combined with other promotions.

attributes.name: issuance_channel
attributes.description: System or process that generated the coupon (e.g., campaign engine, manual upload).

attributes.name: max_discount_amount
attributes.description: Upper cap on the monetary value that can be deducted by the coupon.

attributes.name: min_order_amount
attributes.description: Minimum order subtotal required for the coupon to be applicable.

attributes.description: Free‑form field for internal comments or special handling instructions.

attributes.name: usage_limit_per_customer
attributes.description: Maximum number of times a single customer may redeem this coupon.

products.name: coupon_redemption
products.description: Transactional record capturing each coupon or voucher redemption event, including the redeemed code, order reference, customer reference, discount amount applied, redemption timestamp, channel, and redemption outcome (success, failure, partial). Provides the audit trail for all discount code usage and supports fraud detection and budget tracking.

attributes.name: coupon_redemption_id
attributes.description: Unique surrogate key for each coupon redemption event.

attributes.name: coupon_id
attributes.description: Internal identifier of the coupon definition.

attributes.name: customer_profile_id
attributes.description: Identifier of the customer who redeemed the coupon.

attributes.name: header_id
attributes.description: Identifier of the order associated with the coupon redemption.

attributes.name: marketplace_promotion_id
attributes.description: Identifier of the promotional campaign linked to the coupon.

attributes.name: sku_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to product.sku. Business justification: Coupon redemption tracking requires knowing the specific SKU discounted for fraud detection, promotion analytics, and financial reconciliation. applied_to_sku is a denormalized text reference to produ

attributes.name: applied_quantity
attributes.description: Number of units of the SKU that the coupon discount covered.

attributes.name: channel
attributes.description: Channel through which the coupon was redeemed (e.g., web, mobile app, in‑store, call center).

attributes.name: coupon_redemption_status
attributes.description: Outcome of the redemption attempt: success, failure, or partial.

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when the redemption record was first inserted into the lakehouse.

attributes.name: currency_code
attributes.description: Three‑letter ISO 4217 currency code for the discount amount.

attributes.name: discount_amount
attributes.description: Monetary value of the discount applied by the coupon.

attributes.name: fraud_flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether the redemption was flagged for potential fraud.

attributes.name: notes
attributes.description: Free‑form text for any additional information or comments about the redemption.

attributes.name: redemption_code
attributes.description: The coupon or voucher code that was redeemed.

attributes.name: redemption_timestamp
attributes.description: Exact date and time when the coupon was redeemed.

attributes.name: redemption_type
attributes.description: Classification of how the coupon can be used: single‑use, multi‑use, or auto‑apply.

attributes.name: source_system
attributes.description: Name of the upstream operational system that generated the redemption record (e.g., OMS, PIM).

attributes.name: updated_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp of the most recent update to the redemption record.

products.name: price_override
products.description: Transactional record of a manual or system-initiated price override applied to a specific order line or SKU, capturing the original price, overridden price, override reason code, approving user, approval timestamp, and override scope (order-level, line-level, customer-specific). Supports exception pricing workflows and audit compliance.

attributes.name: price_override_id
attributes.description: Unique system-generated identifier for the price override record.

attributes.name: cost_center_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Price overrides with approval workflows and compliance_flag require cost center attribution for financial variance reporting. In e-commerce, approved overrides exceeding thresholds are charged to a co

attributes.name: customer_profile_id
attributes.description: Identifier of the customer for whom the override is scoped, if applicable.

attributes.name: general_ledger_id
attributes.business_glossary_term: Price Override Gl Account Id (Foreign Key)
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.general_ledger. Business justification: Price overrides affect revenue; finance requires a GL posting to reflect adjusted sales amounts and maintain audit trail of overrides.

attributes.name: header_id
attributes.description: Identifier of the order associated with the price override.

attributes.name: line_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to order.order_line. Business justification: SOX compliance and internal audit require price override audit trails at the line item level. CSR or system price overrides are applied to specific order lines; without this FK, override reports canno

attributes.name: marketplace_listing_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to marketplace.marketplace_listing. Business justification: Price overrides are applied to specific marketplace listings (e.g., manual price corrections on a listing). Marketplace compliance, buy-box audit trails, and override approval workflows require tracin

attributes.name: price_list_item_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to pricing.price_list_item. Business justification: price_override overrides a specific price list item; linking eliminates redundant sku column

attributes.name: sku_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to product.sku. Business justification: Price overrides (e.g., CSR goodwill adjustments, competitive match) are applied to specific SKUs. A direct sku_id FK enables SKU-level override audit trails and compliance reporting; price_list_item_i

attributes.name: applied_timestamp
attributes.description: Date and time when the price override took effect.

attributes.name: approval_status
attributes.description: Result of the approval workflow for the price override.

attributes.name: approval_timestamp
attributes.description: Date and time when the override was approved by the designated approver.

attributes.name: audit_note
attributes.description: Optional free‑text note added during audit actions.

attributes.name: compliance_flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether the override complies with pricing governance policies.

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: Date and time when the override record was initially created in the system.

attributes.name: currency_code
attributes.description: Three‑letter ISO currency code for the price values.

attributes.description: Date when the overridden price becomes effective.

attributes.name: effective_until
attributes.description: Date when the overridden price expires; null if indefinite.

attributes.description: Indicates whether the price override is currently active (true) or has been superseded/revoked (false).

attributes.description: The price of the SKU before the override was applied.

attributes.name: overridden_price
attributes.description: The new price after the override was applied.

attributes.name: override_type
attributes.description: Indicates whether the price override was applied manually, by an automated system, or via a pricing rule.

attributes.name: price_change_amount
attributes.description: Absolute difference between overridden price and original price.

attributes.name: price_change_percentage
attributes.description: Percentage change represented by the price override.

attributes.name: price_override_status
attributes.description: Current lifecycle status of the price override.

attributes.name: reason_code
attributes.description: Standardized code describing why the price override was applied.

attributes.name: reason_description
attributes.description: Free‑text description providing additional context for the override reason.

attributes.name: scope
attributes.description: Defines the granularity of the override – whether it applies to the whole order, a single line, a specific customer, or globally.

attributes.name: source_system
attributes.description: System of origin for the override record.

attributes.name: updated_timestamp
attributes.description: Date and time of the most recent modification to the override record.

products.name: price_history
products.description: Immutable append-only log of all price changes for each SKU across all price lists, capturing previous price, new price, effective timestamp, change source (dynamic rule, manual override, markdown, promotion, experiment adoption), triggering entity reference, and the user or system that initiated the change. Supports price elasticity computation, regulatory compliance (price display laws, consumer protection), competitive analysis, rollback capability, and historical price auditing for dispute resolution.
products.reference: Price Change Log / Price Audit Trail

attributes.name: price_history_id
attributes.description: System-generated unique identifier for each price change event.

attributes.name: general_ledger_id
attributes.business_glossary_term: Price History Gl Account Id (Foreign Key)
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.general_ledger. Business justification: Historical price changes are linked to GL for audit of revenue impact and compliance with financial reporting standards.

attributes.name: marketplace_listing_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to marketplace.marketplace_listing. Business justification: FTC and marketplace compliance require listing-level price change history (e.g., reference price substantiation). price_history has sku_id but not listing_id — multiple listings can share a SKU with d

attributes.name: marketplace_promotion_id
attributes.description: Identifier of the promotion or coupon that triggered the price change, if applicable.

attributes.name: price_list_id
attributes.description: Identifier of the price list (e.g., base, promotional, regional) that the price belongs to.

attributes.name: sku_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to product.sku. Business justification: Price History records must be tied to a SKU to support price audit, margin analysis, and regulatory reporting.

attributes.name: change_reason
attributes.description: Free‑text explanation or business justification for the price modification.

attributes.name: currency_code
attributes.description: Three‑letter ISO 4217 currency code of the price values.

attributes.name: effective_timestamp

attributes.name: initiated_by_system
attributes.description: Name of the automated system or service (e.g., pricing engine, rule engine) that generated the change.

attributes.name: is_manual_override
attributes.description: Indicates whether the price change was manually overridden (true) or system‑driven (false).

attributes.description: Price value after the change was applied.

attributes.name: price_before
attributes.description: Price value before the change was applied.

attributes.name: price_change_amount
attributes.description: Numeric difference between new and previous price (price_after - price_before).

attributes.name: price_change_type
attributes.description: Classification of the change mechanism that caused the price update.

attributes.name: record_audit_created
attributes.description: Timestamp when this price history record was first inserted into the lakehouse.

attributes.name: record_audit_updated
attributes.description: Timestamp of the most recent update to this record (e.g., correction of metadata).

products.name: price_zone
products.description: Geographic or customer-segment-based pricing zone definitions that determine which price list applies to a given buyer. Captures zone code, zone name, geographic boundaries, customer segment criteria, and effective date ranges.

domains.name: marketing
domains.description: Owns customer acquisition and retention campaign execution. Manages CRM-driven campaign management via Salesforce Marketing Cloud, customer segmentation, email marketing, personalization, attribution modeling, SEM/SEO performance, and A/B testing results. Tracks CAC, ROAS, CTR, CPM, CPC, RPM, and campaign ROI across DTC and B2B channels.
domains.references: Marketing Automation Platform - Salesforce Marketing Cloud, GDPR, CCPA, FTC Act

products.name: campaign
products.description: Master record for all marketing campaigns across owned (email, push, SMS), paid (SEM, display, social, shopping, video), and earned (referral, affiliate, organic social) channels. Captures campaign identity, type, channel, objective (acquisition, retention, upsell, brand awareness), budget allocation, flight dates, target audience segment, campaign owner, status lifecycle, UTM parameters, bidding strategy, daily/lifetime budget caps, target geography, platform-specific IDs, ad group hierarchy (as nested structure for paid media), ad format, and associated brand or product line. SSOT for all campaign definitions — performance, attribution, budget, and creative records reference this master. For paid media campaigns, includes ad group-level detail (keyword themes, audience targeting, bid amounts, group status) as child records within the campaign structure.

attributes.name: campaign_id
attributes.description: Unique identifier for the campaign (primary key).

attributes.name: brand_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to product.brand. Business justification: REQUIRED: Campaigns are created per brand; brand‑level ROI reports need a direct link to product.brand.brand_id.

attributes.name: bundle_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to product.bundle. Business justification: Bundle promotions are a core e-commerce marketing tactic. Campaign managers run dedicated campaigns for product bundles (e.g., 'Buy X+Y, save 20%'). Linking campaign to bundle enables bundle-level cam

attributes.name: carrier_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to logistics.carrier. Business justification: Tracks which carrier is featured in a shipping‑promotion campaign, supporting cost analysis and carrier‑specific promotional reporting.

attributes.name: carrier_service_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to logistics.carrier_service. Business justification: E-commerce campaigns frequently promote specific shipping services (e.g., 'free 2-day shipping this weekend'). Linking campaign to carrier_service enables shipping-promotion campaign management, cost

attributes.name: catalog_item_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to product.catalog_item. Business justification: Product launch and sponsored-product campaigns target specific catalog items. Merchandising and campaign management teams need to link a campaign to the exact catalog item being promoted for PDP boost

attributes.name: category_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to product.product_category. Business justification: REQUIRED: Category‑targeted campaigns need to reference product_category.product_category_id for category performance dashboards.

attributes.name: channel_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to marketing.channel. Business justification: Campaign uses a textual channel; replace with FK to channel for referential integrity and remove redundant column.

attributes.name: cost_center_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Required for Campaign Cost Allocation Report linking each campaign to the cost center that bears its expenses.

attributes.name: delivery_zone_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to logistics.delivery_zone. Business justification: E-commerce campaigns are scoped to delivery zones for geo-targeted promotions (e.g., 'same-day delivery available in your area'). Replacing the denormalized target_geography text field with a structur

attributes.name: fulfillment_center_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to fulfillment.center. Business justification: Geo-targeted campaigns (same-day delivery, local promotions) are operationally tied to specific fulfillment centers. Operations and marketing teams jointly plan campaign eligibility based on center ca

attributes.name: general_ledger_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.general_ledger. Business justification: Supports GL posting of campaign‑related expenses for statutory accounting and audit trails.

attributes.name: marketplace_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to marketplace.marketplace. Business justification: Marketing campaigns in multi-marketplace e-commerce are scoped to specific marketplaces (e.g., campaign runs only on marketplace A). Campaign budget allocation, performance reporting, and compliance r

attributes.name: profit_center_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Enables Profitability Dashboard by attributing campaign revenue and costs to the appropriate profit center.

attributes.name: seller_profile_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to seller.seller_profile. Business justification: Seller co-op and sponsored campaigns (e.g., Sponsored Brands, Promoted Listings) are tied to a specific seller. This FK enables seller-level campaign spend reporting, co-op budget reconciliation, and

attributes.name: sla_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to fulfillment.fulfillment_sla. Business justification: Campaigns that promise specific delivery windows (e.g., 'Order today, delivered tomorrow') must reference the fulfillment SLA backing that promise. This link enables campaign-SLA compliance validation

attributes.name: supplier_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to procurement.supplier. Business justification: SUPPLIER-SPONSORED CAMPAIGN: tracks which supplier funds each marketing campaign for compliance, ROI attribution, and budget approval.

products.name: campaign_performance
products.description: Operational record capturing each campaign deployment event and its resulting performance metrics. Combines execution metadata (send date/time, channel, audience size, delivery status, bounce count, batch ID, platform job reference) with engagement metrics (impressions, clicks, CTR, conversions, CVR, spend, revenue attributed, ROAS, CPC, CPM, RPM, CAC, AOV). One record per campaign activation per reporting period. Supports real-time campaign monitoring, ROI analysis, and budget optimization. Subsumes former campaign_execution concept.

attributes.description: Foreign key linking to marketplace.marketplace. Business justification: Campaign performance is reported and benchmarked per marketplace (ROAS, CTR, CVR vary by marketplace). Marketplace-level campaign performance dashboards and budget optimization decisions require this

products.name: audience_segment
products.description: Master record for reusable customer audience segments including membership composition. Captures segment definition (name, type, logic, refresh frequency, owner, status) and per-member association records (customer ID, membership dates, inclusion reason, membership status). Enables historical tracking of segment composition changes, CLTV-based cohort analysis, and real-time personalization decisioning. SSOT for marketing audience definitions and their evaluated membership.

attributes.name: budget_currency
attributes.description: Three‑letter ISO currency code for the budget.

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when the ad group record was initially created in the system.

attributes.name: delivery_status
attributes.description: Current delivery condition of the ad group as reported by the platform.

attributes.name: end_date
attributes.description: Date when the ad group stops serving (nullable for ongoing).

attributes.name: external_code
attributes.description: Identifier assigned by the advertising platform (e.g., Google Ads ID).

attributes.name: frequency_cap
attributes.description: Maximum number of impressions per user within the frequency window.

attributes.name: frequency_cap_window
attributes.description: Number of days over which the frequency cap applies.

attributes.name: is_experiment
attributes.description: Indicates whether the ad group is part of an A/B test or experiment.

attributes.name: label
attributes.description: User‑defined label or tag for grouping ad groups in UI.

attributes.name: optimization_goal
attributes.description: Primary performance metric the ad group is optimized for.

attributes.name: record_audit_created
attributes.description: Timestamp when the record was first ingested into the lakehouse.

attributes.name: record_audit_updated
attributes.description: Timestamp of the latest audit update to the record.

attributes.name: start_date
attributes.description: Date when the ad group becomes eligible to serve.

attributes.name: targeting_criteria
attributes.description: JSON‑encoded rules defining audience, geography, device, and other targeting parameters.

attributes.name: updated_by
attributes.description: User or system that performed the most recent update.

attributes.name: updated_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp of the most recent modification to the ad group record.

attributes.name: created_by
attributes.description: User or system that created the ad group record.

products.name: campaign_creative_assignment
products.description: This association product represents the Assignment (Role) between campaign and creative_asset. It captures the operational record of a specific creative asset being deployed within a specific campaign, including placement context, active date range, primary/variant designation, and A/B test role. Each record links one campaign to one creative_asset with attributes that exist only in the context of this assignment — a creative asset may be assigned to many campaigns, and a campaign may deploy many creative assets, each assignment carrying its own placement and variant metadata.. Existence Justification: In e-commerce marketing operations, creative assets are reusable brand assets managed in a DAM system and deliberately assigned to multiple campaigns (e.g., a hero banner image used across a summer sale campaign AND a retargeting campaign simultaneously). Conversely, a single campaign deploys multiple creative assets (e.g., A/B test variants, different ad formats for different placements). The business actively manages these assignments through a creative assignment workflow, tracking placement type, date ranges, and variant roles — this is not derivable from transactional data but is an operational record humans create and update.

attributes.name: campaign_creative_assignment_id
attributes.business_glossary_term: Campaign Creative Assignment - Campaign Creative Assignment Id
attributes.description: Primary key for the campaign_creative_assignment association

attributes.name: campaign_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to the marketing campaign that this creative asset is assigned to

attributes.name: creative_asset_id
attributes.business_glossary_term: Campaign Creative Assignment - Creative Asset Id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to the creative asset deployed within this campaign assignment

attributes.name: ab_test_variant
attributes.description: Identifies which A/B or multivariate test arm this creative asset represents within the campaign. The same asset may be the control in one campaign and a variant in another, so this designation belongs to the assignment record.

attributes.name: is_primary_creative
attributes.description: Indicates whether this creative asset is the designated hero or primary creative for the campaign. A campaign may have one primary creative and multiple supporting or variant creatives. Belongs to the assignment, not to the asset or campaign independently.

attributes.name: placement_type
attributes.description: The specific ad slot, channel placement, or content zone this creative asset occupies within the campaign. Belongs to the assignment because the same asset may serve different placement roles across different campaigns.

attributes.name: usage_end_date
attributes.description: The date on which this creative asset was retired or replaced within this campaign assignment. Enables historical tracking of which assets were active during which periods of a campaign.

attributes.name: usage_start_date
attributes.description: The date on which this creative asset became active for this specific campaign assignment. Distinct from the campaign flight start date — an asset may be introduced mid-campaign or rotated in during optimization.

domains.name: finance
domains.description: SSOT for enterprise financial records and reporting. Owns general ledger, accounts payable, accounts receivable, cost center management, budget tracking, GMV-to-revenue reconciliation, and regulatory financial reporting. Integrates with SAP S/4HANA for financial consolidation. Ensures SOX compliance for financial controls and audit trails. Tracks MRR, ARR, and revenue recognition.
domains.references: Enterprise Resource Planning - SAP S/4HANA, SOX, GAAP, IFRS

products.name: general_ledger
products.description: SSOT for the enterprise chart of accounts and GL account master data. Defines account codes, account types (asset, liability, equity, revenue, expense), account hierarchies, and natural account groupings. Serves as the structural backbone for all financial postings, trial balance generation, and financial statement preparation. Integrates with SAP S/4HANA GL for multi-company-code chart of accounts and supports SOX-compliant period-end close.

attributes.name: general_ledger_id
attributes.description: Primary key for general_ledger

attributes.name: legal_entity_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.legal_entity. Business justification: GL accounts belong to a specific legal entity's chart of accounts. general_ledger.company_code is a denormalized code; adding legal_entity_id provides a proper FK to the legal_entity master, enabling

attributes.name: account_balance_gc
attributes.business_glossary_term: General Ledger (GL) Account Balance in Group Currency
attributes.description: Current cumulative balance of the GL account in the group/consolidation currency. Used for multi-entity financial consolidation, group-level reporting, and GMV-to-revenue reconciliation across all company codes. Supports MRR and ARR tracking at the enterprise level.
attributes.references: IFRS IAS 21; US GAAP ASC 830; SOX Section 404

attributes.name: account_balance_lc
attributes.business_glossary_term: General Ledger (GL) Account Balance in Local Currency
attributes.description: Current cumulative balance of the GL account in the company code local currency. Represents the net of all debit and credit postings to this account for the current fiscal period. Used for trial balance generation, financial statement preparation, and period-end close reporting.
attributes.references: IFRS IAS 1; US GAAP ASC 205; SOX Section 404

attributes.name: account_category
attributes.description: Secondary classification grouping GL accounts into functional categories such as cash and equivalents, accounts receivable, inventory, fixed assets, accounts payable, accrued liabilities, deferred revenue, cost of goods sold, operating expenses, etc. Supports financial statement line-item grouping and management reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: cash_and_equivalents|accounts_receivable|inventory|fixed_assets|accounts_payable|accrued_liabilities|deferred_revenue|cost_of_goods_sold|operating_expenses|other — promote to reference product]
attributes.references: IFRS IAS 1; US GAAP ASC 210; SAP S/4HANA FI-GL

attributes.name: account_description
attributes.description: Detailed narrative description of the GL account's business purpose, the types of transactions it records, and any special posting rules or restrictions. Used for financial governance documentation, new employee training, and SOX control documentation.

attributes.name: account_effective_from
attributes.description: The date from which this GL account became active and available for financial postings. Used for account lifecycle management, historical reporting, and SOX audit trails. Postings with a document date before this date are not permitted.

attributes.name: account_effective_until
attributes.description: The date after which this GL account is no longer available for financial postings. Null for accounts with no planned end date. Used for account decommissioning, chart of accounts rationalization, and SOX-compliant account lifecycle management.

attributes.name: account_group
attributes.description: Organizational grouping of GL accounts within the chart of accounts that controls the number range and field selection for account master data maintenance. Corresponds to SAP S/4HANA account group (KTOKS). Examples include balance sheet accounts, P&L accounts, cost accounts.

attributes.name: account_long_name
attributes.description: Full descriptive name of the GL account providing complete context for financial reporting and audit purposes. Corresponds to the SAP S/4HANA GL account long text (TXT50). Used in detailed financial statements and regulatory filings.

attributes.name: account_name
attributes.description: Short descriptive name of the GL account as displayed in financial reports, trial balances, and the chart of accounts. Corresponds to the SAP S/4HANA GL account short text (TXT20).

attributes.name: account_number
attributes.description: The externally-known, human-readable GL account number as defined in the chart of accounts. Used in all financial postings, journal entries, and financial statement line items. Corresponds to the SAP S/4HANA GL account key (SAKNR).
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA FI-GL; IFRS IAS 1; SOX Section 404

attributes.name: account_status
attributes.description: Current lifecycle status of the GL account master record. Active accounts accept financial postings; blocked accounts are temporarily restricted from new postings; marked_for_deletion accounts are pending archival after period-end close. Corresponds to SAP S/4HANA account blocking indicators.

attributes.name: account_type
attributes.description: Fundamental financial classification of the GL account per the accounting equation. Determines how the account appears on financial statements: asset and liability/equity accounts appear on the balance sheet; revenue and expense accounts appear on the income statement. Corresponds to SAP S/4HANA account type (KTOKS).
attributes.references: IFRS IAS 1; US GAAP ASC 205; SAP S/4HANA FI-GL

attributes.name: alternative_account_number
attributes.description: Alternative or country-specific account number used for local statutory reporting requirements. Enables mapping between the operational chart of accounts and country-specific or group chart of accounts. Supports multi-GAAP reporting and local statutory compliance.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA FI-GL; IFRS IAS 1; Local GAAP

attributes.name: chart_of_accounts_code
attributes.description: Identifier for the chart of accounts grouping to which this GL account belongs. In SAP S/4HANA, a chart of accounts is a classified directory of all GL accounts used by one or more company codes. Enables multi-COA structures for operational and group reporting.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA FI-GL; IFRS IAS 1; US GAAP ASC 205

attributes.name: company_code
attributes.description: SAP S/4HANA company code representing the legal entity or organizational unit for which the GL account is defined. Supports multi-company-code chart of accounts and financial consolidation across the enterprise.

attributes.name: cost_center_code
attributes.description: SAP S/4HANA cost center code linked to this GL account for cost center accounting. Enables expense tracking and allocation by organizational unit, department, or function. Supports management reporting and budget variance analysis.

attributes.name: cost_element_category
attributes.description: Indicates whether this GL account is also defined as a cost element in SAP Controlling (CO) module. Primary cost elements correspond to GL expense/revenue accounts; secondary cost elements are used for internal cost allocations and have no corresponding GL account. Drives cost center accounting and profitability analysis.

attributes.name: created_by_user
attributes.description: SAP S/4HANA user ID of the person who created this GL account master record. Required for SOX-compliant audit trails, segregation of duties controls, and financial governance. Corresponds to SAP S/4HANA ERNAM field.
attributes.references: SOX Section 302/404; SAP S/4HANA FI-GL; ISO 27001

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when this GL account master record was first created in the system. Provides the audit trail creation point for SOX compliance and data lineage tracking. Corresponds to SAP S/4HANA creation date and time fields.
attributes.references: SOX Section 302/404; SAP S/4HANA FI-GL; ISO 27001

attributes.name: currency_code
attributes.description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the account currency. When set, all postings to this account must be in this currency. If null, the account accepts postings in any currency and balances are maintained in the company code currency. Critical for multi-currency financial reporting and foreign exchange management.
attributes.references: ISO 4217; SAP S/4HANA FI-GL; IFRS IAS 21

attributes.name: field_status_group
attributes.description: SAP S/4HANA field status group code assigned to this GL account that controls which fields are required, optional, or suppressed during document entry for postings to this account. Ensures data completeness and consistency for financial postings.

attributes.name: financial_statement_section
attributes.description: Identifies which primary financial statement this GL account contributes to. Drives automated financial statement preparation and regulatory reporting. Critical for SOX-compliant period-end close and IFRS/GAAP financial reporting.
attributes.references: IFRS IAS 1; US GAAP ASC 205; SOX Section 404

attributes.name: fiscal_period
attributes.description: The fiscal period (accounting period) within the fiscal year to which the current account balance applies. Typically 1-12 for monthly periods, with additional special periods (13-16) for year-end adjustments. Corresponds to SAP S/4HANA posting period (MONAT).
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA FI-GL; SOX Section 404; IFRS IAS 1

attributes.name: fiscal_year
attributes.description: The fiscal year to which the current account balance and period-end close data applies. Supports multi-year financial reporting, year-over-year comparisons, and SOX-compliant audit trails. Corresponds to SAP S/4HANA fiscal year (GJAHR).
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA FI-GL; SOX Section 404; IFRS IAS 1

attributes.name: fiscal_year_variant
attributes.description: SAP S/4HANA fiscal year variant code that defines the fiscal year structure (calendar year, non-calendar year, or shortened fiscal year) and the number of posting periods. Determines how fiscal periods map to calendar months for financial reporting.

attributes.name: functional_area
attributes.description: SAP S/4HANA functional area classification that categorizes GL accounts by business function (e.g., sales, marketing, production, administration, research and development). Enables cost-of-sales accounting and functional income statement reporting per IFRS IAS 1.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA FI-GL; IFRS IAS 1; US GAAP ASC 205

attributes.name: hierarchy_level
attributes.description: Numeric level of this GL account within the account hierarchy tree, where level 1 represents the top-most grouping (e.g., total assets) and higher numbers represent more granular accounts. Used for financial statement rollup logic and management reporting drill-down.

attributes.name: is_balance_sheet_relevant
attributes.description: Indicates whether this GL account carries a balance that is reported on the balance sheet. Balance sheet accounts (assets, liabilities, equity) carry forward their balances at year-end; P&L accounts are reset to zero. Drives automated year-end closing and balance carryforward processes.
attributes.references: IFRS IAS 1; US GAAP ASC 205; SAP S/4HANA FI-GL

attributes.name: is_posting_allowed
attributes.description: Indicates whether direct financial postings are permitted to this GL account. When false, the account is a summary/header account used only for hierarchical reporting and cannot receive direct journal entry postings. Corresponds to SAP S/4HANA posting block indicator.

attributes.name: is_reconciliation_account
attributes.description: Indicates whether this GL account is a reconciliation account that is automatically updated when postings are made to subledger accounts (accounts receivable, accounts payable, asset accounting). Reconciliation accounts cannot receive direct manual postings. Critical for AR/AP subledger-to-GL reconciliation.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA FI-GL; SOX Section 404; IFRS IAS 39

attributes.name: last_posting_date
attributes.description: The date of the most recent financial posting made to this GL account. Used for account activity monitoring, dormant account identification, and chart of accounts maintenance. Supports period-end close validation and SOX control testing.

attributes.name: line_item_display
attributes.description: Indicates whether individual line items posted to this GL account are stored and displayable for audit and analysis purposes. When true, each posting line is retained for drill-down reporting. Required for SOX audit trails and financial investigation. Corresponds to SAP S/4HANA line item display indicator (XKRES).

attributes.name: normal_balance
attributes.description: Indicates whether the GL account normally carries a debit or credit balance per double-entry bookkeeping rules. Assets and expenses normally carry debit balances; liabilities, equity, and revenue normally carry credit balances. Used for trial balance validation and automated posting rules.
attributes.references: IFRS IAS 1; US GAAP ASC 205; SAP S/4HANA FI-GL

attributes.name: open_item_management
attributes.description: Indicates whether open item management is activated for this GL account. When true, individual line items remain open until they are cleared against offsetting entries (e.g., payment clearing for bank accounts, GR/IR clearing accounts). Essential for bank reconciliation and clearing account management.

attributes.name: parent_account_number
attributes.description: Account number of the parent GL account in the account hierarchy. Enables multi-level account hierarchies for financial reporting rollups, management reporting, and financial consolidation. Null for top-level accounts in the hierarchy.

attributes.name: profit_center_code
attributes.description: SAP S/4HANA profit center code associated with this GL account for profitability analysis and segment reporting. Enables revenue and cost attribution to business segments, product lines, or geographic regions. Supports IFRS 8 operating segment disclosures.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA CO-PCA; IFRS 8; US GAAP ASC 280

attributes.name: reconciliation_account_type
attributes.description: Specifies the subledger type this reconciliation account is linked to. Determines which subledger transactions automatically update this GL account. Null or 'none' for non-reconciliation accounts. Corresponds to SAP S/4HANA reconciliation account type (MITKZ).

attributes.name: segment_code
attributes.description: Business segment identifier associated with this GL account for segment reporting. Supports IFRS 8 and US GAAP ASC 280 operating segment disclosures. Enables financial reporting by business segment such as marketplace, fulfillment, advertising, or subscription services.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA FI-GL; IFRS 8; US GAAP ASC 280

attributes.name: sort_key
attributes.description: SAP S/4HANA sort key code that determines which field is used to populate the allocation field in line items posted to this account. Controls how line items are sorted and displayed in account statements. Examples: 001 (posting date), 003 (document number), 031 (order number).

attributes.name: tax_category
attributes.description: Specifies the tax treatment applicable to postings on this GL account. Controls whether tax codes are required, optional, or not applicable for journal entries. Relevant for VAT/GST compliance, sales tax reporting, and indirect tax management. Corresponds to SAP S/4HANA tax category (MWSKZ). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: input_tax|output_tax|not_relevant|all_tax_codes|minus_sign — promote to reference product]
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA FI-GL; EU VAT Directive; IRS Tax Code

attributes.name: tolerance_group
attributes.description: SAP S/4HANA tolerance group assigned to this GL account that defines the permitted payment differences and rounding tolerances for clearing transactions. Controls automatic write-off of small differences during payment processing and bank reconciliation. Supports SOX controls over financial accuracy.

attributes.name: updated_by_user
attributes.description: SAP S/4HANA user ID of the person who last modified this GL account master record. Required for SOX-compliant audit trails, change management controls, and financial governance. Corresponds to SAP S/4HANA AENAM field.
attributes.references: SOX Section 302/404; SAP S/4HANA FI-GL; ISO 27001

attributes.name: updated_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp of the most recent modification to this GL account master record. Tracks changes to account attributes for SOX-compliant audit trails and data lineage. Essential for change management controls and period-end close integrity.
attributes.references: SOX Section 302/404; SAP S/4HANA FI-GL; ISO 27001

products.name: journal_entry
products.description: Authoritative record of all double-entry accounting transactions posted to the general ledger, including both header-level metadata and line-level debit/credit detail. Header captures posting dates, fiscal periods, document types, reference documents (order IDs, payment IDs, invoice numbers), reversal indicators, and SAP document numbers. Each line item captures GL account, cost center, profit center, amount in transaction and local currency, tax code, business area, and trading partner. Supports SOX audit trails, automated period-end close postings (accruals, depreciation, revaluations), and granular reconciliation workflows. Every entry must balance (total debits = total credits).

attributes.name: journal_entry_id
attributes.description: Unique surrogate identifier for each journal entry record in the general ledger. Serves as the primary key for the journal_entry data product in the Databricks Silver Layer.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA FI Module; SOX Section 302/404

attributes.name: accounts_payable_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.accounts_payable. Business justification: Journal entries are generated from AP invoice postings and payment events. Linking journal_entry to accounts_payable enables AP-to-GL drill-through for three-way match reconciliation and audit trails.

attributes.name: accounts_receivable_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.accounts_receivable. Business justification: Journal entries are generated from AR invoice postings, cash application, and write-off events. Linking journal_entry to accounts_receivable enables AR-to-GL drill-through for subledger reconciliation

attributes.name: cost_center_id
attributes.description: Reference to the cost center responsible for the expenditure or revenue captured in this journal entry. Enables cost allocation, departmental P&L reporting, and budget variance analysis.

attributes.name: finance_bank_account_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.finance_bank_account. Business justification: Bank-related journal entries (bank receipts, payments, reconciliation postings) reference specific bank accounts. Linking journal_entry to finance_bank_account enables bank-to-GL reconciliation and tr

attributes.name: legal_entity_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.legal_entity. Business justification: Journal entries are posted within a specific legal entity's books. journal_entry.company_code is a denormalized code; adding legal_entity_id provides a proper FK to the legal_entity master for entity-

attributes.name: line_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to order.order_line. Business justification: Journal entries for COGS, revenue, and line-level tax are posted at the order line level. Finance teams need drill-down from GL journal entries to source order lines for transaction-level audit, COGS

attributes.name: primary_journal_general_ledger_id
attributes.description: Reference to the General Ledger (GL) account to which this journal entry header is primarily associated. The GL account determines the financial statement line item and chart of accounts classification.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA FI-GL; GAAP/IFRS; SOX Section 302/404

attributes.name: profit_center_id
attributes.description: Reference to the profit center associated with this journal entry. Supports segment-level profitability reporting, GMV-to-revenue reconciliation, and management accounting.

attributes.name: settlement_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to payment.settlement. Business justification: Journal entries for settlement cash postings reference the settlement batch. Finance GL posting process for cash receipts from payment settlements requires tracing journal entries back to the settleme

attributes.name: supplier_id
attributes.description: Reference to the intercompany trading partner entity involved in this journal entry. Required for intercompany elimination during financial consolidation and statutory group reporting.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA FI-GL; GAAP/IFRS Consolidation; SOX Section 302/404

attributes.name: tax_record_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.tax_record. Business justification: Journal entries are generated from tax posting events (tax accruals, remittances, adjustments). Linking journal_entry to tax_record enables tax-to-GL reconciliation and SOX-compliant audit trails for

attributes.name: assignment_number
attributes.description: A free-form alphanumeric field used to group or sort journal entry line items for reconciliation and reporting purposes (e.g., payment run ID, clearing document number, batch reference). Supports open item management and account clearing workflows.

attributes.name: business_area
attributes.description: SAP business area code representing a distinct operational segment of the enterprise (e.g., marketplace, fulfillment, advertising). Enables cross-company-code financial reporting by business segment.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA FI-GL; GAAP/IFRS Segment Reporting (IFRS 8)

attributes.name: clearing_date
attributes.description: The date on which the open item represented by this journal entry line was cleared (offset by a payment or contra-entry). Used for aging analysis, DSO (Days Sales Outstanding) calculation, and AR/AP reconciliation.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA FI-GL; GAAP/IFRS; SOX Section 302/404

attributes.name: clearing_document_number
attributes.description: The SAP document number of the clearing transaction that offset this open item (e.g., payment clearing an invoice). Populated when the journal entry line has been cleared. Supports accounts receivable and accounts payable reconciliation workflows.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA FI-GL; GAAP/IFRS; SOX Section 302/404

attributes.name: company_code
attributes.description: SAP company code representing the legal entity or organizational unit for which the journal entry is posted. Used for entity-level financial consolidation and statutory reporting.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA FI Module; SOX Section 302/404; ISO 27001

attributes.name: debit_credit_indicator
attributes.description: Indicates whether the journal entry line represents a debit (D) or credit (C) posting in double-entry accounting. Every journal entry must balance such that total debits equal total credits, enforcing the fundamental accounting equation.
attributes.references: GAAP/IFRS; SAP S/4HANA FI-GL; SOX Section 302/404

attributes.name: document_date
attributes.description: The date of the original source document (invoice, receipt, contract) that triggered the journal entry. May differ from the posting date for accruals, reversals, or late-arriving documents.

attributes.name: document_type
attributes.description: SAP document type code classifying the nature of the journal entry (e.g., SA=General Ledger, KR=Vendor Invoice, DR=Customer Invoice, AB=Accounting Document, ZP=Payment Posting, AA=Asset Posting). Drives posting rules and account determination. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: SA|KR|DR|AB|ZP|AA|RE|RV|WA|WE — promote to reference product]

attributes.name: entry_timestamp
attributes.description: The precise date and time when the journal entry record was first created and captured in the system. Used for SOX audit trails, data lineage, and period-end close monitoring.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA FI-GL; SOX Section 302/404; ISO 27001

attributes.name: entry_type
attributes.description: Business classification of the journal entry indicating its accounting purpose: original posting, reversal of a prior entry, period-end accrual, asset depreciation run, currency revaluation, or intercompany elimination.
attributes.references: GAAP/IFRS; SAP S/4HANA FI-GL; SOX Section 302/404

attributes.name: exchange_rate
attributes.description: The foreign currency exchange rate applied to convert the transaction currency amount to the local currency at the time of posting. Critical for multi-currency reconciliation, currency revaluation, and GAAP/IFRS IAS 21 compliance.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA FI-GL; GAAP/IFRS IAS 21; ISO 4217

attributes.name: fiscal_period
attributes.description: The accounting period (1–16) within the fiscal year to which the journal entry belongs. Periods 13–16 are typically used for special period-end adjustments, accruals, and audit postings.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA FI-GL; GAAP/IFRS; SOX Section 302/404

attributes.name: fiscal_year
attributes.description: The fiscal year in which the journal entry is recorded (e.g., 2024). Used for annual financial reporting, SOX compliance, and year-end close processes.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA FI-GL; SOX Section 302/404; GAAP/IFRS

attributes.name: group_amount
attributes.description: The monetary amount of the journal entry converted to the group (consolidation) currency. Used for consolidated financial statements, MRR/ARR tracking, and enterprise-level GMV-to-revenue reconciliation.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA FI-GL; GAAP/IFRS IAS 21; SOX Section 302/404

attributes.name: group_currency
attributes.description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code of the enterprise group's reporting currency (e.g., USD). Used for consolidated financial statements and cross-entity comparisons.
attributes.references: ISO 4217; SAP S/4HANA FI-GL; GAAP/IFRS IAS 21

attributes.name: header_text
attributes.description: Free-text description at the journal entry header level explaining the business purpose or context of the posting (e.g., 'Q4 Accrual - Marketplace Seller Commissions', 'Monthly Depreciation Run'). Used for audit review and financial close documentation.

attributes.name: is_intercompany
attributes.description: Flag indicating whether this journal entry represents an intercompany transaction between two legal entities within the same corporate group (True) or an external third-party transaction (False). Intercompany entries require elimination during group financial consolidation.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA FI-GL; GAAP/IFRS IAS 27; SOX Section 302/404

attributes.name: is_statistical
attributes.description: Flag indicating whether this journal entry is a statistical (non-real) posting used for management reporting and cost allocation purposes only (True), as opposed to a real posting that updates actual financial balances (False). Statistical postings do not affect the balance sheet.

attributes.name: last_updated_timestamp
attributes.description: The date and time when the journal entry record was last modified. Supports change tracking, audit trails, and incremental data pipeline processing in the Databricks Silver Layer.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA FI-GL; SOX Section 302/404; ISO 27001

attributes.name: line_item_number
attributes.description: Sequential line number within the journal entry document identifying the position of each debit or credit line. Used to uniquely reference individual line items within a multi-line journal entry for reconciliation and audit purposes.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA FI-GL; GAAP/IFRS; SOX Section 302/404

attributes.name: line_item_text
attributes.description: Free-text description at the individual line item level providing additional context for the specific debit or credit posting (e.g., 'Accrued seller commission - Nov 2024', 'Revenue recognition - Order #ORD-98765'). Supports granular audit trails and reconciliation.

attributes.name: local_amount
attributes.description: The monetary amount of the journal entry converted to the local (company code) currency. Used for statutory financial reporting and local GAAP compliance in the jurisdiction of the legal entity.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA FI-GL; GAAP/IFRS IAS 21; SOX Section 302/404

attributes.name: local_currency
attributes.description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code of the company code's local (functional) currency (e.g., USD for US entities, EUR for EU entities). Used for local statutory reporting and tax filings.
attributes.references: ISO 4217; SAP S/4HANA FI-GL; GAAP/IFRS IAS 21

attributes.name: posting_date
attributes.description: The principal real-world accounting date on which the journal entry is posted to the general ledger. Determines the fiscal period assignment and is the primary date used for period-end close, financial reporting, and regulatory submissions.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA FI-GL; GAAP/IFRS; SOX Section 302/404

attributes.name: posting_key
attributes.description: SAP two-digit posting key that determines the account type, debit/credit assignment, and field selection for the journal entry line (e.g., 40=GL Debit, 50=GL Credit, 01=Customer Invoice, 31=Vendor Invoice). Drives account determination and field status.

attributes.name: posting_status
attributes.description: Current lifecycle state of the journal entry in the general ledger workflow. Draft entries are pending approval; posted entries are final; reversed entries have been offset by a counter-entry; parked entries await completion; held entries are saved but not yet posted; error entries failed validation.

attributes.name: reference_document_type
attributes.description: The type of source business document that originated this journal entry (e.g., order, payment, invoice, purchase_order, return, shipment). Enables traceability from the GL back to the originating operational transaction.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA FI-GL; SOX Section 302/404; GAAP/IFRS

attributes.name: reversal_date
attributes.description: The date on which the reversal of this journal entry is scheduled or was executed. Used for automated period-end accrual reversals and audit trail completeness.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA FI-GL; GAAP/IFRS; SOX Section 302/404

attributes.name: reversal_indicator
attributes.description: Flag indicating whether this journal entry is a reversal of a previously posted entry (True) or an original posting (False). Reversal entries offset prior postings and are critical for period-end close accuracy and SOX audit trails.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA FI-GL; GAAP/IFRS; SOX Section 302/404

attributes.name: reversed_document_number
attributes.description: The SAP document number of the original journal entry that this entry reverses. Populated only when reversal_indicator is True. Enables audit trail linkage between original and reversal postings.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA FI-GL; SOX Section 302/404; GAAP/IFRS

attributes.name: sap_document_number
attributes.description: The externally-known accounting document number assigned by SAP S/4HANA upon posting. This is the primary business identifier used by finance teams to reference a journal entry across systems, audit trails, and reconciliation workflows.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA FI-GL Module; SOX Section 302/404

attributes.name: source_system
attributes.description: Identifies the operational system of record that originated this journal entry (e.g., SAP_S4HANA for ERP-generated entries, OMS for order management system auto-postings, PAYMENT_GATEWAY for payment settlement entries, WMS for inventory adjustments, MANUAL for manually entered entries). Supports data lineage and audit trail requirements.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA FI-GL; SOX Section 302/404; ISO 27001

attributes.name: tax_amount
attributes.description: The calculated tax amount (VAT, GST, or sales tax) associated with this journal entry in the transaction currency. Used for tax liability reporting, tax reconciliation, and regulatory filings.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA FI-TX; EU VAT Directive; SOX Section 302/404

attributes.name: tax_code
attributes.description: SAP tax code identifying the applicable tax rate and tax category (e.g., VAT, GST, sales tax) for the journal entry line. Drives automatic tax calculation and tax reporting to regulatory authorities.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA FI-TX; EU VAT Directive; FTC; GDPR

attributes.name: transaction_amount
attributes.description: The gross monetary amount of the journal entry in the transaction currency (the currency of the originating business event). Used as the base amount before currency conversion for multi-currency reconciliation.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA FI-GL; GAAP/IFRS IAS 21; SOX Section 302/404

attributes.name: transaction_currency
attributes.description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code of the originating transaction (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Represents the currency in which the business event was denominated before conversion to local or group currency.
attributes.references: ISO 4217; SAP S/4HANA FI-GL; GAAP/IFRS IAS 21

products.name: journal_entry_line
products.description: Individual debit or credit line items within a journal entry. Captures GL account, cost center, profit center, amount, currency, tax code, and business area for each posting line. Enables granular financial analysis at the line-item level and supports reconciliation workflows in SAP S/4HANA.

attributes.name: journal_entry_line_id
attributes.description: Unique surrogate identifier for each individual debit or credit line item within a journal entry. Serves as the primary key for this entity in the Databricks Silver Layer.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA FI Module — Journal Entry Line Item (ACDOCA)

attributes.name: cost_center_id
attributes.description: Reference to the cost center responsible for this expenditure or revenue posting. Used for internal management accounting, departmental P&L reporting, and budget variance analysis.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA CO Module — Cost Center Master (CSKS); SOX Section 302/404

attributes.name: general_ledger_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.general_ledger. Business justification: Each journal entry line posts to a specific GL account — this is the fundamental double-entry accounting relationship. The existing gl_account_id FK points to customer.account (cross-domain, semantica

attributes.name: journal_entry_id
attributes.description: Reference to the parent journal entry header that this line item belongs to. Establishes the header-detail relationship for the posting document. Satisfies TRANSACTION_LINE HEADER_REFERENCE category.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA FI Module — Accounting Document Header (BKPF)

attributes.name: legal_entity_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.legal_entity. Business justification: Journal entry lines belong to a specific legal entity's books. journal_entry_line.company_code is a denormalized code; adding legal_entity_id provides a proper FK to the legal_entity master for entity

attributes.name: profit_center_id
attributes.description: Reference to the profit center associated with this posting line. Enables profitability analysis by business segment, product line, or geographic region within the enterprise.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA EC-PCA Module — Profit Center Master (CEPC); IFRS 8 Operating Segments

attributes.name: account_type
attributes.description: SAP account type indicator for this line item: A=Asset, D=Customer (Debtor), K=Vendor (Creditor), M=Material, S=General Ledger. Determines which subledger the posting affects. Corresponds to KOART in SAP S/4HANA.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA FI Module — BSEG.KOART; GAAP Chart of Accounts Classification

attributes.name: amount
attributes.description: The monetary amount of this journal entry line in the transaction currency. Always stored as a positive value; the debit_credit_indicator determines the sign direction. Satisfies TRANSACTION_LINE LINE_VALUE_OR_RESULT category. Corresponds to WRBTR in SAP S/4HANA.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA FI Module — ACDOCA.WRBTR; GAAP ASC 820; IFRS IFRS 13

attributes.name: amount_group_currency
attributes.description: The monetary amount of this line item translated into the group (consolidation) currency for enterprise-wide financial consolidation and consolidated financial statement preparation. Corresponds to KZBTR in SAP S/4HANA.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA FI Module — ACDOCA; IFRS 10 Consolidated Financial Statements; SOX Section 302

attributes.name: amount_local_currency
attributes.description: The monetary amount of this line item translated into the company code local currency (functional currency). Used for statutory financial reporting in the entity's home jurisdiction. Corresponds to DMBTR in SAP S/4HANA.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA FI Module — ACDOCA.DMBTR; IFRS IAS 21 Foreign Currency Transactions; GAAP ASC 830

attributes.name: assignment_number
attributes.description: Alphanumeric assignment field used for sorting and matching open items in accounts receivable and accounts payable clearing. Typically populated with the customer/vendor reference or payment advice number. Corresponds to ZUONR in SAP S/4HANA.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA FI Module — BSEG.ZUONR; AR/AP Reconciliation Best Practices

attributes.name: business_area
attributes.description: SAP business area code representing a distinct area of operations or responsibility within the enterprise (e.g., marketplace, fulfillment, logistics). Enables cross-company-code financial reporting by business segment. Corresponds to GSBER in SAP S/4HANA.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA FI Module — ACDOCA.GSBER; IFRS 8 Operating Segments

attributes.name: clearing_date
attributes.description: The date on which this open item was cleared (matched and offset) against a payment or credit memo. Null if the item remains open. Used for aging analysis and DSO (Days Sales Outstanding) calculations. Corresponds to AUGDT in SAP S/4HANA.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA FI Module — BSEG.AUGDT; GAAP ASC 310 Receivables

attributes.name: clearing_document_number
attributes.description: The accounting document number that cleared (offset) this open item in accounts receivable or accounts payable. Populated when an invoice is matched against a payment. Corresponds to AUGBL in SAP S/4HANA.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA FI Module — BSEG.AUGBL; AR/AP Clearing and Reconciliation Standards

attributes.name: company_code
attributes.description: SAP company code representing the legal entity or organizational unit for which this journal entry line is posted. Enables multi-entity financial consolidation and legal reporting. Corresponds to BUKRS in SAP S/4HANA.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA FI Module — Company Code (T001); SOX Section 302/404; IFRS 10 Consolidated Financial Statements

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: The date and time when this journal entry line record was created in the system. Provides the audit trail creation timestamp required for SOX compliance and financial record integrity. Corresponds to CPUDT/CPUTM in SAP S/4HANA.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA FI Module — BKPF.CPUDT/CPUTM; SOX Section 404; ISO 27001

attributes.name: currency_code
attributes.description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the transaction amount on this line item (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Enables multi-currency financial reporting and foreign exchange revaluation. Corresponds to WAERS in SAP S/4HANA.
attributes.references: ISO 4217 Currency Codes; SAP S/4HANA FI Module — ACDOCA.WAERS; IFRS IAS 21

attributes.name: debit_credit_indicator
attributes.description: Indicates whether this line item is a debit (D) or credit (C) posting. Fundamental to double-entry bookkeeping; every journal entry must have equal debits and credits. Corresponds to SHKZG in SAP S/4HANA.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA FI Module — ACDOCA.SHKZG; GAAP Double-Entry Accounting Principles; IFRS Conceptual Framework

attributes.name: document_date
attributes.description: The date of the original source document (invoice, receipt, contract) that triggered this journal entry line. May differ from the posting date when documents are processed with a lag. Corresponds to BLDAT in SAP S/4HANA.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA FI Module — BKPF.BLDAT; GAAP ASC 606 Revenue Recognition

attributes.name: document_type
attributes.description: Two-character SAP document type classifying the nature of the accounting document (e.g., SA=GL posting, KR=vendor invoice, DR=customer invoice, ZP=payment, AB=asset posting). Controls number range assignment and account types allowed. Corresponds to BLART in SAP S/4HANA.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA FI Module — BKPF.BLART; GAAP Source Document Classification

attributes.name: due_date
attributes.description: The calculated due date for payment of this line item based on the document date and payment terms. Used for cash flow forecasting, payment run scheduling, and overdue item identification. Corresponds to FAEDT in SAP S/4HANA.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA FI Module — BSEG.FAEDT; GAAP ASC 310 Receivables Management

attributes.name: fiscal_period
attributes.description: The fiscal period (accounting period, typically 1–12 for monthly, or 1–16 including special periods) within the fiscal year for this posting. Enables period-level financial reporting and month-end close tracking. Corresponds to POPER in SAP S/4HANA.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA FI Module — ACDOCA.POPER; GAAP ASC 270

attributes.name: fiscal_year
attributes.description: The fiscal year in which this journal entry line is recorded for financial reporting purposes. Used for annual financial statement preparation and year-over-year comparisons. Corresponds to GJAHR in SAP S/4HANA.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA FI Module — ACDOCA.GJAHR; GAAP ASC 270; IFRS IAS 1

attributes.name: functional_area
attributes.description: Functional area classification for this posting line, used to categorize expenses by business function (e.g., cost of goods sold, selling, general and administrative, research and development) for income statement presentation. Corresponds to FKBER in SAP S/4HANA.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA FI Module — ACDOCA.FKBER; GAAP ASC 220; IFRS IAS 1

attributes.name: is_cleared
attributes.description: Boolean flag indicating whether this open item has been cleared (True) or remains open (False) in the subledger. Drives open item management reporting for AR and AP aging analysis.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA FI Module — BSEG Open Item Management; GAAP ASC 310

attributes.name: line_item_text
attributes.description: Free-text description of this individual journal entry line item, providing business context for the posting (e.g., 'Q3 marketplace commission accrual', 'FBA fulfillment fee reversal'). Corresponds to SGTXT in SAP S/4HANA.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA FI Module — BSEG.SGTXT; SOX Section 404 Documentation Requirements

attributes.name: line_number
attributes.description: Sequential line number within the parent journal entry, used to order and uniquely identify each posting line within a document. Satisfies TRANSACTION_LINE LINE_SEQUENCE category. Corresponds to BUZEI in SAP S/4HANA ACDOCA.

attributes.name: local_currency_code
attributes.description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the company code local (functional) currency. Enables reconciliation between transaction currency and statutory reporting currency. Corresponds to HWAER in SAP S/4HANA.
attributes.references: ISO 4217 Currency Codes; SAP S/4HANA FI Module — ACDOCA.HWAER; IFRS IAS 21

attributes.name: payment_terms
attributes.description: Payment terms key applicable to this line item, defining the due date calculation and any early payment discount conditions (e.g., NET30, 2/10NET30). Relevant for vendor invoice and customer invoice line items. Corresponds to ZTERM in SAP S/4HANA.
