attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA MIRO WMWST; GDPR Article 5; EU VAT Directive 2006/112/EC; SOX Section 404

attributes.name: tax_code
attributes.description: Tax classification code applied to the invoice for VAT/GST determination and input tax recovery. Maps to SAP S/4HANA MIRO MWSKZ tax code, which drives the tax calculation procedure and GL tax account assignment.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA MIRO MWSKZ; EU VAT Directive 2006/112/EC; GDPR Article 5

attributes.name: three_way_match_status
attributes.description: Result of the three-way match validation comparing the Purchase Order (PO), Goods Receipt (GR), and supplier invoice for quantity and price alignment. PENDING: match not yet executed; MATCHED: all three documents reconcile within tolerance; PARTIAL_MATCH: some line items match; FAILED: material discrepancy detected. Core control in the procure-to-pay process per SAP MIRO.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA MIRO Three-Way Match; SOX Section 404; COSO Internal Controls Framework

attributes.name: updated_timestamp
attributes.description: Date and time when the supplier invoice record was last modified in the procurement data platform. Tracks the most recent status change, discrepancy update, or approval action. Used for change data capture (CDC) in the Databricks Silver Layer pipeline.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA AEDAT/AEZET; SOX Section 404; ISO 27001 - Audit Logging

products.name: supplier_performance
products.description: Periodic supplier performance scorecard capturing operational KPIs per supplier per evaluation period (monthly/quarterly/annual). Tracks on-time delivery rate, fill rate, defect rate (DPPM), invoice accuracy rate, lead time adherence, return rate, SLA breach count, NPS from procurement team, overall weighted performance score, and performance tier classification (gold/silver/bronze/probation). Drives supplier development programs, contract renewal decisions, and automated supplier tier reclassification. SSOT for supplier operational performance metrics; does not own financial credit risk scores (finance domain).

attributes.name: supplier_performance_id
attributes.description: Unique surrogate identifier for each supplier performance scorecard record. Primary key for the supplier_performance data product in the procurement domain.
attributes.references: ISO/IEC 11179 - Data Element Naming and Definition

attributes.name: category_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to product.category. Business justification: Category-level supplier performance scorecards are a standard procurement KPI report in e-commerce. Category managers evaluate supplier on-time delivery, defect rates, and fill rates within their spec

attributes.name: seller_profile_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to seller.seller_profile. Business justification: Performance monitoring: supplier performance scores are evaluated per seller to drive procurement decisions and SLA enforcement.

attributes.name: supplier_id
attributes.description: Reference to the supplier (vendor) being evaluated in this performance scorecard period. Links to the master supplier/vendor record managed in SAP S/4HANA Supplier Master.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA Supplier Master - Vendor Account

attributes.name: vendor_contract_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to procurement.vendor_contract. Business justification: A supplier performance scorecard measures KPIs (on-time delivery, fill rate, defect rate) against SLA targets defined in the governing vendor contract. vendor_contract carries sla_on_time_delivery_pct

attributes.name: avg_actual_lead_time_days
attributes.description: Average number of calendar days between purchase order placement and goods receipt for all POs evaluated during the period. Compared against contracted lead time to compute lead_time_adherence_rate and inform replenishment planning.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA Supply Chain Planning - Lead Time Management

attributes.name: contract_renewal_recommendation
attributes.description: Procurement team's formal recommendation for the supplier's contract renewal based on this evaluation period's performance scorecard. Drives contract lifecycle management decisions in SAP S/4HANA. Classified as confidential due to commercial sensitivity.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA Vendor Contract Management; Ecommerce Procurement Policy; SOX Internal Controls

attributes.name: contracted_lead_time_days
attributes.description: The agreed-upon lead time in calendar days as specified in the supplier's active vendor contract or purchase agreement. Serves as the benchmark for lead_time_adherence_rate calculation and SLA compliance assessment.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA Vendor Contract Management; SLA (Service Level Agreement) Terms

attributes.name: corrective_action_count
attributes.description: Number of formal corrective action requests (CARs) issued to the supplier during the evaluation period due to quality, delivery, or compliance failures. High counts indicate systemic supplier issues requiring escalation or probation review.
attributes.references: ISO 9001:2015 - Corrective Action Process; Ecommerce Supplier Quality Management

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: The timestamp when this supplier performance scorecard record was first created in the system. Audit trail field aligned with ISO 27001 data governance requirements and SOX internal control documentation.
attributes.references: ISO 27001 - Audit Trail; SOX Section 404 - Record Keeping

attributes.name: critical_sla_breach_count
attributes.business_glossary_term: Critical Service Level Agreement (SLA) Breach Count
attributes.description: Number of high-severity SLA breaches that caused material business impact (e.g., stockouts, production stoppages, customer order cancellations) during the evaluation period. Subset of sla_breach_count; used for escalation and probation decisions.
attributes.references: SLA (Service Level Agreement) Contract Terms; Ecommerce Procurement Escalation Policy

attributes.name: data_source_system
attributes.description: Identifies the operational system of record from which the KPI data for this scorecard was primarily sourced. SAP_S4HANA for ERP-driven metrics; WMS for warehouse receiving data; TMS for delivery performance; SELLER_PORTAL for supplier self-reported data; MANUAL for procurement team input.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA ERP; WMS (Warehouse Management System); TMS (Transportation Management System)

attributes.name: defect_rate_dppm
attributes.business_glossary_term: Defect Rate - Defective Parts Per Million (DPPM)
attributes.description: Number of defective units received per one million units delivered by the supplier during the evaluation period. Industry-standard quality metric (DPPM - Defective Parts Per Million) used to assess product quality compliance and drive supplier quality improvement programs.
attributes.references: ISO 9001:2015 - Product Quality; Six Sigma DPPM Methodology; CPSC Product Safety Standards

attributes.name: development_program_enrolled_flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether the supplier has been enrolled in a formal supplier development program as a result of this evaluation period's performance. Typically triggered by bronze tier or probation classification.
attributes.references: Ecommerce Supplier Development Program Policy; ISO 9001:2015 - Supplier Improvement

attributes.name: dispute_count
attributes.description: Number of formal disputes raised by either Ecommerce or the supplier regarding PO terms, invoice discrepancies, or delivery claims during the evaluation period. Sourced from SAP S/4HANA dispute management module.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA Dispute Management; SOX Internal Controls

attributes.name: documentation_compliance_rate
attributes.description: Percentage of shipments accompanied by complete and accurate documentation (packing lists, certificates of conformance, customs documents, safety data sheets) as required by contract and regulatory standards during the evaluation period.
attributes.references: CPSC Product Safety Documentation; WTO International Trade Documentation; ISO 9001:2015

attributes.name: evaluation_notes
attributes.description: Free-text field for the procurement evaluator to capture qualitative observations, context, or exceptions that are not reflected in the quantitative KPI scores (e.g., force majeure events, supply chain disruptions, extraordinary performance).

attributes.name: evaluation_period_end_date
attributes.description: The last calendar date of the performance evaluation window. Paired with evaluation_period_start_date to define the full measurement interval for all KPIs.

attributes.name: evaluation_period_start_date
attributes.description: The first calendar date of the performance evaluation window. Used to define the measurement boundary for all KPIs captured in this scorecard.

attributes.name: evaluation_period_type
attributes.description: Granularity of the performance evaluation cycle: monthly for operational monitoring, quarterly for strategic reviews, and annual for contract renewal decisions.

attributes.name: fill_rate
attributes.description: Percentage of ordered quantity actually fulfilled and shipped by the supplier during the evaluation period. Calculated as (units shipped / units ordered) × 100. Measures supplier's ability to meet demand without shortfalls, critical for inventory replenishment planning.
attributes.references: ISO 9001:2015 - Order Fulfillment Performance; WMS Fill Rate Standards

attributes.name: invoice_accuracy_rate
attributes.description: Percentage of supplier invoices that matched the corresponding purchase order without discrepancies (price, quantity, or terms) during the evaluation period. Calculated as (accurate invoices / total invoices) × 100. Impacts accounts payable efficiency and procure-to-pay cycle time in SAP S/4HANA.
attributes.references: SOX Section 404 - Internal Controls; SAP S/4HANA AP Invoice Matching

attributes.name: last_updated_timestamp
attributes.description: The timestamp when this supplier performance scorecard record was most recently modified. Tracks revisions during the draft, review, and dispute resolution lifecycle stages. Required for data lineage in the Databricks Silver Layer.
attributes.references: ISO 27001 - Audit Trail; SOX Section 404 - Record Keeping; Databricks Lakehouse Silver Layer Standards

attributes.name: lead_time_adherence_rate
attributes.description: Percentage of purchase orders where the supplier's actual lead time met or was within the contracted lead time during the evaluation period. Calculated as (orders within lead time / total orders) × 100. Critical for inventory replenishment planning and MOQ (Minimum Order Quantity) management.
attributes.references: ISO 9001:2015 - Lead Time Management; MOQ (Minimum Order Quantity) Standards

attributes.name: moq_compliance_rate
attributes.description: Percentage of purchase orders where the supplier honored the contracted MOQ (Minimum Order Quantity) terms during the evaluation period. Non-compliance may indicate supply constraints or contract violations requiring procurement intervention.
attributes.references: MOQ (Minimum Order Quantity) Contract Terms; SAP S/4HANA Procurement Policy

attributes.name: on_time_delivery_rate
attributes.description: Percentage of purchase orders delivered by the supplier on or before the confirmed delivery date during the evaluation period. Calculated as (on-time deliveries / total deliveries) × 100. Key operational KPI for SLA compliance monitoring.
attributes.references: ISO 9001:2015 - Delivery Performance; SLA (Service Level Agreement) Standards

attributes.name: open_corrective_action_count
attributes.description: Number of corrective action requests (CARs) issued to the supplier that remain unresolved as of the scorecard evaluation date. Unresolved CARs beyond SLA timelines may trigger escalation to probation tier.
attributes.references: ISO 9001:2015 - Corrective Action Closure; Ecommerce Supplier Escalation Policy

attributes.name: overall_performance_score
attributes.description: Composite weighted performance score (0–100) aggregating all KPI dimensions (on-time delivery, fill rate, defect rate, invoice accuracy, lead time adherence, return rate, SLA compliance, NPS) per the procurement scoring model. Primary driver for performance tier classification and contract renewal decisions.
attributes.references: ISO 9001:2015 - Supplier Scorecard Methodology; Ecommerce Procurement Scoring Model

attributes.name: performance_tier
attributes.description: Classified performance tier assigned to the supplier based on the weighted overall performance score for the evaluation period. Gold = top-tier preferred supplier; Silver = satisfactory; Bronze = needs improvement; Probation = at risk of contract termination. Drives supplier development programs and contract renewal decisions.
attributes.references: ISO 9001:2015 - Supplier Classification; Ecommerce Procurement Policy

attributes.name: previous_performance_tier
attributes.description: The supplier's performance tier from the immediately preceding evaluation period. Used to detect tier reclassification events and trigger automated supplier development workflows.
attributes.references: Ecommerce Procurement Policy - Supplier Tier Reclassification

attributes.name: procurement_cost_variance_pct
attributes.description: Percentage variance between the contracted unit price and the actual invoiced price across all POs in the evaluation period. Positive value indicates supplier overcharged vs. contract; negative indicates undercharge. Flags pricing compliance issues for AP reconciliation in SAP S/4HANA.
attributes.references: SOX Section 404 - Procurement Controls; SAP S/4HANA AP Price Variance

attributes.name: procurement_nps_score
attributes.description: Net Promoter Score (NPS) provided by the internal procurement team rating the supplier's responsiveness, communication quality, and overall partnership experience during the evaluation period. Scale: -100 to +100. Captures qualitative relationship health not reflected in operational KPIs.
attributes.references: NPS (Net Promoter Score) Methodology - Bain & Company; Ecommerce Procurement Relationship Management

attributes.name: return_rate
attributes.description: Percentage of received units returned to the supplier due to defects, non-conformance, or incorrect shipments during the evaluation period. Calculated as (units returned / units received) × 100. Feeds into RMA (Return Merchandise Authorization) processing and supplier quality programs.
attributes.references: ISO 9001:2015 - Non-Conformance Management; RMA (Return Merchandise Authorization) Process

attributes.name: scorecard_published_date
attributes.description: The date on which the finalized supplier performance scorecard was formally published and shared with the supplier. Marks the start of the supplier's response and dispute window.
attributes.references: ISO 9001:2015 - Supplier Communication; Ecommerce Procurement Policy

attributes.name: scorecard_reference_number
attributes.description: Externally-known business identifier for this supplier performance scorecard record, used in procurement communications, supplier development meetings, and audit trails. Format: SPC-YYYY-MM-SUPPID.

attributes.name: scorecard_status
attributes.description: Current lifecycle state of the supplier performance scorecard. Draft indicates data collection in progress; pending_review awaits procurement manager sign-off; approved is finalized; published is shared with supplier; disputed is under supplier challenge; closed is archived.

attributes.name: sla_breach_count
attributes.description: Total number of SLA (Service Level Agreement) violations recorded against the supplier during the evaluation period. Includes delivery, quality, and communication SLA breaches as defined in the vendor contract. Triggers penalty clauses and escalation workflows.
attributes.references: SLA (Service Level Agreement) Contract Terms; ISO 9001:2015 - Supplier Compliance

attributes.name: supplier_acknowledged_flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether the supplier has formally acknowledged receipt and review of the published performance scorecard via the Seller Management Portal. Required for audit trail and dispute resolution process initiation.
attributes.references: Ecommerce Seller Management Portal - Supplier Acknowledgement; ISO 9001:2015

attributes.name: supplier_acknowledgement_date
attributes.description: The date on which the supplier formally acknowledged the performance scorecard in the Seller Management Portal. Null if supplier_acknowledged_flag is False. Used to track response timeliness.
attributes.references: Ecommerce Seller Management Portal; ISO 9001:2015 - Supplier Communication

attributes.name: sustainability_compliance_score
attributes.description: Score (0–100) reflecting the supplier's adherence to environmental, social, and governance (ESG) standards and Ecommerce's supplier code of conduct during the evaluation period. Assessed via audits, certifications, and self-reported data. Increasingly required for regulatory and investor reporting.
attributes.references: ISO 14001 - Environmental Management; UN Global Compact; Ecommerce Supplier Code of Conduct

attributes.name: tier_reclassification_flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether the supplier's performance tier changed between the previous and current evaluation period. True triggers automated supplier development program enrollment or contract review workflows in the ERP.
attributes.references: Ecommerce Procurement Policy - Automated Supplier Tier Reclassification

attributes.name: total_pos_evaluated
attributes.description: Total number of purchase orders (POs) included in the performance evaluation for this period. Provides the denominator context for rate-based KPIs and ensures statistical significance of the scorecard.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA Purchase Order Management; PO (Purchase Order) Standards

attributes.name: total_units_received
attributes.description: Total quantity of units received from the supplier across all purchase orders during the evaluation period. Used as the volume denominator for defect rate, return rate, and fill rate calculations. Sourced from WMS (Warehouse Management System) receiving records.
attributes.references: WMS (Warehouse Management System) - Receiving Records; ISO 9001:2015

products.name: supplier_price_list
products.description: Agreed pricing catalog between the e-commerce platform and a supplier for specific SKUs, maintained under a contract or as a standalone purchasing info record. Captures supplier, SKU, price list version, unit price, currency, quantity break tiers (volume discount schedules), discount percentage, effective date, expiry date, and price list status (active/expired/superseded). SSOT for supplier-agreed pricing used in automated PO price validation, cost-of-goods calculations, and price comparison during requisition-to-PO conversion. Does not own dynamic selling prices (pricing domain) or actual invoice amounts (supplier_invoice).
products.reference: SAP Purchasing Info Record (EINE/EINA) / Price List

attributes.name: supplier_price_list_id
attributes.description: Unique surrogate identifier for the supplier price list record. Primary key for this entity in the procurement domain Silver layer.
attributes.references: ISO/IEC 11179 - Data Element Naming and Definition

attributes.name: buyer_account_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to customer.account. Business justification: B2B e-commerce supports account-specific supplier pricing (negotiated rates for enterprise buyers). supplier_price_list already has segment_id for segment-level pricing, but account-level pricing is a

attributes.name: cost_center_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Price‑list maintenance needs cost‑center attribution for budgeting of negotiated pricing programs.

attributes.name: general_ledger_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.general_ledger. Business justification: Ensures price‑list changes are reflected in the GL for expense forecasting and audit compliance.

attributes.name: marketplace_listing_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to marketplace.marketplace_listing. Business justification: Supplier price lists define the buy-side cost for products listed on the marketplace. Linking supplier_price_list to marketplace_listing enables real-time gross margin calculation (listing price minus

attributes.name: segment_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to customer.segment. Business justification: Enables segment‑specific pricing; the pricing engine selects a price list based on the customer.segment of the buyer.

attributes.name: seller_profile_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to seller.seller_profile. Business justification: Pricing: sellers receive negotiated price lists from suppliers; linking allows price‑list selection and margin calculations per seller.

attributes.name: sku_id
attributes.description: Reference to the specific SKU (Stock Keeping Unit) for which this price list entry applies. Each price list record covers exactly one SKU.
attributes.references: GS1 Global Trade Item Number (GTIN) Standard; SAP S/4HANA MM - Material Master (MM60)

attributes.name: superseded_by_supplier_price_list_id
attributes.description: Reference to the newer supplier_price_list record that replaced this one when status is 'superseded'. Enables version chain traversal for price history analysis and audit. Null if this is the current active version.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA MM - Purchasing Info Record Versioning; ISO 9001 Document Control

attributes.name: supplier_id
attributes.description: Reference to the supplier (vendor) who owns this price list. Links to the supplier master record in the procurement domain.

attributes.name: vendor_contract_id
attributes.description: Reference to the vendor contract or purchasing outline agreement under which this price list is governed. Null if the price list is a standalone purchasing info record not tied to a formal contract.

attributes.name: approval_date
attributes.description: The date on which this price list was formally approved by the authorized procurement officer. Required for SOX compliance audit trails and procurement governance reporting.
attributes.references: SOX Section 404 - Internal Controls; ISO 9001 Document Approval; SAP S/4HANA MM - Release Strategy Date

attributes.name: approved_by
attributes.description: The name or employee identifier of the procurement officer or manager who approved this price list for use in purchase order processing. Supports SOX procurement controls and audit trail requirements.
attributes.references: SOX Section 404 - Internal Controls over Financial Reporting; SAP S/4HANA MM - Release Strategy; ISO 9001 Procurement Approval

attributes.name: auto_po_pricing_enabled
attributes.business_glossary_term: Automatic Purchase Order (PO) Pricing Enabled Flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether this price list is enabled for automatic price determination during purchase order creation in the ERP system. When True, the system automatically applies this price list during PO generation without manual price entry.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA MM - Automatic Price Determination (OMFI); Procurement Automation Best Practices

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: The timestamp when this price list record was first created in the system. Provides the audit trail creation marker required for SOX compliance and data lineage tracking in the Silver layer.
attributes.references: SOX Section 404 - Audit Trail Requirements; ISO/IEC 27001 - Information Security; GDPR Article 5 (Accountability)

attributes.name: currency_code
attributes.description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which the unit price and all pricing tiers are denominated (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Required for multi-currency procurement and FX conversion in cost-of-goods calculations.
attributes.references: ISO 4217 Currency Codes; SAP S/4HANA MM - Purchasing Info Record Currency (EINE-WAERS)

attributes.name: discount_percentage
attributes.description: The agreed percentage discount applied to the list price to arrive at the net unit price. Represents the base-level trade discount negotiated with the supplier, separate from volume-tier discounts. Expressed as a percentage (e.g., 5.00 = 5%).
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA MM - Purchasing Info Record Condition (KONP-KBETR); CIPS Procurement Standards

attributes.name: effective_date
attributes.description: The date from which this price list becomes valid and can be used for automated purchase order price determination and cost-of-goods calculations. Aligns with the validity start date in the ERP purchasing info record.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA MM - Purchasing Info Record Validity Period (EKPO-MEPRF); ISO 8601 Date Standard

attributes.name: erp_info_record_number
attributes.business_glossary_term: Enterprise Resource Planning (ERP) Purchasing Info Record Number
attributes.description: The SAP S/4HANA Purchasing Info Record (PIR) number corresponding to this price list entry. Enables direct traceability between the Silver layer data product and the ERP system of record for procurement price determination.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA MM - Purchasing Info Record (ME11/ME12/ME13); ERP Integration Standards

attributes.name: expiry_date
attributes.description: The date after which this price list is no longer valid for purchase order pricing. Null indicates an open-ended price list with no defined expiration. Used to trigger price list renewal workflows.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA MM - Purchasing Info Record Validity Period (EKPO-MEPRF); ISO 8601 Date Standard

attributes.name: freight_included
attributes.description: Indicates whether freight/shipping costs are included in the quoted unit price. When True, no separate freight charge is added during PO creation. When False, freight is billed separately or calculated via the TMS.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA MM - Freight Condition (FRA1); ICC Incoterms 2020; APICS Supply Chain Standards

attributes.name: has_volume_tiers
attributes.description: Indicates whether this price list includes a volume discount schedule (quantity break tiers) in addition to the base unit price. When True, the associated supplier_price_list_tier records define the tiered pricing structure.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA MM - Scale Pricing (Condition Scales); CIPS Volume Discount Standards

attributes.name: incoterm_code
attributes.description: The International Commercial Terms (Incoterms) code defining the delivery obligations, risk transfer point, and cost responsibilities between the supplier and the e-commerce platform for shipments under this price list (e.g., FOB, DDP, EXW). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EXW|FCA|CPT|CIP|DAP|DPU|DDP|FAS|FOB|CFR|CIF — 11 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
attributes.references: ICC Incoterms 2020; SAP S/4HANA MM - Purchasing Info Record Incoterms (EKKO-INCO1); WTO International Trade Regulations

attributes.name: incoterm_location
attributes.description: The named place or port associated with the Incoterms code (e.g., 'Port of Shanghai' for FOB, 'Buyer Warehouse' for DDP). Required to fully specify the delivery terms per ICC Incoterms 2020 rules.
attributes.references: ICC Incoterms 2020; SAP S/4HANA MM - Purchasing Info Record Incoterms Location (EKKO-INCO2)

attributes.name: lead_time_days
attributes.description: The agreed supplier lead time in calendar days from purchase order placement to expected delivery at the warehouse. Used in inventory replenishment planning, safety stock calculations, and SLA monitoring.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA MM - Purchasing Info Record Planned Delivery Time (EINE-WEBAZ); APICS Supply Chain Standards

attributes.name: moq
attributes.description: The minimum quantity of the SKU that must be ordered per purchase order line to qualify for the agreed unit price. Orders below the MOQ may be rejected by the supplier or subject to a surcharge. Key procurement constraint for replenishment planning.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA MM - Purchasing Info Record Minimum Order Quantity (EINE-MINBM); CIPS Procurement Standards

attributes.name: notes
attributes.description: Free-text field for procurement team annotations, special conditions, or supplier-communicated caveats associated with this price list (e.g., 'Price valid only for orders placed before end of quarter', 'Subject to raw material surcharge above $X/tonne').
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA MM - Purchasing Info Record Text; ISO 9001 Document Management

attributes.name: origin_country_code
attributes.description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the country of origin for the SKU under this price list. Used for import duty calculation, customs compliance, trade restriction checks, and landed cost estimation.
attributes.references: ISO 3166-1 Alpha-3 Country Codes; WTO Rules of Origin; US CBP Import Regulations; EU Customs Code

attributes.name: payment_terms_code
attributes.description: The agreed payment terms code applicable to purchases under this price list (e.g., NET30, NET60, 2/10NET30 for 2% discount if paid within 10 days). Aligns with the payment terms configured in the ERP vendor master and accounts payable module.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA FI-AP - Payment Terms (T052); SOX Section 404 - Procurement Controls

attributes.name: price_basis
attributes.description: Indicates whether the unit price represents the gross list price (before discounts), the net agreed price (after discounts), or the landed cost (inclusive of freight, duties, and insurance). Critical for correct COGS calculation and PO price validation.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA MM - Condition Type (KONP); CIPS Cost Analysis Standards; APICS Landed Cost

attributes.name: price_list_category
attributes.description: Business category of the price list indicating its commercial purpose. 'standard' = regular agreed pricing; 'promotional' = time-limited promotional pricing; 'seasonal' = seasonal pricing adjustment; 'clearance' = end-of-life or excess inventory pricing; 'emergency' = expedited sourcing price.
attributes.references: CIPS Procurement Category Management; SAP S/4HANA MM - Condition Category

attributes.name: price_list_name
attributes.description: Human-readable name or description of the price list, such as 'Q1 2025 Electronics Pricing' or 'Annual Volume Agreement - Supplier XYZ'. Used for display and search in procurement portals.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA MM - Purchasing Info Record; Procurement Best Practices

attributes.name: price_list_number
attributes.description: Externally-known business identifier for this price list, used in supplier communications, purchase order references, and ERP purchasing info records. Follows the format PL-{alphanumeric code}.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA MM - Purchasing Info Record (ME11/ME12); ISO/IEC 11179

attributes.name: price_list_type
attributes.description: Classification of the price list by its commercial basis. 'contract_based' = tied to a formal vendor contract; 'standalone_pir' = standalone Purchasing Info Record (PIR) in ERP; 'spot_quote' = one-time quoted price; 'catalog' = supplier-published catalog price; 'blanket_order' = price under a blanket purchase order.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA MM - Purchasing Info Record Category; CIPS Procurement Standards

attributes.name: price_list_version
attributes.description: Version number of this price list, enabling tracking of successive revisions for the same supplier-SKU combination. Incremented each time the supplier submits a revised pricing schedule.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA MM - Purchasing Info Record versioning; ISO 9001 Document Control

attributes.name: price_tolerance_percentage
attributes.description: The maximum allowable percentage deviation between the price list unit price and the actual invoice price before a three-way match exception is raised. Used in automated invoice verification to flag price discrepancies requiring manual review.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA FI-AP - Invoice Verification Tolerance (OMR6); SOX Section 404 - AP Controls; CIPS Invoice Matching Standards

attributes.name: price_unit_quantity
attributes.description: The quantity basis for which the unit price is quoted (e.g., price per 1 unit, per 100 units, per 1000 units). Used to correctly compute per-unit cost when the supplier quotes prices in bulk multiples. Corresponds to the SAP Purchasing Info Record 'Price Unit' field.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA MM - Purchasing Info Record Price Unit (EINE-PEINH); GS1 Unit of Measure Standards

attributes.name: renewal_reminder_days
attributes.description: The number of days before the expiry date at which a renewal reminder notification should be triggered to the procurement team. Supports proactive price list renewal management and prevents procurement disruption due to expired pricing.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA MM - Purchasing Info Record Reminder; CIPS Contract Management Best Practices

attributes.name: source_document_reference
attributes.description: The reference number of the source document from which this price list was derived, such as a supplier quotation number, RFQ response number, or EDI catalog transmission ID. Provides traceability back to the originating supplier communication.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA MM - Request for Quotation (ME41); EDI X12 843 Price/Sales Catalog; ISO 9001 Document Traceability

attributes.name: supplier_price_list_status
attributes.description: Current lifecycle state of the price list. 'active' = currently valid for PO pricing; 'expired' = past expiry date; 'superseded' = replaced by a newer version; 'draft' = under preparation; 'pending_approval' = awaiting procurement approval; 'suspended' = temporarily deactivated.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA MM - Purchasing Info Record Status; ISO 9001 Procurement Controls

attributes.name: supplier_sku_code
attributes.description: The supplier's own part number or item code for this SKU, as used in the supplier's catalog and order acknowledgements. Required for EDI purchase order transmission and supplier invoice matching. Distinct from the internal SKU identifier.
attributes.references: GS1 GTIN Standards; EDI X12 850 Purchase Order Standard; SAP S/4HANA MM - Info Record Supplier Material Number (EINE-IDNLF)

attributes.name: supplier_sku_description
attributes.description: The supplier's description of the SKU as it appears in their catalog or price list document. Used for three-way matching during invoice verification and for resolving discrepancies between internal and supplier item descriptions.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA MM - Info Record Material Description (EINE-TXZ01); EDI X12 850 Standard

attributes.name: tax_classification_code
attributes.description: The tax classification code assigned to this SKU-supplier combination for VAT/GST determination during purchase order processing. Maps to the tax condition type in the ERP pricing procedure.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA MM - Tax Indicator (EINE-MWSKZ); EU VAT Directive; GDPR (not applicable); FTC Consumer Protection

attributes.name: tier_count
attributes.description: The number of quantity break tiers defined in the volume discount schedule for this price list. Zero if has_volume_tiers is False. Provides a quick summary of pricing complexity without joining to tier detail records.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA MM - Condition Scale Count; CIPS Volume Pricing Standards

attributes.name: unit_price
attributes.description: The agreed supplier unit price for one unit of the SKU at the base quantity tier. This is the SSOT for supplier-agreed cost used in automated PO price validation and cost-of-goods (COGS) calculations. Does not represent the selling price or invoice amount.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA MM - Purchasing Info Record Net Price (EINE-NETPR); PCI DSS (not applicable - non-payment data); CIPS Procurement Standards

attributes.name: uom_code
attributes.description: The unit of measure in which the SKU is priced and ordered (e.g., EA for each, CS for case, KG for kilogram, L for litre). Aligns with the UOM defined in the product master and used in purchase order line items.
attributes.references: GS1 Unit of Measure Codes; SAP S/4HANA MM - Material Master UOM (MARA-MEINS); ISO 80000 Quantities and Units

attributes.name: updated_timestamp
attributes.description: The timestamp when this price list record was last modified. Used for incremental ETL processing, change data capture, and audit trail maintenance in the Databricks Silver layer.
attributes.references: SOX Section 404 - Audit Trail Requirements; ISO/IEC 27001 - Information Security; Databricks Delta Lake Change Data Feed

products.name: approved_vendor_list
products.description: Master list of pre-qualified and approved vendors eligible for purchase order issuance, capturing approval status, qualification criteria met, and expiration dates.

attributes.name: approved_vendor_list_id
attributes.description: System-generated unique identifier for each record in the approved vendor list.

attributes.name: brand_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to product.brand. Business justification: Brand-authorized supplier qualification is a critical e-commerce procurement process: brand owners (e.g., Apple, Nike) authorize specific suppliers to source their products. Brand managers and procure

attributes.name: listing_category_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to marketplace.listing_category. Business justification: Vendor approval is often category-scoped — a supplier approved for Electronics may not be approved for Hazmat or Age-Restricted categories. This FK links AVL entries to marketplace listing categories,

attributes.name: marketplace_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to marketplace.marketplace. Business justification: Vendor approval is marketplace-specific in multi-marketplace platforms — a supplier approved for one marketplace may not be approved for another due to differing compliance, regulatory, or category re

attributes.name: primary_approved_supplier_id
attributes.description: Unique identifier of the vendor (supplier) referenced in the approved list.

attributes.name: superseded_approved_vendor_list_id
attributes.description: Self-referencing FK on approved_vendor_list (superseded_approved_vendor_list_id)

attributes.name: vendor_contract_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to procurement.vendor_contract. Business justification: The approved_vendor_list carries denormalized contract fields (contract_number, contract_start_date, contract_end_date, contract_status) that duplicate data already authoritatively stored in vendor_co

attributes.name: approval_status
attributes.description: Current status of the vendor's approval within the list.

attributes.name: approved_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when the vendor was officially approved.

attributes.name: compliance_iso_certified
attributes.description: True if the vendor holds a current ISO certification relevant to the business.

attributes.name: cpsc_certification_expiry
attributes.description: Expiration of the vendor's CPSC product safety certification.

attributes.name: cpsc_compliant
attributes.description: Indicates compliance with Consumer Product Safety Commission regulations.

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when the approved vendor record was first created.

attributes.name: data_source_system
attributes.description: Logical source identifier used for data lineage tracking.

attributes.name: effective_date
attributes.description: Date when the vendor's approved status becomes effective.

attributes.name: expiration_date
attributes.description: Date when the vendor's approved status expires, requiring re‑evaluation.

attributes.name: gdpr_compliant
attributes.description: True if the vendor adheres to GDPR data‑protection obligations.

attributes.name: is_diversity_supplier
attributes.description: True if the vendor qualifies under the company's diversity supplier program.

attributes.name: is_preferred_vendor
attributes.description: Indicates whether the vendor is marked as a preferred supplier for strategic sourcing.

attributes.name: iso_certification_expiry
attributes.description: Expiration date of the vendor's ISO certification.

attributes.name: last_review_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp of the most recent periodic review of the vendor's approval status.

attributes.name: lead_time_days
attributes.description: Average number of days the vendor takes to deliver after order receipt.

attributes.name: minimum_order_quantity
attributes.description: Minimum quantity that must be ordered from the vendor.

attributes.name: notes
attributes.description: Free‑form comments or observations about the vendor.

attributes.name: payment_currency
attributes.description: ISO 4217 currency code used for payments to the vendor.

attributes.name: payment_terms_code
attributes.description: Code representing the agreed payment terms (e.g., NET30, EOM).

attributes.name: pci_dss_compliant
attributes.description: Indicates whether the vendor complies with PCI DSS requirements.

attributes.name: pci_dss_expiry
attributes.description: Date when the vendor's PCI DSS compliance certification expires.

attributes.name: primary_contact_email
attributes.description: Email address of the vendor's primary contact.

attributes.name: primary_contact_name
attributes.description: Name of the main point‑of‑contact for the vendor.

attributes.name: primary_contact_phone
attributes.description: Phone number of the vendor's primary contact.

attributes.name: qualification_criteria_met
attributes.description: Textual description of the specific criteria the vendor satisfied for approval.

attributes.name: qualification_score
attributes.description: Numerical score reflecting how well the vendor meets qualification criteria.

attributes.name: risk_category
attributes.description: Risk tier derived from the risk score.

attributes.name: risk_score
attributes.description: Aggregated risk rating assigned to the vendor based on financial, compliance, and performance factors.

attributes.name: source_system
attributes.description: Name of the upstream system that supplied the vendor data (e.g., SAP, Oracle).

attributes.name: updated_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp of the most recent update to the record.

attributes.name: vendor_category
attributes.description: Broad product or service category the vendor supplies (e.g., electronics, apparel).

attributes.name: vendor_type
attributes.description: Classification of the vendor based on its business role.

domains.name: service
domains.description: Manages customer support cases, RMA processing, returns and refunds workflows, customer inquiries, and service tickets. Tracks NPS, CSAT, resolution time, and support SLA compliance. Integrates with CRM for unified customer service experience and case management.

products.name: support_case
products.description: Core master entity representing a customer support case or service ticket. Tracks the full lifecycle from creation through resolution including case type (billing, shipping, product defect, account access, returns, marketplace dispute referral), priority level (P1-P4), originating channel (phone, email, chat, social, self-service), assigned agent, current queue, escalation status, SLA policy reference, customer entitlement tier, first response timestamp, resolution timestamp, CSAT score, NPS score, and outcome disposition (resolved, unresolved, duplicate, transferred). Serves as the central operational record linking all service interactions, escalations, RMAs, refunds, and feedback for a single customer issue.

attributes.name: support_case_id
attributes.description: Unique identifier for the customer support case or service ticket. Primary key for the service support case entity.

attributes.name: account_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to customer.account. Business justification: Account‑level case reporting KPI for B2B customers requires linking each support case directly to the billing account.

attributes.name: accounts_receivable_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.accounts_receivable. Business justification: Billing dispute cases require direct reference to the AR record for resolution, dunning holds, and dispute status updates. E-commerce customer service teams routinely open cases against specific invoi

attributes.name: assigned_agent_id
attributes.description: Reference to the customer service agent currently assigned to work on this case. May be null if case is unassigned or in queue.

attributes.name: assigned_team_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to service.team. Business justification: service_support_case currently stores assigned_queue as a denormalized STRING representing the team or queue a case is routed to. Adding assigned_team_id → service.team.team_id normalizes this relatio

attributes.name: bundle_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to product.bundle. Business justification: Support cases are raised for bundle-specific issues (missing component, bundle pricing dispute, partial return eligibility). Agents need to identify the bundle to apply correct return policy and calcu

attributes.name: carrier_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to logistics.carrier. Business justification: Support case investigations often need the carrier responsible for the shipment to resolve delivery issues; agents reference carrier_id for accurate root‑cause analysis.

attributes.name: carrier_service_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to logistics.carrier_service. Business justification: When a case concerns service level or transit time, the specific carrier service used must be known for SLA compliance reporting.

attributes.name: catalog_item_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to product.catalog_item. Business justification: Product quality reporting, CPSC compliance tracking, and recall management require grouping all support cases by catalog item across all SKUs. Agents investigating product-level defects need this link

attributes.name: center_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to fulfillment.center. Business justification: Support cases often arise from issues at a specific fulfillment center (e.g., inventory or pickup problems); linking enables Center‑Level case reporting and SLA tracking.

attributes.name: channel_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to marketing.channel. Business justification: Omnichannel support routing and channel-level SLA assignment require linking each case to its originating marketing channel. E-commerce operations report CSAT and resolution time by channel; `originat

attributes.name: contact_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to customer.contact. Business justification: In B2B e-commerce, support cases are raised by a specific contact (buyer persona within an account). Contact-level case tracking drives SLA reporting per contact, escalation routing, and communication

attributes.name: cost_center_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Cost allocation report requires each support case to be charged to a cost center for internal accounting.

attributes.name: coupon_redemption_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to pricing.coupon_redemption. Business justification: Coupon failure and misapplication are among the top support case drivers in e-commerce. Linking the case directly to the coupon_redemption record allows agents to inspect redemption status, fraud flag

attributes.name: customer_profile_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to customer.customer_profile. Business justification: GDPR subject access requests and B2C NPS attribution require direct case-to-profile linkage. Service platforms universally link cases to the individual customer profile for personalization, data subje

attributes.name: fulfillment_order_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to fulfillment.fulfillment_order. Business justification: Support cases for missing items, wrong items, or late delivery are opened against a specific fulfillment order. Agents need direct access to fulfillment order status, method, and SLA tier during case

attributes.name: goods_receipt_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to procurement.procurement_goods_receipt. Business justification: Goods receipt discrepancy case management: when buyers report receiving wrong or damaged goods, the service case must trace to the GR record for three-way match investigation and discrepancy resolutio

attributes.name: header_id
attributes.description: Reference to the order associated with this support case, if the case relates to a specific order (e.g., shipping issue, order cancellation, item defect).

attributes.name: knowledge_article_id
attributes.description: Reference to the knowledge base article used to resolve this case. Links case resolution to documented solutions for knowledge management and self-service improvement.

attributes.name: line_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to order.order_line. Business justification: Support cases for wrong/damaged/missing items require line-level order linkage for accurate case routing, seller accountability reporting, and SLA tracking. The existing header_id identifies the order

attributes.name: logistics_shipment_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to logistics.logistics_shipment. Business justification: Customer service agents handling delivery complaints, lost packages, or SLA breach disputes need direct access to the logistics_shipment record for tracking, proof of delivery, and declared value — a

attributes.name: loyalty_enrollment_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to customer.loyalty_enrollment. Business justification: customer_entitlement_tier on service_support_case requires real-time loyalty tier validation for SLA routing and VIP case handling. Direct FK to loyalty_enrollment enables tier-based case prioritizati

attributes.name: marketplace_promotion_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to marketplace.marketplace_promotion. Business justification: Support cases frequently arise from marketplace promotion disputes (promotional price not honored, eligibility errors). Direct FK to marketplace_promotion enables promotion-specific case routing, FTC

attributes.name: marketplace_transaction_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to marketplace.marketplace_transaction. Business justification: Required for support case reporting: each case must reference the specific marketplace transaction that triggered the issue, enabling accurate SLA tracking and root‑cause analysis.

attributes.name: parent_case_service_support_case_id
attributes.description: Reference to a parent case if this case is a child or follow-up case. Used for linking related cases and tracking case hierarchies (e.g., original case and subsequent escalation or related inquiry).

attributes.name: price_override_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to pricing.price_override. Business justification: Pricing dispute cases — where customers question why they were charged a non-standard price — require agents to directly inspect the price_override record. This is one of the most common case types in

attributes.name: product_listing_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to product.product_listing. Business justification: Cases originating from listing-level issues (incorrect price displayed, suppressed listing, wrong description) require direct listing linkage for listing quality reporting, suppression root-cause anal

attributes.name: profit_center_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Profitability analysis of support services links cases to profit centers to measure contribution.

attributes.name: promotional_campaign_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to pricing.promotional_campaign. Business justification: Required for Promotion Impact on Support Cases report, linking each case to the promotional campaign that triggered the purchase, enabling analysis of support volume per campaign.

attributes.name: proof_of_delivery_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to fulfillment.proof_of_delivery. Business justification: Delivery dispute resolution is a named e-commerce support process: when a customer claims non-delivery, the agent must access the POD record (photo, GPS, signature) directly from the case. No existing

attributes.name: purchase_order_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to procurement.purchase_order. Business justification: PO dispute case management: buyers raise service cases referencing a specific purchase order for unfulfilled, incorrect, or billing-disputed POs. This link enables procurement dispute resolution workf

attributes.name: rma_id
attributes.description: Reference to the RMA record if this support case resulted in or is associated with a product return authorization.

attributes.name: sku_id
attributes.description: Stock Keeping Unit of the product related to this support case, if the case involves a specific product (e.g., defect, return, compatibility issue).

attributes.name: sla_policy_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to service.sla_policy. Business justification: service_support_case currently stores sla_policy_code as a denormalized STRING reference to the governing SLA policy. Adding a proper FK sla_policy_id → service.sla_policy.sla_policy_id normalizes thi

attributes.name: supplier_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to procurement.supplier. Business justification: Supplier dispute/complaint case management: procurement teams raise service cases against suppliers for SLA breaches, defective goods, or compliance violations. Linking service_support_case to supplie

attributes.name: supplier_invoice_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to procurement.supplier_invoice. Business justification: Invoice dispute case management: procurement teams raise service cases for supplier invoice discrepancies (overbilling, incorrect amounts, payment disputes). Linking service_support_case to supplier_i

attributes.name: vendor_contract_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to procurement.vendor_contract. Business justification: Contract dispute/SLA breach case management: service cases raised for vendor contract violations (missed SLAs, exclusivity breaches, penalty triggers) must reference the governing vendor contract. E-c

attributes.name: case_number
attributes.description: Externally visible unique case identifier displayed to customers and agents. Typically follows a format like CS-12345678 or CASE-0001234567.

attributes.name: case_type
attributes.description: Classification of the support case by the nature of the customer issue: billing inquiry, shipping problem, product defect, account access issue, returns processing, refund request, marketplace dispute, or general inquiry. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: billing|shipping|product_defect|account_access|returns|refund|marketplace_dispute|general_inquiry — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

attributes.name: closed_timestamp
attributes.description: Date and time when the case was formally closed and removed from active case inventory. May occur after resolution once customer confirms satisfaction or after a waiting period.

attributes.name: compensation_amount
attributes.description: Monetary compensation or goodwill credit provided to the customer as part of case resolution (e.g., account credit, discount voucher, refund). Null if no compensation was provided.

attributes.name: contact_attempts
attributes.description: Number of times the support team attempted to contact the customer regarding this case. Used for tracking customer responsiveness and case aging.

attributes.name: country_code
attributes.description: Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code indicating the country from which the customer initiated the support case (e.g., USA, GBR, DEU).

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: Date and time when the support case was first created in the system. Marks the beginning of the case lifecycle and SLA clock.

attributes.name: csat_score
attributes.description: Customer satisfaction score collected after case resolution, typically on a scale of 1.00 to 5.00. Measures customer satisfaction with the support experience and resolution quality.

attributes.name: customer_entitlement_tier
attributes.description: The service entitlement level of the customer at the time the case was created: standard (basic support), premium (priority support), VIP (dedicated support), enterprise (white-glove service). Influences SLA targets and routing.

attributes.name: escalation_status
attributes.description: Indicates whether and to what level the case has been escalated: not_escalated (standard handling), tier2 (specialist support), tier3 (expert/engineering), management (supervisor review), executive (C-level attention).

attributes.name: feedback_comments
attributes.description: Free-text feedback comments provided by the customer in post-case surveys. Captures qualitative insights about the support experience, agent performance, and areas for improvement.

attributes.name: first_response_timestamp
attributes.description: Date and time when the first substantive response was provided to the customer by an agent. Key SLA metric for measuring initial response time.

attributes.name: interaction_count
attributes.description: Total number of customer-agent interactions (calls, emails, chats) associated with this case. Indicates case complexity and effort required for resolution.

attributes.name: is_vip_case
attributes.description: Boolean flag indicating whether this case involves a VIP or high-value customer requiring special handling, expedited service, or executive attention. True for VIP cases, False otherwise.
attributes.references: Customer Segmentation and Service Tier Standards

attributes.name: language_code
attributes.description: Two-letter ISO 639-1 language code indicating the language in which the case was handled (e.g., en for English, es for Spanish, fr for French).

attributes.name: last_modified_timestamp
attributes.description: Date and time of the most recent update to any field in the case record. Used for tracking case activity and audit purposes.

attributes.name: nps_score
attributes.description: Net Promoter Score collected from the customer after case resolution, on a scale of 0 to 10. Measures customer loyalty and likelihood to recommend the company based on the support experience.

attributes.name: outcome_disposition
attributes.description: Final disposition of the case: resolved (issue successfully addressed), unresolved (could not be resolved), duplicate (merged with another case), transferred (moved to another system or team), withdrawn (customer cancelled request).

attributes.name: priority_level
attributes.description: Business priority assigned to the case based on urgency and impact. P1 (critical/urgent), P2 (high), P3 (medium), P4 (low). Determines SLA targets and routing rules.
attributes.references: ITIL Service Management - Incident Priority Matrix

attributes.name: reopened_count
attributes.description: Number of times this case has been reopened after being marked as resolved or closed. Indicates resolution quality issues or recurring customer problems.

attributes.name: resolution_notes
attributes.description: Detailed notes documenting the resolution provided to the customer, actions taken, root cause identified, and any follow-up required. Populated when case is resolved or closed.
attributes.references: Knowledge Management and Case Resolution Standards

attributes.name: resolution_timestamp
attributes.description: Date and time when the case was marked as resolved, indicating that a solution was provided to the customer. Key SLA metric for measuring time-to-resolution.

attributes.name: root_cause_category
attributes.description: Classification of the underlying root cause identified during case resolution (e.g., system_error, process_gap, product_defect, user_error, policy_exception, carrier_issue). Used for trend analysis and continuous improvement. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: system_error|process_gap|product_defect|user_error|policy_exception|carrier_issue|payment_failure|inventory_issue|data_quality — promote to reference product]

attributes.name: sentiment_score
attributes.description: Automated sentiment analysis score derived from customer communications (emails, chat transcripts) during the case lifecycle. Scale typically -1.00 (very negative) to +1.00 (very positive).
attributes.references: Natural Language Processing (NLP) Sentiment Analysis Standards

attributes.name: service_support_case_description
attributes.description: Detailed description of the customer issue, inquiry, or request as captured during case creation. May include customer-provided details, agent notes, and initial diagnostic information.

attributes.name: service_support_case_status
attributes.description: Current lifecycle status of the support case: new (just created), open (assigned), pending_customer (awaiting customer response), pending_internal (awaiting internal action), in_progress (actively being worked), escalated (moved to higher tier), resolved (solution provided), closed (finalized), cancelled (withdrawn). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: new|open|pending_customer|pending_internal|in_progress|escalated|resolved|closed|cancelled — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

attributes.name: sla_breach_flag
attributes.description: Boolean indicator of whether this case breached its SLA targets for first response time or resolution time. True indicates SLA was breached, False indicates SLA was met.

attributes.name: subject
attributes.description: Brief summary or subject line describing the customer issue or inquiry. Typically 100-200 characters.

products.name: case_interaction
products.description: Transactional record capturing every individual interaction or communication event within a support case lifecycle. Tracks inbound and outbound messages, call logs, chat transcripts, email threads, internal notes, and agent responses. Stores interaction channel, direction (inbound/outbound), agent ID, timestamp, content summary, attachment references, and interaction outcome. Enables full audit trail of case communications and supports agent performance measurement.

attributes.name: case_interaction_id
attributes.description: Unique identifier for the case interaction record. Primary key.

attributes.name: account_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to customer.account. Business justification: Interaction logs are aggregated by account to compute enterprise support metrics and SLA compliance per account.

attributes.name: channel_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to marketing.channel. Business justification: Channel-level interaction analytics (cost-per-interaction, handle time by channel) are standard e-commerce service KPIs. Each interaction occurs on a specific channel; `channel` plain string is a deno

attributes.name: contact_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to customer.contact. Business justification: Case interactions (calls, chats, emails) are conducted with a specific contact person in B2B e-commerce. Linking interaction to contact enables contact-level service history, communication preference

attributes.name: customer_profile_id
attributes.description: Identifier of the customer involved in this interaction. Links to customer master entity.

attributes.name: marketplace_transaction_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to marketplace.marketplace_transaction. Business justification: Interaction logs need the transaction context to surface correct communication history in support dashboards.

attributes.name: primary_case_agent_id
attributes.description: Identifier of the customer service agent who handled this interaction. Links to employee or agent entity.

attributes.name: support_case_id
attributes.description: Reference to the parent support case that this interaction belongs to. Links to the service case entity.

attributes.name: attachment_count
attributes.description: Number of file attachments associated with this interaction (screenshots, documents, logs).

attributes.name: attachment_references
attributes.description: Comma-separated list of attachment identifiers or URIs pointing to files stored in external document management system.

attributes.name: contact_method
attributes.description: The specific contact method used for this interaction (phone number, email address, chat handle, social media handle).

attributes.name: content_summary
attributes.description: Summarized content or transcript of the interaction, capturing key points discussed without full verbatim text.

attributes.name: country_code
attributes.description: Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code representing the country from which the interaction originated (e.g., USA, GBR, CAN).

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when this interaction record was first created in the system in the format yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.

attributes.name: csat_score
attributes.description: Customer Satisfaction (CSAT) score provided by the customer for this specific interaction, typically on a scale of 1-5 or 1-10.

attributes.name: direction
attributes.description: Direction of the interaction: inbound (customer-initiated) or outbound (agent-initiated).

attributes.name: duration_seconds
attributes.description: Total duration of the interaction in seconds, applicable for calls and chat sessions.

attributes.name: escalation_flag
attributes.description: Boolean flag indicating whether this interaction resulted in an escalation to a higher support tier or management (True/False).

attributes.name: escalation_reason
attributes.description: Reason or justification for escalating the interaction, if escalation occurred.

attributes.name: external_interaction_reference
attributes.description: Unique identifier for this interaction in the external source system, used for cross-system reconciliation and traceability.

attributes.name: feedback_comment
attributes.description: Free-text feedback or comment provided by the customer about this interaction.

attributes.name: full_transcript_reference
attributes.description: Reference pointer or URI to the full transcript or recording stored in external system (e.g., CDN (Content Delivery Network), document management system).

attributes.name: interaction_number
attributes.description: Human-readable business identifier for the interaction, typically formatted as INT- followed by numeric sequence.

attributes.name: interaction_sequence
attributes.description: Sequential ordering of this interaction within the parent case lifecycle, starting from 1.

attributes.name: interaction_status
attributes.description: Current status of the interaction: completed, pending follow-up, escalated to higher tier, abandoned by customer, or transferred to another agent.

attributes.name: interaction_timestamp
attributes.description: The exact date and time when the interaction event occurred in the format yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.

attributes.name: interaction_type
attributes.description: The type or category of interaction event: call, email, chat, SMS, social media, or web form submission.

attributes.name: internal_notes
attributes.description: Internal notes or comments added by the agent for internal reference, not visible to the customer.

attributes.name: language_code
attributes.description: Three-letter ISO 639-2 language code representing the language used in the interaction (e.g., ENG for English, SPA for Spanish).

attributes.name: modified_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when this interaction record was last modified or updated in the format yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.

attributes.name: nps_score
attributes.description: Net Promoter Score (NPS) provided by the customer for this interaction, typically on a scale of 0-10.

attributes.name: outcome
attributes.description: The outcome or result of the interaction: resolved, unresolved, escalated, referred to another department, information provided, or callback scheduled.

attributes.name: priority
attributes.description: Priority level assigned to this interaction: low, medium, high, or critical.
attributes.references: ITIL (Information Technology Infrastructure Library) Service Management

attributes.name: resolution_provided_flag
attributes.description: Boolean flag indicating whether a resolution or answer was provided during this interaction (True/False).

attributes.name: response_time_seconds
attributes.description: Time in seconds taken by the agent to provide the first substantive response after interaction initiation.

attributes.name: sentiment_score
attributes.description: Automated sentiment analysis score for the interaction, typically ranging from -1.00 (negative) to +1.00 (positive), derived from NLP (Natural Language Processing) analysis of interaction content.
attributes.references: AI/ML Best Practices for Customer Experience

attributes.name: sla_breach_reason
attributes.description: Reason or explanation for SLA (Service Level Agreement) breach, if the interaction did not meet SLA targets.

attributes.name: sla_compliance_flag
attributes.description: Boolean flag indicating whether this interaction met the defined SLA (Service Level Agreement) targets for response and resolution time (True/False).

attributes.name: source_system
attributes.description: Name or identifier of the source system that captured this interaction (e.g., CRM (Customer Relationship Management), call center platform, chat system).

attributes.name: subject
attributes.description: Brief subject line or title summarizing the purpose or topic of the interaction.

attributes.name: tags
attributes.description: Comma-separated list of tags or labels applied to categorize or classify the interaction (e.g., billing, technical, complaint, refund).

attributes.name: transfer_flag
attributes.description: Boolean flag indicating whether the interaction was transferred to another agent or department (True/False).

attributes.name: wait_time_seconds
attributes.description: Time in seconds the customer waited before the interaction was answered or acknowledged by an agent.

products.name: rma
products.description: Return Merchandise Authorization master record managing the end-to-end RMA lifecycle for e-commerce returns. Captures RMA number, originating order reference, return reason code, return type (refund, exchange, store credit), customer-declared condition, carrier return label details, expected return window, actual receipt date, inspection status, disposition decision (restock, liquidate, destroy), and financial settlement status. SSOT for all return authorization records across the platform.

attributes.name: rma_id
attributes.description: Unique identifier for the return merchandise authorization record. Primary key for the RMA entity.

attributes.name: account_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to customer.account. Business justification: RMA processing often needs the customer's account to validate credit limits and apply account‑level return policies.

attributes.name: accounts_receivable_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.accounts_receivable. Business justification: RMA approval triggers an AR credit memo against the original invoice. E-commerce returns processing requires linking the RMA to the AR record to apply credits, update aging buckets, and reconcile open

attributes.name: carrier_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to logistics.carrier. Business justification: Return processing reports require linking each RMA to the carrier handling the return; replaces the free‑text return_carrier field.

attributes.name: carrier_service_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to logistics.carrier_service. Business justification: Return RMA tracking includes the specific carrier service tier used, enabling cost and SLA analysis for returned shipments.

attributes.name: contact_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to customer.contact. Business justification: In B2B e-commerce, returns are authorized and coordinated by a specific contact (procurement or operations contact). Linking RMA to contact enables return authorization tracking, contact-level return

attributes.name: cost_center_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Return processing costs are allocated to cost centers for internal budgeting and expense tracking.

attributes.name: delivery_confirmation_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to order.delivery_confirmation. Business justification: RMA return window eligibility is calculated from confirmed delivery date; linking RMA to delivery_confirmation enables automated return eligibility validation, proof-of-delivery dispute resolution, an

attributes.name: fulfillment_order_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to fulfillment.fulfillment_order. Business justification: RMA processing requires the original fulfillment order to reconcile inventory, validate return eligibility, and generate accurate financial adjustments.

attributes.name: fulfillment_shipment_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to fulfillment.fulfillment_shipment. Business justification: Returns management requires validating the original shipment's actual delivery date to confirm return window eligibility and generate return labels. E-commerce ops teams link every RMA to its originat

attributes.name: journal_entry_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.journal_entry. Business justification: RMA processing generates journal entries for inventory write-backs, restocking fees, and refund liabilities. Linking rma to journal_entry enables financial impact tracking of returns, supports invento

attributes.name: marketplace_transaction_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to marketplace.marketplace_transaction. Business justification: RMAs are initiated against marketplace transactions in e-commerce. Seller settlement deduction, commission reversal, and dispute resolution all require tracing which marketplace transaction generated

attributes.name: profit_center_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Profitability of return handling is measured by linking each RMA to a profit center.

attributes.name: promotional_campaign_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to pricing.promotional_campaign. Business justification: Items purchased under a promotional campaign often have special return policies (e.g., final sale, reduced return window, non-refundable). Linking RMA to promotional_campaign enables return eligibilit

attributes.name: return_from_customer_address_id
attributes.business_glossary_term: Return From Customer Address Id (Foreign Key)
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to customer.customer_address. Business justification: Return label generation and carrier pickup scheduling require the customer's return-from address. The rma table has no address FK currently. Role-prefixed return_from_ distinguishes pickup address fro

attributes.name: seller_profile_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to seller.seller_profile. Business justification: Marketplace RMAs for seller-fulfilled orders must reference the responsible seller for return routing, seller-funded refund chargebacks, and violation tracking. Returns management and seller performan

attributes.name: tax_record_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.tax_record. Business justification: Product returns require tax reversals on the original transaction. E-commerce tax compliance (marketplace facilitator laws) requires RMAs to reference the originating tax record to process accurate ta

attributes.name: warehouse_node_id
attributes.description: Reference to the warehouse facility designated to receive and process the returned item. Used for routing and capacity planning.

attributes.name: approval_required
attributes.description: Flag indicating whether this RMA requires manual approval by customer service or management. Triggered by policy exceptions or high-value returns.

attributes.name: approved_by_user_code
attributes.description: Reference to the customer service representative or manager who approved the RMA request. Used for audit trail and performance tracking.

attributes.name: approved_date
attributes.description: Date when the RMA request was approved by the system or customer service. Marks the start of the return fulfillment process.

attributes.name: closed_date
attributes.description: Date when the RMA case was fully closed and all associated workflows completed. Marks the end of the return lifecycle for reporting purposes.

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when the RMA record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.

attributes.name: currency_code
attributes.description: Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary amounts in this RMA record. Ensures consistent financial reporting across multi-currency operations.

attributes.name: customer_declared_condition
attributes.description: Condition of the returned item as declared by the customer at the time of RMA request. Used for initial eligibility assessment and compared against inspection findings.

attributes.name: customer_service_notes
attributes.description: Free-text notes entered by customer service representatives regarding the RMA case. Captures context, customer interactions, and special handling instructions.

attributes.name: disposition_decision
attributes.description: Final decision on how the returned item will be handled post-inspection. Drives inventory allocation, financial write-offs, and reverse logistics.

attributes.name: expected_return_date
attributes.description: Estimated date by which the returned item is expected to arrive at the warehouse. Used for receiving planning and exception alerts.

attributes.name: inspected_condition
attributes.description: Actual condition of the returned item as determined by warehouse inspection team. Used to validate customer declaration and determine disposition.

attributes.name: inspection_completed_date
attributes.description: Date when the warehouse inspection of the returned item was completed. Triggers disposition decision and refund processing.

attributes.name: inspection_notes
attributes.description: Free-text notes entered by warehouse inspection team regarding the physical condition and findings of the returned item.

attributes.name: inspection_status
attributes.description: Current status of the physical inspection process for the returned item. Tracks warehouse receiving and quality control workflow.

attributes.name: is_within_policy
