attributes.description: Flag indicating whether the RMA request complies with the applicable return policy rules. Used for auto-approval workflows and exception handling.

attributes.name: label_issued_date
attributes.description: Date when the return shipping label was generated and provided to the customer. Used for tracking customer action and carrier pickup scheduling.

attributes.name: label_paid_by
attributes.description: Party responsible for paying the return shipping cost. Determines financial settlement and customer experience.

attributes.name: modified_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when the RMA record was last modified. Used for change tracking and data synchronization across systems.

attributes.name: received_date
attributes.description: Actual date when the returned item was physically received at the warehouse. Triggers inspection workflow and service level measurement.

attributes.name: refund_amount
attributes.description: Total monetary amount to be refunded to the customer in the transaction currency. May differ from original order amount due to restocking fees or partial returns.

attributes.name: refund_method
attributes.description: Method by which the refund will be issued to the customer. Drives payment processing workflow and customer communication.

attributes.name: refund_processed_date
attributes.description: Date when the refund transaction was successfully processed and funds were released to the customer. Used for financial reconciliation and customer satisfaction tracking.

attributes.name: refund_status
attributes.description: Current status of the refund transaction. Tracks financial settlement workflow and customer notification triggers.

attributes.name: requested_date
attributes.description: Date when the customer initiated the RMA request. Used for return window calculation and service level tracking.

attributes.name: restocking_fee
attributes.description: Fee charged to the customer for processing the return, deducted from the refund amount. Applied per return policy and product category.

attributes.name: return_label_cost
attributes.description: Cost of the return shipping label in the transaction currency. Used for return cost analysis and customer charge-back decisions.

attributes.name: return_reason_code
attributes.description: Standardized code indicating the primary reason for the return. Used for root cause analysis, product quality tracking, and return rate analytics. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: defective|wrong_item|not_as_described|damaged_in_transit|no_longer_needed|size_fit_issue|buyer_remorse|duplicate_order|late_delivery|other — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

attributes.name: return_tracking_number
attributes.description: Carrier-provided tracking number for the return shipment. Enables real-time visibility into return transit status.

attributes.name: return_type
attributes.description: Classification of the return resolution method requested by the customer or determined by policy. Drives downstream financial and fulfillment workflows.

attributes.name: return_window_days
attributes.description: Number of days from original order delivery date within which the return must be initiated per policy. Used for eligibility validation.

attributes.name: rma_status
attributes.description: Current lifecycle status of the RMA. Tracks the return authorization workflow from initial request through final disposition. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: pending_approval|approved|rejected|label_issued|in_transit|received|inspecting|completed|cancelled — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

products.name: rma_line
products.description: Line-item detail record for each SKU included in an RMA. Captures the specific product SKU, quantity requested for return, quantity actually received, unit return reason, item condition on receipt, restocking eligibility flag, refund amount per line, exchange SKU if applicable, and inspection notes. Supports multi-item returns under a single RMA authorization and enables SKU-level return analytics and inventory disposition.

attributes.name: rma_line_id
attributes.description: Unique identifier for the RMA line item. Primary key for the RMA line detail record.

attributes.name: bundle_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to product.bundle. Business justification: Bundle returns require component-level disposition rules and restocking eligibility checks defined on the bundle. Existing rma_line FKs cover catalog_item and sku but not bundle, leaving bundle return

attributes.name: catalog_item_id
attributes.description: Reference to the product master record for the item being returned. Links to the product catalog.

attributes.name: header_id
attributes.description: Reference to the new order created for the exchange shipment, if applicable. Links the return to the replacement order.

attributes.name: line_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to order.order_line. Business justification: Return eligibility validation, return_window_days enforcement, and unit_price reconciliation require tracing each RMA line back to the specific order line. E-commerce return processing and seller char

attributes.name: order_line_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to fulfillment.fulfillment_order_line. Business justification: Each RMA line must trace back to the original fulfillment order line for refund amount validation, inventory restocking disposition, and return fraud detection. E-commerce returns processing always re

attributes.name: price_list_item_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to pricing.price_list_item. Business justification: RMA line processing requires the original price_list_item to validate unit_price for refund computation, determine restocking fee eligibility based on pricing tier, and confirm return window policy. R

attributes.name: rma_id
attributes.description: Reference to the parent RMA authorization under which this line item is processed. Links this line to the RMA header.

attributes.name: sku_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to product.sku. Business justification: Needed for accurate inventory and refund calculations; RMA line must reference the SKU entity to determine restocking fees, unit price, and stock adjustments.

attributes.name: stock_position_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to inventory.stock_position. Business justification: After RMA inspection, disposition (restock, quarantine, scrap) maps to a specific stock_position. rma_line has restocking_eligible_flag and disposition_code but no FK to stock_position. Returns proces

attributes.name: warehouse_location_id
attributes.description: Reference to the specific warehouse location where the returned item was received and inspected. Supports multi-warehouse return processing.

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: The date and time when this RMA line record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail and lifecycle tracking.

attributes.name: currency_code
attributes.description: Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary amounts on this line. Ensures proper currency handling for international returns.

attributes.name: disposition_code
attributes.description: The final disposition decision for the returned item. Determines how the item will be handled after inspection and processing.

attributes.name: exchange_requested_flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether the customer has requested an exchange rather than a refund for this item. True if exchange requested, false if refund only.

attributes.name: exchange_sku
attributes.description: The SKU of the replacement product requested by the customer if this is an exchange. Null if this is a refund-only return.

attributes.name: inspected_by_user_code
attributes.description: Reference to the warehouse staff member who performed the inspection of this returned item. Supports quality control and accountability.

attributes.name: inspected_date
attributes.description: The date the returned item completed quality inspection. Marks the completion of the inspection process and readiness for disposition.

attributes.name: inspection_notes
attributes.description: Detailed notes recorded by warehouse inspection staff regarding the condition, completeness, and quality of the returned item. Supports disposition decisions.

attributes.name: item_condition_code
attributes.description: Standardized assessment of the physical condition of the returned item upon receipt at the warehouse. Determines restocking eligibility and disposition.

attributes.name: line_status
attributes.description: Current processing status of this RMA line item. Tracks the line through the return workflow from initial request to final disposition. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: PENDING_RECEIPT|RECEIVED|INSPECTING|APPROVED|REJECTED|REFUNDED|EXCHANGED|CLOSED — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

attributes.name: line_subtotal
attributes.description: The total amount for this line before taxes and fees, calculated as unit price multiplied by quantity approved. Represents the base refund amount.

attributes.name: line_tax_amount
attributes.description: The tax amount to be refunded for this line item. Calculated based on the approved quantity and applicable tax rates.

attributes.name: quantity_approved
attributes.description: The number of units approved for refund or exchange after inspection. May be less than quantity received if some units fail inspection criteria.

attributes.name: quantity_received
attributes.description: The actual number of units physically received back from the customer for this SKU. May differ from quantity requested if customer sends partial return.

attributes.name: quantity_requested
attributes.description: The number of units the customer has requested to return for this SKU. Represents the customer's initial return request quantity.

attributes.name: received_date
attributes.description: The date the returned item was physically received at the warehouse. Used for Service Level Agreement (SLA) tracking and refund processing timelines.

attributes.name: refund_amount
attributes.description: The total refund amount for this line item including subtotal, tax, minus any restocking fees. Represents the actual amount to be credited to the customer.

attributes.name: refund_processed_date
attributes.description: The date the refund for this line item was processed and initiated to the customer's payment method. Marks completion of the financial return transaction.

attributes.name: rejection_reason
attributes.description: Explanation for why this line item was rejected for refund or exchange, if applicable. Provides transparency to customer and supports dispute resolution.

attributes.name: restocking_eligible_flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether the returned item is in suitable condition to be restocked and resold as new inventory. True if eligible for resale, false if must be liquidated or disposed.

attributes.name: restocking_fee_amount
attributes.description: The restocking fee charged to the customer for this line item, if applicable. Typically applied for non-defective returns or opened items per return policy.

attributes.name: return_reason_code
attributes.description: Standardized code indicating why the customer is returning this specific item. Used for return analytics and quality improvement.

attributes.name: return_reason_description
attributes.description: Detailed free-text explanation provided by the customer or service agent describing the specific reason for returning this item.

attributes.name: serial_number
attributes.description: The unique serial number of the specific unit being returned, if applicable for serialized products. Enables unit-level tracking and warranty validation.

attributes.name: unit_price
attributes.description: The original unit price paid by the customer for this SKU at the time of purchase. Used to calculate refund amounts.

attributes.name: updated_timestamp
attributes.description: The date and time when this RMA line record was last modified. Tracks the most recent change to any field in the record.

products.name: service_refund
products.description: Transactional record representing all customer remediation, refunds, and compensation issued through the service domain. Covers full refunds, partial refunds, store credits, gift cards, coupon codes, free shipping vouchers, loyalty point grants, and goodwill compensation for service failures or proactive retention gestures. Tracks remediation type (refund, exchange credit, compensation, goodwill gesture, retention offer), amount/value, currency, method (original payment method, store credit, gift card), status lifecycle (pending, approved, processing, completed, failed, redeemed, expired), initiating RMA or case reference, approval workflow with authorization thresholds by amount tier, issuing agent, manager override flag, redemption status, expiry date, compensation reason category, and settlement confirmation reference. Owns the service-side authorization decision while payment domain owns financial execution. SSOT for all service-originated customer remediation and compensation records.

attributes.name: service_refund_id
attributes.description: Unique identifier for the service refund record. Primary key for the service refund entity.

attributes.name: account_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to customer.account. Business justification: Refunds must be associated with the billing account for financial reconciliation and audit reporting.

attributes.name: accounts_receivable_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.accounts_receivable. Business justification: Refund issuance directly reduces or offsets an AR balance. E-commerce financial reconciliation requires linking each service refund to the originating AR record to apply credit memos, update open amou

attributes.name: coupon_redemption_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to pricing.coupon_redemption. Business justification: When a refund reverses a coupon discount, the service agent must reference the specific coupon_redemption record to determine the discount amount to reverse and update redemption status. Coupon refund

attributes.name: general_ledger_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.general_ledger. Business justification: Refund transactions must be posted to the general ledger for financial reconciliation and reporting.

attributes.name: header_id
attributes.description: Reference to the original order associated with this refund. Links the refund to the transaction being remediated.

attributes.name: journal_entry_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.journal_entry. Business justification: Every refund disbursement generates a journal entry (debit revenue/liability, credit cash). Linking service_refund to journal_entry enables financial audit trail, SOX compliance, and period-close reco

attributes.name: legal_entity_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.legal_entity. Business justification: In multi-entity e-commerce platforms, refunds must be processed and reported under the correct legal entity for regulatory compliance, VAT/GST reporting, and intercompany settlement. Domain experts ex

attributes.name: line_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to order.order_line. Business justification: Partial refunds on specific line items require direct line-level linkage for financial reconciliation, tax recalculation, and seller payout adjustments. Traversing via rma_id is insufficient for goodw

attributes.name: logistics_shipment_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to logistics.logistics_shipment. Business justification: Refunds issued for shipping failures (lost, damaged, late delivery) must reference the logistics_shipment to support carrier chargeback processing and cost-recovery workflows. service_refund has heade

attributes.name: loyalty_enrollment_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to customer.loyalty_enrollment. Business justification: service_refund.loyalty_points_granted requires knowing which loyalty enrollment to credit. Direct FK to loyalty_enrollment enables automated point posting for refund compensation — a standard loyalty

attributes.name: marketplace_transaction_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to marketplace.marketplace_transaction. Business justification: Refunds are processed against the original marketplace transaction; linking provides auditability and financial reconciliation across systems.

attributes.name: payment_method_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to payment.payment_method. Business justification: Enables audit of refunds by payment method, supporting compliance reports and method‑level performance metrics.

attributes.name: price_list_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to pricing.price_list. Business justification: Needed for Refund Pricing Source Audit, tracking which price list was used for the original sale to ensure correct refund calculations and compliance.

attributes.name: price_list_item_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to pricing.price_list_item. Business justification: Refund calculation validation requires referencing the exact price_list_item applied to the original sale to confirm the unit price, tier pricing, and discount percent used. E-commerce refund audits a

attributes.name: price_override_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to pricing.price_override. Business justification: Refunds on orders with price overrides must reference the override record to validate the overridden price used and ensure the refund amount matches the actual amount charged. Price override audit tra

attributes.name: primary_service_issuing_agent_id
attributes.description: Reference to the customer service agent who initiated the refund request. Links to the employee or agent record.

attributes.name: rma_id
attributes.description: Reference to the RMA that initiated this refund or compensation. Links the refund to the originating return case.

attributes.name: seller_profile_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to seller.seller_profile. Business justification: Marketplace refunds are often charged back to the responsible seller (seller-funded refunds). Finance and seller operations teams require seller attribution on refund records for chargeback processing

attributes.name: seller_settlement_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to marketplace.seller_settlement. Business justification: Refunds issued on marketplace transactions must be deducted from the seller's settlement disbursement. Finance and marketplace ops require this link for seller payout reconciliation, net disbursement

attributes.name: settlement_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to payment.settlement. Business justification: Finance teams require direct linkage between service-initiated refunds and payment settlement batches for period-end reconciliation and GL posting. settlement_reference_number is a plain-text denormal

attributes.name: support_case_id
attributes.description: Reference to the customer service case that authorized this refund or compensation. Links to the support ticket or inquiry.

attributes.name: tax_record_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.tax_record. Business justification: Refunds require tax reversals or credit adjustments against the original tax record. E-commerce marketplace facilitator tax compliance mandates linking refund transactions to their originating tax rec

attributes.name: amount
attributes.description: Monetary value of the refund or compensation issued to the customer. Represents the gross amount before any adjustments.

attributes.name: approval_status
attributes.description: Authorization state of the refund request. Indicates whether the refund has been approved and by what authority level.

attributes.name: approval_tier
attributes.description: Authorization level required for this refund based on amount thresholds. Defines the approval hierarchy tier.

attributes.name: approved_timestamp
attributes.description: Date and time when the refund was authorized by the agent or manager. Null if still pending approval or if rejected.

attributes.name: completed_timestamp
attributes.description: Date and time when the refund was successfully settled and funds were returned to the customer. Marks the end of the refund lifecycle.

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: Date and time when this refund record was first created in the system. Audit field for data lineage and compliance.

attributes.name: currency_code
attributes.description: Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the refund amount. Defines the monetary unit of the refund value.

attributes.name: customer_satisfaction_impact
attributes.description: Assessed impact of the refund on customer satisfaction based on follow-up surveys or agent assessment. Used for service quality tracking.

attributes.name: expiry_date
attributes.description: Date after which the compensation instrument (store credit, gift card, coupon) can no longer be redeemed. Null for direct refunds with no expiration.

attributes.name: is_goodwill_gesture
attributes.description: Indicates whether the refund was issued as a proactive goodwill gesture rather than in response to a specific complaint or return. True for retention offers.

attributes.name: is_retention_offer
attributes.description: Indicates whether the compensation was issued as part of a customer retention strategy to prevent churn. True for proactive retention gestures.

attributes.name: last_modified_timestamp
attributes.description: Date and time when this refund record was most recently updated. Audit field for change tracking and data quality monitoring.

attributes.name: loyalty_points_granted
attributes.description: Number of loyalty program points awarded as compensation. Zero if compensation was not in the form of loyalty points.

attributes.name: manager_override_flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether the refund required manager override of standard policy or amount thresholds. True if exception was granted.

attributes.name: net_refund_cost
attributes.description: Total cost to the business including refund amount and processing fees. Used for financial impact analysis and reporting.

attributes.name: notes
attributes.description: Additional free-text notes or comments from service agents regarding the refund. Used for context and audit trail documentation.

attributes.name: nps_survey_response
attributes.description: Customer NPS score (0-10) collected after refund resolution. Null if no survey response was received. Used for service quality measurement.

attributes.name: original_payment_method_type
attributes.description: Type of payment method used in the original order. Used to determine refund routing and processing requirements. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: credit_card|debit_card|paypal|bank_transfer|gift_card|store_credit|cash_on_delivery|buy_now_pay_later — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

attributes.name: override_justification
attributes.description: Explanation provided by the approving manager for policy override or exception. Required when manager_override_flag is true.

attributes.name: processed_timestamp
attributes.description: Date and time when the refund was submitted to the payment system for execution. Marks handoff from service to payment domain.

attributes.name: processing_fee
attributes.description: Fee charged by payment processor or financial institution for processing the refund transaction. Represents cost to business.

attributes.name: reason_category
attributes.description: High-level categorization of why the refund or compensation was issued. Used for root cause analysis and service improvement. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: defective_product|wrong_item_shipped|damaged_in_transit|late_delivery|service_failure|customer_dissatisfaction|pricing_error|goodwill_gesture|retention_offer|policy_exception — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

attributes.name: reason_detail
attributes.description: Detailed explanation of the specific reason for issuing the refund or compensation. Free-text field for agent notes.

attributes.name: redemption_code
attributes.description: Unique code issued for store credit, gift card, or coupon compensation. Used by customer to claim the value. Confidential business data.

attributes.name: redemption_status
attributes.description: Tracks whether non-monetary compensation (store credit, gift card, coupon) has been used by the customer. Not applicable for direct refunds.

attributes.name: redemption_value_remaining
attributes.description: Remaining unredeemed value for partial-use compensation instruments like store credit or gift cards. Null for one-time refunds.

attributes.name: refund_method
attributes.description: Method by which the refund or compensation is delivered to the customer. Defines the channel for value return. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: original_payment_method|store_credit|gift_card|bank_transfer|check|loyalty_account|promotional_code — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

attributes.name: refund_number
attributes.description: Externally visible unique business identifier for the refund. Used in customer communications and tracking.

attributes.name: remediation_type
attributes.description: Category of customer remediation issued. Defines the form of compensation provided to the customer. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: full_refund|partial_refund|store_credit|gift_card|coupon_code|free_shipping_voucher|loyalty_points|goodwill_compensation — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

attributes.name: requested_timestamp
attributes.description: Date and time when the refund was initially requested by the customer or initiated by the service agent. Marks the start of the refund lifecycle.

attributes.name: service_refund_status
attributes.description: Current lifecycle state of the refund. Tracks the refund through authorization, processing, and completion stages. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: pending|approved|processing|completed|failed|redeemed|expired|cancelled — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

attributes.name: source_system
attributes.description: Originating system or channel where the refund request was initiated. Used for data lineage and integration tracking. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: crm|oms|rma_system|service_portal|call_center|chat_system|email_system — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

products.name: agent
products.description: Master record for customer service agents and support staff operating within the service domain. Captures agent name, employee ID reference, team assignment, skill set tags (returns, billing, technical, escalations), supported channels, language proficiencies, current availability status, shift schedule reference, performance tier, and active/inactive status. Serves as the SSOT for agent identity within the service domain and supports case routing, workload balancing, and performance measurement.

attributes.name: agent_id
attributes.description: Unique identifier for the customer service agent. Primary key for the agent entity.

attributes.name: supervisor_agent_id
attributes.description: FK to service.agent

attributes.name: active_status
attributes.description: Current employment and operational status of the agent. Determines whether the agent is eligible for case assignment.

attributes.name: availability_status
attributes.description: Current real-time availability status of the agent. Used by the case routing engine to determine if the agent can accept new cases.

attributes.name: average_handle_time_minutes
attributes.description: Rolling average time in minutes the agent takes to resolve a case from assignment to closure. Key performance indicator for agent efficiency.

attributes.name: certification_expiry_date
attributes.description: Date when the agents current service certification expires and requires renewal. Null if certification does not expire.

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when the agent record was first created in the service domain system. Audit field for data lineage.

attributes.name: customer_satisfaction_score
attributes.description: Average customer satisfaction score for cases handled by this agent, typically on a 1-5 scale. Derived from post-interaction surveys.

attributes.name: email_address
attributes.description: Corporate email address for the agent. Used for internal communication and system notifications.

attributes.name: employee_code
attributes.description: External employee identifier from the HR system. Links the agent to their employee record in the Enterprise Resource Planning (ERP) system.

attributes.name: escalation_rate
attributes.description: Percentage of cases handled by the agent that required escalation to a supervisor or specialist. Lower rates indicate higher agent competency.

attributes.name: first_contact_resolution_rate
attributes.description: Percentage of cases resolved by the agent on first contact without escalation or follow-up. Key quality metric for agent performance.

attributes.name: full_name
attributes.description: Full legal name of the customer service agent as recorded in the HR system.

attributes.name: hire_date
attributes.description: Date the agent was hired into the customer service organization. Used for tenure analysis and performance benchmarking.

attributes.name: language_proficiencies
attributes.description: Comma-separated list of languages the agent can support with proficiency level (e.g., EN-native, ES-fluent, FR-conversational). Enables multilingual customer support routing.

attributes.name: last_login_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp of the agents most recent login to the customer service platform. Used for activity monitoring and availability tracking.

attributes.name: max_concurrent_cases
attributes.description: Maximum number of cases the agent can handle simultaneously. Used by the workload balancing engine to prevent agent overload.

attributes.name: net_promoter_score
attributes.description: Average Net Promoter Score (NPS) for interactions handled by this agent. Measures customer loyalty and likelihood to recommend based on agent service.

attributes.name: performance_tier
attributes.description: Agent performance classification tier based on experience, skill level, and performance metrics. Used for case complexity routing and escalation paths.

attributes.name: phone_extension
attributes.description: Internal phone extension number for the agent within the contact center phone system.

attributes.name: quality_assurance_score
attributes.description: Average quality assurance audit score for the agent based on case review and compliance checks. Typically scored 0-100 by QA team.

attributes.name: shift_schedule_reference
attributes.description: Reference identifier to the agents current shift schedule or roster. Links to workforce management system for capacity planning.

attributes.name: skill_set_tags
attributes.description: Comma-separated list of agent competencies and specializations (e.g., returns, billing, technical, escalations, fraud, international). Used for intelligent case routing and workload balancing.

attributes.name: sla_compliance_rate
attributes.business_glossary_term: Service Level Agreement (SLA) Compliance Rate Percentage
attributes.description: Percentage of cases where the agent met or exceeded the defined Service Level Agreement (SLA) response and resolution time targets.

attributes.name: supported_channels
attributes.description: Comma-separated list of customer service channels the agent is trained and authorized to handle (e.g., phone, email, chat, social media, SMS).

attributes.name: team_assignment
attributes.description: Name or identifier of the service team to which the agent is assigned (e.g., Returns Team, Billing Support, Technical Support, Escalations).

attributes.name: termination_date
attributes.description: Date the agent left the organization or was terminated. Null for active agents. Used for historical case ownership tracking.

attributes.name: total_cases_handled
attributes.description: Cumulative count of all cases assigned to and handled by the agent since hire date. Used for productivity and experience analysis.

attributes.name: training_completion_date
attributes.description: Date the agent completed initial onboarding and training program and was certified to handle customer cases independently.

attributes.name: updated_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when the agent record was last modified. Audit field for change tracking and data quality monitoring.

products.name: team
products.description: Master record defining customer service teams and organizational groupings within the support operation. Captures team name, team type (tier-1 frontline, tier-2 escalation, specialist, back-office), supported contact channels, operating hours, geographic coverage, team lead reference, SLA tier assignment, and capacity targets. Enables hierarchical case routing, team-level SLA tracking, and workforce planning for the service organization.

attributes.name: team_id
attributes.description: Unique identifier for the customer service team. Primary key.

attributes.name: agent_id
attributes.description: Reference to the employee or manager who leads this team. Used for escalation paths and team performance accountability.

attributes.name: cost_center_id
attributes.description: Reference to the finance cost center to which this team's expenses are allocated. Enables financial reporting and budget tracking.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA Finance - Cost Center Accounting

attributes.name: parent_team_id
attributes.description: Reference to the parent team in a hierarchical team structure. Null for top-level teams. Enables organizational hierarchy and escalation path modeling.

attributes.name: primary_escalation_team_id
attributes.description: Reference to the team to which cases are escalated when this team cannot resolve them. Null if this is the highest escalation tier. Enables escalation path configuration.
attributes.references: ITIL Service Operation - Incident Management - Escalation

attributes.name: profit_center_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Service teams are P&L-accountable units in e-commerce operations. Linking team to profit_center enables revenue and cost attribution per service team for management reporting, budgeting, and profitabi

attributes.name: capacity_fte
attributes.description: Total team capacity expressed in Full-Time Equivalent (FTE) headcount. Used for workforce planning, capacity forecasting, and case load balancing.

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when the team record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage.

attributes.name: csat_target
attributes.description: Target Customer Satisfaction (CSAT) score for the team, expressed as a percentage (0-100). Key performance indicator (KPI) for customer experience quality.

attributes.name: effective_date
attributes.description: Date when the team became operational and started accepting cases. Used for team lifecycle tracking and historical reporting.

attributes.name: email_address
attributes.description: Primary email address for the team, used for case assignment notifications and internal communication. Organizational contact data, classified as confidential.

attributes.name: geographic_coverage
attributes.description: Geographic region or market this team serves, using 3-letter ISO country codes or region identifiers (e.g., USA, CAN, GBR, EUR, APAC, GLOBAL). Supports geo-based routing and regional SLA (Service Level Agreement) management.

attributes.name: is_remote
attributes.description: Indicates whether the team operates remotely (distributed workforce) or from a centralized physical location. True if remote, False if on-site.

attributes.name: language_support
attributes.description: Comma-separated list of languages the team can support using ISO 639-1 two-letter codes (e.g., en, es, fr, de, zh, ja). Enables language-based case routing.

attributes.name: max_concurrent_cases
attributes.description: Maximum number of cases the team can handle concurrently based on capacity and workload models. Used for case routing throttling and queue management.

attributes.name: modified_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when the team record was last modified. Used for audit trail and change tracking.

attributes.name: notes
attributes.description: Free-text field for additional notes, comments, or context about the team (e.g., special operating procedures, temporary capacity changes, pilot program details).

attributes.name: nps_target
attributes.description: Target Net Promoter Score (NPS) for the team, ranging from -100 to +100. Measures customer loyalty and likelihood to recommend based on team interactions.

attributes.name: operates_holidays
attributes.description: Indicates whether the team operates on public holidays. True if holiday coverage is provided, False if holidays are non-operational.

attributes.name: operates_weekends
attributes.description: Indicates whether the team operates on weekends (Saturday and Sunday). True if weekend coverage is provided, False if weekends are non-operational.

attributes.name: operating_hours_end
attributes.description: Daily end time of the team's operating hours in HH:mm format (24-hour clock, team's local timezone). Used for case routing and availability scheduling.

attributes.name: operating_hours_start
attributes.description: Daily start time of the team's operating hours in HH:mm format (24-hour clock, team's local timezone). Used for case routing and availability scheduling.

attributes.name: phone_number
attributes.description: Primary phone number for the team, used for internal escalations and coordination. Organizational contact data, classified as confidential.

attributes.name: physical_location
attributes.description: Physical office or site location where the team is based (e.g., New York HQ, Manila Contact Center, Remote). Used for workforce planning and facility management.

attributes.name: primary_function
attributes.description: Brief description of the team's primary business function and scope of responsibility (e.g., Order inquiries and tracking, Returns and refunds processing, Account and billing support, Technical troubleshooting).

attributes.name: quality_score_target
attributes.description: Target quality assurance score for the team, expressed as a percentage (0-100). Represents the expected standard for case handling quality, monitored through QA audits.
attributes.references: ITIL Continual Service Improvement - Quality Management

attributes.name: sla_tier
attributes.description: The SLA tier this team is assigned to deliver: standard (baseline service levels), priority (faster response for high-value segments), premium (enhanced service commitments), vip (white-glove service for top customers). Drives case routing and performance targets.
attributes.references: ITIL Service Design - Service Level Management

attributes.name: specialization_tags
attributes.description: Comma-separated list of specialization keywords or skill tags that describe the team's expertise (e.g., rma, fraud_detection, vip_service, technical_support, billing_disputes). Used for intelligent case routing based on issue type.

attributes.name: target_first_response_minutes
attributes.description: Target time in minutes for the team to provide the first response to a customer inquiry, as defined by the team's SLA tier. Key performance indicator (KPI) for responsiveness.

attributes.name: target_resolution_hours
attributes.description: Target time in hours for the team to resolve a case, as defined by the team's SLA tier. Key performance indicator (KPI) for case closure efficiency.

attributes.name: team_code
attributes.description: Short alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the team for operational systems and reporting (e.g., T1-FRONT, T2-ESC, RMA-SPEC).

attributes.name: team_name
attributes.description: Human-readable name of the customer service team (e.g., Tier-1 Frontline Support, Returns Specialist Team, VIP Customer Care).

attributes.name: team_status
attributes.description: Current operational status of the team: active (currently operational and accepting cases), inactive (temporarily not operational), suspended (on hold pending review), dissolved (permanently closed).

attributes.name: team_type
attributes.description: Classification of the team based on its role in the service organization: tier_1_frontline (first contact resolution), tier_2_escalation (complex issue handling), specialist (domain-specific expertise such as RMA or fraud), back_office (non-customer-facing support), quality_assurance (monitoring and coaching), training (onboarding and skill development).
attributes.references: ITIL Service Operation - Support Team Classification

attributes.name: termination_date
attributes.description: Date when the team was dissolved or permanently closed. Null for active teams. Used for team lifecycle tracking and historical analysis.

attributes.name: timezone
attributes.description: IANA timezone identifier for the team's operating location (e.g., America/New_York, Europe/London, Asia/Tokyo). Used to calculate local operating hours and SLA deadlines.

products.name: sla_policy
products.description: Reference master defining SLA (Service Level Agreement) policies governing response and resolution time targets for customer support cases. Captures SLA policy name, applicable case type and priority matrix, first response time target (hours), resolution time target (hours), escalation trigger thresholds, business hours vs. calendar hours flag, breach penalty rules, and effective date range. Provides the authoritative SLA configuration used to evaluate case compliance and drive automated escalations.

attributes.name: sla_policy_id
attributes.description: Unique identifier for the SLA policy record. Primary key.

attributes.name: channel_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to marketing.channel. Business justification: SLA policies in e-commerce are channel-specific (social media: 1-hour response; email: 24-hour). Linking sla_policy to marketing.channel enables proper policy lookup by channel and channel-level SLA c

attributes.name: cost_center_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: SLA policies carry breach_penalty_amount and breach_penalty_currency. Linking to cost_center enables allocation of SLA breach penalty costs to the responsible cost center, supporting operational cost

attributes.name: segment_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to customer.segment. Business justification: sla_policy.customer_segment is a denormalized text field. SLA policies are defined per customer segment (VIP, B2B, standard). Proper FK to segment enables automated SLA assignment during case creation

attributes.name: superseded_by_policy_sla_policy_id
attributes.description: Reference to the newer SLA policy that replaces this one. Nullable for current active policies. Creates version chain for policy evolution tracking.

attributes.name: approval_status
attributes.description: Approval status of the SLA policy. Policies must be approved before becoming active. Supports governance and change control processes.

attributes.name: approved_by_user_code
attributes.description: Identifier of the user who approved this SLA policy. Typically a senior operations leader or executive. Nullable for unapproved policies.

attributes.name: approved_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when this SLA policy was approved. Nullable for unapproved policies. Supports audit trail and compliance reporting.

attributes.name: auto_escalation_enabled
attributes.description: Boolean flag indicating whether automatic escalation is enabled when escalation_threshold_hours is reached. True enables automated escalation workflow, False requires manual escalation.

attributes.name: breach_penalty_amount
attributes.description: Monetary amount of penalty or credit applied when SLA is breached. Applicable when breach_penalty_type is financial or credit. Stored in base currency.

attributes.name: breach_penalty_currency
attributes.description: Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the breach penalty amount (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).

attributes.name: breach_penalty_type
attributes.description: Type of penalty or action triggered when SLA is breached. Options include no penalty, financial penalty, customer credit, automatic escalation, or notification to management.

attributes.name: business_days
attributes.description: Comma-separated list of business days when SLA is active (e.g., 'Monday,Tuesday,Wednesday,Thursday,Friday'). Used when time_calculation_method is business_hours to exclude weekends or specific days.

attributes.name: business_hours_end
attributes.description: End time of business hours in HH:MM format (24-hour clock) when time_calculation_method is business_hours. Used to calculate SLA compliance during operational hours only.

attributes.name: business_hours_start
attributes.description: Start time of business hours in HH:MM format (24-hour clock) when time_calculation_method is business_hours. Used to calculate SLA compliance during operational hours only.

attributes.name: case_priority
attributes.description: Priority level of cases this SLA policy applies to. Used in conjunction with case_type to form the SLA matrix (e.g., High priority order inquiries have different SLA targets than Low priority inquiries).

attributes.name: case_type
attributes.description: Type of customer service case this SLA policy applies to (e.g., 'Order Inquiry', 'Product Return', 'Technical Issue', 'Billing Dispute', 'Account Management'). Defines the scope of cases governed by this policy.

attributes.name: compliance_required
attributes.description: Boolean flag indicating whether this SLA policy is driven by regulatory compliance requirements (e.g., GDPR data subject request response times, financial services complaint handling). True indicates regulatory mandate.

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when this SLA policy record was first created in the system. Part of standard audit trail.

attributes.name: effective_end_date
attributes.description: Date when this SLA policy expires or is superseded by a new version. Nullable for currently active policies with no planned end date.

attributes.name: effective_start_date
attributes.description: Date when this SLA policy becomes active and begins governing case handling. Part of the effective date range for policy versioning.

attributes.name: escalation_level
attributes.description: Numeric escalation tier this SLA policy triggers (e.g., 1 = supervisor, 2 = manager, 3 = director). Defines the organizational level to which cases are escalated when thresholds are breached.

attributes.name: escalation_threshold_hours
attributes.description: Time threshold in hours that triggers automatic escalation if the case is not resolved. Typically set before the resolution target to allow time for escalated handling.

attributes.name: exclusions
attributes.description: Description of cases or conditions excluded from this SLA policy (e.g., 'Excludes cases opened during system maintenance windows', 'Does not apply to third-party vendor issues'). Defines policy boundaries.

attributes.name: first_response_target_hours
attributes.description: Target time in hours for the first response to a customer case under this SLA policy. Represents the maximum time allowed before initial acknowledgment or response to the customer.

attributes.name: geographic_region
attributes.description: Geographic region or market this SLA policy applies to (e.g., 'North America', 'EMEA', 'APAC'). Supports region-specific service level commitments.

attributes.name: is_default_policy
attributes.description: Boolean flag indicating whether this is the default SLA policy applied when no specific policy matches a case. Only one policy per case_type should be marked as default.

attributes.name: modified_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when this SLA policy record was last updated. Part of standard audit trail for change tracking.

attributes.name: notification_enabled
attributes.description: Boolean flag indicating whether automated notifications are sent when SLA thresholds are approaching or breached. True enables notification workflow.

attributes.name: notification_threshold_percent
attributes.description: Percentage of SLA target time elapsed that triggers a warning notification (e.g., 80 means notify when 80% of resolution_target_hours has passed). Used for proactive SLA management.

attributes.name: owner_user_code
attributes.description: Identifier of the business user or manager responsible for defining and maintaining this SLA policy. Typically a customer service director or operations manager.

attributes.name: policy_code
attributes.description: Unique business identifier code for the SLA policy used in operational systems and reporting.

attributes.name: policy_name
attributes.description: Human-readable name of the SLA policy (e.g., 'Premium Support SLA', 'Standard Response Policy').

attributes.name: policy_type
attributes.description: Classification of the SLA policy indicating the service area it governs (customer support, technical support, returns processing, escalation handling, VIP service, standard service).

attributes.name: regulatory_framework
attributes.description: Name of the regulatory framework or law that mandates this SLA policy (e.g., 'GDPR', 'CCPA', 'FTC Consumer Protection', 'PCI DSS'). Populated when compliance_required is True.

attributes.name: resolution_target_hours
attributes.description: Target time in hours for full resolution of a customer case under this SLA policy. Represents the maximum time allowed to completely resolve and close the case.

attributes.name: sla_policy_description
attributes.description: Detailed description of the SLA policy including its purpose, scope, and any special conditions or exceptions. Provides context for policy application and interpretation.

attributes.name: sla_policy_status
attributes.description: Current lifecycle status of the SLA policy indicating whether it is actively enforced, inactive, in draft state, archived, or temporarily suspended.

attributes.name: time_calculation_method
attributes.description: Method used to calculate SLA time targets. Business hours counts only operational hours (e.g., 9am-5pm weekdays), while calendar hours counts all hours including nights and weekends.

attributes.name: version_number
attributes.description: Version number of this SLA policy. Incremented when policy terms are updated. Supports policy change tracking and historical analysis.

products.name: escalation
products.description: Transactional record tracking case escalation events within the support workflow. Captures the originating case reference, escalation trigger reason (SLA breach, customer demand, complexity, VIP customer), escalation tier (tier-1 to tier-2, tier-2 to specialist, agent to supervisor), escalating agent, receiving agent or team, escalation timestamp, resolution at escalated tier flag, and time-to-resolution post-escalation. Supports escalation pattern analysis and service quality improvement.

attributes.name: escalation_id
attributes.description: Unique identifier for the escalation event. Primary key.

attributes.name: channel_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to marketing.channel. Business justification: Channel-level escalation rate reporting is a core e-commerce service metric; escalations may occur on a different channel than the originating case. `channel` plain string is a denormalization of mark

attributes.name: contact_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to customer.contact. Business justification: Escalations in B2B e-commerce are initiated by or directed to a specific contact. Contact-level escalation history drives SLA accountability reporting, executive escalation routing, and contact satisf

attributes.name: dispute_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to marketplace.dispute. Business justification: Marketplace disputes that exceed standard resolution thresholds trigger service escalations. Escalation management and SLA compliance reporting require direct traceability from the escalation record t

attributes.name: escalation_escalating_agent_id
attributes.description: Reference to the support agent who initiated or triggered the escalation.

attributes.name: fulfillment_shipment_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to fulfillment.fulfillment_shipment. Business justification: Escalations triggered by shipment failures (SLA breach, lost package, repeated delivery failures) require direct reference to the shipment for carrier chargeback filing and root-cause analysis. Escala

attributes.name: header_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to order.header. Business justification: Escalations triggered by order fulfillment failures (late delivery, wrong item, fraud) require direct order linkage for SLA breach root-cause analysis, seller performance scorecards, and customer comp

attributes.name: receiving_agent_id
attributes.description: Reference to the support agent who received and accepted the escalated case.

attributes.name: receiving_team_id
attributes.description: Reference to the support team or queue to which the case was escalated.

attributes.name: segment_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to customer.segment. Business justification: escalation.customer_segment is a denormalized text field. Proper FK to segment enables segment-level escalation rate reporting, SLA breach analysis by customer tier, and escalation routing rules by se

attributes.name: seller_profile_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to seller.seller_profile. Business justification: Marketplace escalations are frequently triggered by seller behavior (late shipment, item not as described, policy violations). Seller performance management, scorecard updates, and violation workflows

attributes.name: sla_policy_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to service.sla_policy. Business justification: escalation tracks sla_breach_flag and sla_target_resolution_minutes but has no FK to the governing SLA policy. Adding sla_policy_id → service.sla_policy.sla_policy_id directly links each escalation ev

attributes.name: support_case_id
attributes.description: Reference to the originating support case that was escalated.

attributes.name: compensation_amount
attributes.description: Monetary value of compensation issued to the customer as part of escalation resolution.

attributes.name: compensation_issued_flag
attributes.description: Boolean indicator of whether customer compensation (refund, credit, discount, gift) was issued as part of escalation resolution (True) or not (False).

attributes.name: compensation_type
attributes.description: The type of compensation provided to the customer (e.g., refund, account credit, discount code, gift card, free shipping, product replacement).

attributes.name: cost
attributes.description: Estimated or actual cost associated with handling the escalation, including labor, compensation, and operational overhead.

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: The date and time when the escalation record was first created in the system, formatted as yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.

attributes.name: customer_satisfaction_score
attributes.description: Post-resolution customer satisfaction score for the escalated case, typically on a scale of 1-5 or 1-10.

attributes.name: escalation_number
attributes.description: Human-readable business identifier for the escalation event, formatted as ESC-NNNNNNNN.

attributes.name: escalation_status
attributes.description: Current lifecycle status of the escalation event within the support workflow.

attributes.name: escalation_timestamp
attributes.description: The date and time when the escalation event occurred, formatted as yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.

attributes.name: escalation_type
attributes.description: Classification of the escalation mechanism: functional (to specialist team), hierarchical (to supervisor/manager), automatic (system-triggered), or manual (agent-initiated).

attributes.name: notes
attributes.description: Free-text notes or comments provided by the escalating agent explaining the context, urgency, or special handling requirements for the escalation.

attributes.name: nps_score
attributes.description: Net Promoter Score collected after escalation resolution, measuring customer loyalty on a scale of 0-10.

attributes.name: priority
attributes.description: The priority level assigned to the escalation, indicating urgency and required response time.

attributes.name: reason
attributes.description: The trigger or reason that caused the case to be escalated. Common reasons include SLA (Service Level Agreement) breach, customer demand, case complexity, VIP customer status, regulatory concern, or repeat issue.
attributes.references: ITIL (Information Technology Infrastructure Library) Service Management Framework

attributes.name: resolution_at_escalated_tier_flag
attributes.description: Boolean indicator of whether the case was successfully resolved at the escalated tier (True) or required further escalation (False).

attributes.name: resolution_code
attributes.description: Standardized code indicating how the escalated case was resolved (e.g., refund issued, replacement shipped, technical fix applied).

attributes.name: resolution_timestamp
attributes.description: The date and time when the escalated case was resolved, formatted as yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.

attributes.name: root_cause_category
attributes.description: The identified root cause category for the issue that led to escalation, used for continuous improvement and pattern analysis.

attributes.name: sla_breach_flag
attributes.description: Boolean indicator of whether the escalation was triggered by an SLA breach (True) or not (False).

attributes.name: sla_target_resolution_minutes
attributes.business_glossary_term: SLA (Service Level Agreement) Target Resolution Minutes
attributes.description: The target resolution time in minutes as defined by the applicable SLA for the escalated case.

attributes.name: source_tier
attributes.description: The support tier or level from which the case was escalated.

attributes.name: target_tier
attributes.description: The support tier or level to which the case was escalated.

attributes.name: time_to_resolution_minutes
attributes.description: The elapsed time in minutes from escalation timestamp to resolution timestamp, used for performance analysis and SLA compliance tracking.
attributes.references: Customer Service KPI (Key Performance Indicator) Framework

attributes.name: updated_timestamp
attributes.description: The date and time when the escalation record was last modified, formatted as yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.

products.name: feedback_response
products.description: Transactional record capturing all customer feedback survey responses tied to service interactions. Covers CSAT (1-5 satisfaction rating for specific interactions), NPS (0-10 loyalty score with promoter/passive/detractor classification), and CES (Customer Effort Score) surveys. Stores survey type, numeric score, classification category, verbatim feedback text, survey trigger event (post-case closure, post-delivery, periodic relationship survey), delivery channel (email, SMS, in-app), response timestamp, associated case and agent references, customer segment, and follow-up action flag. SSOT for all service-domain customer feedback and satisfaction measurement data.

attributes.name: feedback_response_id
attributes.description: Primary key for feedback_response

attributes.name: campaign_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to marketing.campaign. Business justification: Post-purchase NPS/CSAT surveys are frequently triggered by marketing campaigns in e-commerce. Linking feedback_response to the triggering campaign enables campaign-level satisfaction measurement, a cr

attributes.name: catalog_item_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to product.catalog_item. Business justification: Post-purchase NPS and CSAT surveys are triggered by specific product purchases. Linking feedback to catalog_item enables product quality scoring dashboards, PDP improvement decisions, and brand-level

attributes.name: channel_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to marketing.channel. Business justification: Post-purchase surveys are delivered via specific channels; channel-level survey response rate and NPS analysis are standard e-commerce CX metrics. `delivery_channel` plain string is a denormalization

attributes.name: customer_profile_id
attributes.description: Reference to the customer who provided the feedback response. Links to the unified customer profile in the Customer Data Platform.

attributes.name: fulfillment_shipment_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to fulfillment.fulfillment_shipment. Business justification: Post-delivery CSAT/NPS surveys are triggered by shipment delivery events. Linking feedback_response to fulfillment_shipment enables delivery experience analytics — correlating satisfaction scores with

attributes.name: header_id
attributes.description: Reference to the order associated with this feedback, if the survey was triggered by an order-related service interaction or post-delivery survey.

attributes.name: loyalty_enrollment_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to customer.loyalty_enrollment. Business justification: feedback_response.incentive_offered_flag indicates loyalty point incentives for survey completion. Direct FK to loyalty_enrollment enables automated point crediting for survey participation — a standa

attributes.name: marketplace_transaction_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to marketplace.marketplace_transaction. Business justification: Post-purchase feedback surveys are triggered by marketplace transactions. Seller performance dashboards, marketplace quality scoring, and FTC compliance reporting all require linking feedback response

attributes.name: primary_feedback_agent_id
attributes.description: Reference to the customer service agent who handled the case being evaluated. Used for agent performance tracking and coaching.

attributes.name: segment_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to customer.segment. Business justification: feedback_response.customer_segment is a denormalized text field. Proper FK to segment enables segment-level NPS/CSAT reporting, regulatory consent-aware survey targeting, and CX performance benchmarki

attributes.name: support_case_id
attributes.description: Reference to the service case or support ticket that triggered this feedback survey. Links the feedback to the specific service interaction being evaluated.

attributes.name: case_category
attributes.description: The category of the service case that triggered this feedback (e.g., order_inquiry, return_request, product_defect, delivery_issue, account_management, technical_support). Used to analyze satisfaction by case type and identify problem areas.

attributes.name: case_resolution_time_hours
attributes.description: The total time in hours from case creation to case closure for the service case being evaluated. Captured at the time of survey to enable correlation analysis between resolution speed and customer satisfaction.

attributes.name: channel_of_service
attributes.description: The channel through which the customer received service for the case being evaluated. Used to analyze satisfaction by service channel and optimize channel strategy. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: phone|email|chat|social_media|in-app|self-service|store — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

attributes.name: classification_category
attributes.description: The categorical classification derived from the numeric score. For NPS: promoter (9-10), passive (7-8), detractor (0-6). For CSAT: satisfied (4-5), neutral (3), dissatisfied (1-2). For CES: low_effort (6-7), high_effort (1-3). Used for segmentation and aggregate metrics calculation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: promoter|passive|detractor|satisfied|neutral|dissatisfied|low_effort|high_effort — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
attributes.references: Net Promoter Score (NPS) Methodology, Customer Satisfaction (CSAT) Standards

attributes.name: contact_reason
attributes.description: The specific reason the customer contacted support, as classified by the service team. More granular than case_category, capturing the detailed nature of the inquiry or issue (e.g., damaged_item, late_delivery, wrong_item_shipped, refund_status, promo_code_issue).

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: The date and time when this feedback response record was first created in the system. Used for data lineage, audit trails, and tracking data freshness.
attributes.references: ISO 8601 - Date and Time Format, Data Governance Standards

attributes.name: escalation_flag
attributes.description: Boolean flag indicating whether the service case was escalated to a supervisor or specialist team. Escalated cases typically have different satisfaction patterns and require special analysis.

attributes.name: external_survey_reference
attributes.description: The unique identifier for this survey response in the external survey platform or CRM system. Used for cross-system reconciliation and to link back to the source system for detailed survey metadata.

attributes.name: first_contact_resolution_flag
attributes.description: Boolean flag indicating whether the customer's issue was resolved on the first contact without requiring follow-up interactions. Key performance indicator (KPI) for service efficiency and a strong predictor of customer satisfaction.

attributes.name: follow_up_action_flag
attributes.description: Boolean flag indicating whether this feedback response requires follow-up action from the service team. Typically set to true for detractor responses, low CSAT scores, or when verbatim feedback indicates unresolved issues. Triggers service recovery workflows.

attributes.name: follow_up_completed_flag
attributes.description: Boolean flag indicating whether the required follow-up action has been completed. Used to track closure of service recovery loops and ensure no customer feedback is left unaddressed.

attributes.name: follow_up_completed_timestamp
attributes.description: The date and time when the follow-up action was completed. Used to measure time-to-resolution for service recovery and closed-loop feedback processes.

attributes.name: incentive_offered_flag
attributes.description: Boolean flag indicating whether an incentive (discount, reward points, gift card) was offered to encourage survey completion. Used to analyze response bias and response rate impact of incentives.

attributes.name: interaction_count
attributes.description: The total number of customer-agent interactions required to resolve the case being evaluated. Used to measure case complexity and correlate with effort scores and satisfaction ratings.

attributes.name: numeric_score
attributes.description: The numeric rating provided by the customer. For CSAT surveys this is typically 1-5 (1=very dissatisfied, 5=very satisfied). For NPS surveys this is 0-10 (0=not at all likely to recommend, 10=extremely likely). For CES surveys this is typically 1-7 (1=very difficult, 7=very easy).

attributes.name: primary_feedback_theme
attributes.description: The main topic or theme identified in the verbatim feedback through text analytics or manual coding. Examples include product_quality, delivery_speed, agent_professionalism, resolution_time, website_usability, pricing, return_process. Used for root cause analysis and improvement prioritization.

attributes.name: response_device_type
attributes.description: Type of device used by the customer to submit the feedback response. Used to analyze response patterns by device and optimize survey design for different form factors.

attributes.name: response_language
attributes.description: ISO 639-1 two-letter language code indicating the language in which the customer provided their feedback. Used for multilingual sentiment analysis and to route feedback to appropriate language-capable service teams.

attributes.name: response_status
attributes.description: Status of the survey response. Completed indicates all required questions were answered, partial indicates the survey was started but not finished, abandoned indicates the customer left mid-survey, expired indicates the survey link expired before response, bounced indicates the survey invitation was undeliverable.

attributes.name: response_timestamp
attributes.description: The date and time when the customer submitted their feedback response. Used to calculate response time from survey delivery and to track feedback recency.

attributes.name: sentiment_classification
attributes.description: Categorical sentiment classification derived from sentiment score and verbatim text analysis. Positive indicates favorable feedback, negative indicates unfavorable feedback, neutral indicates balanced or no strong sentiment, mixed indicates conflicting sentiments within the same response.
attributes.references: Text Analytics and Sentiment Analysis Standards

attributes.name: sentiment_score
attributes.description: Machine-generated sentiment score derived from verbatim feedback text analysis. Typically ranges from -1.0 (very negative) to +1.0 (very positive), with 0 being neutral. Used to augment numeric ratings with natural language understanding.

attributes.name: sla_met_flag
attributes.description: Boolean flag indicating whether the service case was resolved within the committed SLA timeframe. Used to correlate SLA compliance with customer satisfaction and identify the impact of SLA breaches on customer experience.

attributes.name: source_system
attributes.description: Identifier of the source system that captured this feedback response (e.g., Qualtrics, Medallia, SurveyMonkey, Salesforce Service Cloud, custom feedback platform). Used for data lineage and multi-system integration tracking.

attributes.name: survey_sent_timestamp
attributes.description: The date and time when the survey invitation was sent to the customer. Used to calculate response lag and survey completion rates.

attributes.name: survey_trigger_event
attributes.description: The specific business event that triggered the delivery of this feedback survey. Identifies the touchpoint in the customer journey being measured.

attributes.name: survey_type
attributes.description: Type of customer feedback survey administered. CSAT measures satisfaction with specific interactions (1-5 scale), NPS measures customer loyalty and likelihood to recommend (0-10 scale), CES measures ease of interaction (effort score). Post-Delivery surveys capture feedback after order fulfillment, Relationship surveys are periodic assessments, Post-Case surveys follow service case closure.

attributes.name: survey_version
attributes.description: Version identifier of the survey template used. Enables tracking of survey design changes over time and ensures consistent analysis when survey questions or scales are modified.
attributes.references: Survey Design and Version Control Standards

attributes.name: updated_timestamp
attributes.description: The date and time when this feedback response record was last modified. Tracks changes to follow-up status, theme classification, or other mutable fields.
attributes.references: ISO 8601 - Date and Time Format, Data Governance Standards

attributes.name: verbatim_feedback_text
attributes.description: Free-form text comments provided by the customer explaining their rating. This qualitative feedback provides context for the numeric score and is used for sentiment analysis, theme extraction, and identifying specific improvement opportunities. Classified as confidential due to potential inclusion of customer-specific details.
attributes.references: GDPR Article 4 - Personal Data, Text Analytics Standards

products.name: knowledge_article
products.description: Master record for knowledge base articles supporting agent-assisted resolution and customer self-service deflection. Captures article title, rich content body, article type (FAQ, troubleshooting guide, policy document, how-to, known issue), applicable product categories and issue taxonomy tags, authoring agent, review/approval workflow status, publish date, expiry/review-by date, version history, locale/language, view count, helpfulness rating (thumbs up/down), linked case types, and case deflection attribution metrics. Enables measurement of self-service effectiveness, agent knowledge utilization, and content gap identification.

attributes.name: knowledge_article_id
attributes.description: Unique identifier for the knowledge base article. Primary key.

attributes.name: bundle_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to product.bundle. Business justification: Bundle-specific knowledge articles (assembly guides, component compatibility, bundle return procedures) are a distinct content type in e-commerce support. Direct FK enables agents to retrieve bundle a

attributes.name: campaign_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to marketing.campaign. Business justification: Campaign-specific knowledge articles (promotional FAQs, offer terms, campaign issue resolutions) are standard in e-commerce support operations. Linking knowledge_article to campaign enables agents to

attributes.name: catalog_item_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to product.catalog_item. Business justification: Agents surface product-specific troubleshooting and setup articles when a case is opened for a catalog item. Direct FK enables article coverage gap analysis by product and powers automated article sug

attributes.name: category_id
attributes.description: Identifier of the primary product category this article applies to. Links article to relevant product catalog segments.

attributes.name: listing_category_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to marketplace.listing_category. Business justification: Knowledge articles are scoped to marketplace listing categories (e.g., hazmat return policies, age-restricted item handling, CPSC compliance guides). Support agents need category-specific articles sur

attributes.name: primary_knowledge_author_agent_id
attributes.description: Identifier of the customer service agent or knowledge manager who authored the article.

attributes.name: reviewer_agent_id
attributes.description: Identifier of the agent or manager who reviewed and approved the article for publication.

attributes.name: agent_usage_count
attributes.description: Number of times agents have referenced or attached this article during case resolution. Indicates agent knowledge utilization.

attributes.name: article_number
attributes.description: Externally-visible unique article number used for reference and citation in support interactions. Format: KA-XXXXXXXX.

attributes.name: article_type
attributes.description: Classification of the article content type. Determines presentation format and usage context.

attributes.name: attachment_urls
attributes.description: Comma-separated list of URLs pointing to supplementary attachments such as PDFs, videos, screenshots, or downloadable resources stored in Content Delivery Network (CDN).

attributes.name: case_deflection_count
attributes.description: Number of support cases attributed as deflected by this article through self-service resolution. Measured via customer journey tracking.

attributes.name: case_type_tags
attributes.description: Comma-separated list of case types this article is designed to resolve. Links article to specific support case categories.

attributes.name: content_body
attributes.description: Rich text content body of the article containing the full resolution steps, policy details, or instructional content. May include HTML formatting, images, and embedded media.

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when the article record was first created in the knowledge management system.

attributes.name: expiry_date
attributes.description: Date when the article is scheduled to expire and be automatically archived or removed from active knowledge base. Nullable for evergreen content.

attributes.name: helpful_votes
attributes.description: Count of positive helpfulness ratings (thumbs up) received from users who viewed the article.

attributes.name: helpfulness_score
attributes.description: Calculated helpfulness percentage based on ratio of helpful to total votes. Range 0.00 to 100.00. Used to identify high-quality and low-quality content.

attributes.name: issue_taxonomy_tags
attributes.description: Comma-separated list of issue taxonomy tags for categorization and search optimization. Examples: shipping_delay, payment_failure, account_access, product_defect.

attributes.name: knowledge_article_status
attributes.description: Current lifecycle status of the article in the approval and publication workflow.

attributes.name: last_modified_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when the article record was last modified, including content updates, metadata changes, or status transitions.

attributes.name: last_updated_date
attributes.description: Date when the article content was last modified and republished.

attributes.name: locale
attributes.description: Language and region locale code for the article content. Format: ISO 639-1 language code underscore ISO 3166-1 country code (e.g., en_US, es_MX, fr_FR).

attributes.name: not_helpful_votes
attributes.description: Count of negative helpfulness ratings (thumbs down) received from users who viewed the article.

attributes.name: priority_level
attributes.description: Business priority level of the article content. Critical articles address high-impact issues and are promoted in search results.

attributes.name: publish_date
attributes.description: Date when the article was first published and made available to agents or customers.

attributes.name: review_by_date
attributes.description: Target date by which the article should be reviewed for accuracy and relevance. Used to trigger periodic content audits.

attributes.name: search_keywords
attributes.description: Comma-separated list of keywords and phrases optimized for internal search engine indexing and article discovery.

attributes.name: title
attributes.description: Human-readable title of the knowledge article displayed in search results and article headers.

attributes.name: version_number
attributes.description: Sequential version number of the article. Incremented with each published revision to track content evolution.

attributes.name: view_count
attributes.description: Total number of times the article has been viewed by agents or customers. Used to measure article popularity and relevance.

attributes.name: visibility_scope
attributes.description: Defines the audience scope for article visibility. Determines which user groups can access the article.

domains.name: marketplace
